review_text: Ms Jae is a truly wonderful childcare teacher. She is caring, gentle, and deeply dedicated to her work. My child has been at the centre since 18 months old and is now 6 years old. Ms Jae previously left childcare and later rejoined, and we are extremely grateful to have her back. I am writing this review to express my heartfelt appreciation for the outstanding work being done at Touch ChildCare - Clementi. As a parent, it brings me immense peace of mind to know that my child is in such a caring, supportive, and enriching environment every day.

I would like to especially acknowledge the dedication and leadership - Ms Adeline Yip- Principal, consistently demonstrate. Her commitment to maintaining high standards of care and education is clearly reflected in the positive atmosphere of the center and in the joyful, engaged faces of the children.

Additionally, I would like to extend a sincere thank you to Ms Jenny and for Ye 老师 for their remarkable efforts in the classroom. Their patience, creativity, and genuine affection for my girl do not go unnoticed. It is evident that they take great pride in their role, and it shows in the way the children learn, grow, and feel safe and loved under their guidance.

Would like to express my gratitude to them for creating a nurturing space where children are not only looked after but are also encouraged to flourish. The impact of their hard work is deeply appreciated and truly valued.

We are very happy to enrol our firstborn at TOUCH Clementi. Everyone in the centre is so friendly and welcoming to all children, making it a loving and conducive environment for the kids to learn and grow.

My child has learnt so much since starting school; we were constantly surprised by his learning development! We are very thankful to the staff and teachers and would definitely recommend this centre to others.

Always ensure safe and clean environment at the centre is I admired the most when I am quite particular with certain level of cleanliness and I admired the most is they only on air con when necessary as my kids have sinusitis condition; Very caring, patient, friendly, responsible and experience teachers and principle! Both my daughter and son like to go childcare everyday. There is no issue with me during the transition period from nanny to childcare at their 2 years old. My husband and I have a peace of mind when we knowing that our kids are in good hands when we are at work! The centre has engaged fun and exciting curriculum to cater child’s development and practice their motor skills which I signed up for all the courses for my kids. They love it very much! Grateful that we found touch childcare, the best choice and most suitable childcare for both of my kids in many ways! Good job to all teaching and non teaching staff! Highly recommended!

**What we like about TCC (Clementi):
- Environment: very good number of teacher-students ratio. They have aircons but not for turning on the whole time. We really don't like places turn on aircon all the time, my kids seldom catch a cold if compare when they in previous childcare. Even without aircons, the design and fan system make the place really cool & airy.

- Food: At first when my kids join TCC, food is catering. Now in childcare, there are 2 aunties cooking & preparing daily meals for kids.

- Teachers (including principal): friendly, supportive & easy to communicate. They all received feedbacks and take it as the points to consider for the general better.

- Curriculum design: A lot in the characters & values-which we believed that most important to kids. A lot of hands on activities
and outing is design to meet the teaching focus. I love most is every morning, my kids have chance to go out for 1 hour walk which expose them to knows our neighbourhood and expose to sunlight, which very hard for us who are working parents to bring them out in everyday morning.

- Communication: There is an app for parents-teachers to communicate with. Teachers also updates on children progress bi-weekly.

- Most of all: TCC takes in special needs kids with mild symtoms, which creates a wonderful environment for kids to learn how to love & share.

Again, we very appreciate your hard work of all staffs at TCC Clementi. I can see my kids grow in TCC. Thank you 💕

Our family has been part of the Clementi Woods Kindergarten (CWK) community for several years, and we couldn’t be more grateful for the experience.

From day one, CWK has felt like a second home — a place where our child has been cared for and encouraged.  Every child is welcomed by name, from the morning greetings at the gate to the cheerful assembly and into the classroom. It’s something that truly touched us — knowing that not just our child, but every student is known personally by the staff.

The teachers, many of whom have been with the school for over a decade, bring not only experience but a deep love for what they do. They take time to understand each child, helping them grow in confidence, independence, and character.

What sets CWK apart is its strong foundation in faith and values. The principles of love, care, sharing, giving, and growing aren’t just taught — they’re lived out daily in the classroom and playground. The admin team, led by the principal, is warm, responsive, and always open to feedback. We’ve felt heard and supported every step of the way.

One of our child’s favourite parts of the day has been the morning assembly — filled with praise and worship songs, laughter, and energy. It’s a joyful start that sets the tone for learning and connection.

CWK may not have all the bells and whistles of a commercial preschool, but it offers something far more valuable: a safe, consistent, and faith grounded environment where children thrive.

We’re so thankful for the strong foundation it’s given our child — and for the community we’ve found here.

A fairly good kindergarten with kind and helpful staff. The admin team is really good and accommodating.

However, the start of each day at CWK is overstimulating for a preschooler as loud music will be blasted at assembly - I supposed the school wants to hype up the entire atmosphere for kids but a more calming start might be an easier transition for kids.

Some teaching staff have been around for more than 15 years so some of their methods, though well-intentioned, might be less attuned to the needs of current generation of kids and borders on being disempowering for children who need a little more support. There is a lot of focus on traditional teaching methods like worksheets and less hands-on activities that promotes experiential learning and creative thinking.

School bus driver is a little eccentric and would pick a bone with parents who enter the school compound earlier (understandably, cars can only enter at 2.20pm due to limited space available) - even if permission has been sought to enter the school (happened to me twice, and I’ve witnessed this happen to other parents several times too).

I will recommend this school for a faith-based environment, super nice/easy to work with admin team (principal, admin, etc). However, if you’re looking for teachers who are nurturing, patient, supportive, and updated in their teaching methods, it might be less than desirable. The school is big on conformity and very unsupportive of independent thinking.

We have switched our child from this school to another school and found that we really prefer a quieter/ calming start to the day and we find it more empowering for our preschooler to be in the company of teachers who understand the need for hands-on learning, are patient and more nurturing. We find our child now more confident and less scared to express her views in class. It’s regretful that our child did not have a positive experience with her teachers at CWK as she often talks about her fear of being scolded. This has a lasting impact on her that we are still trying to work on how. It’s very regretful and one of the poorest choices I have made.

This is a personal and honest view from my end, with due respect given to the school and their staff. Written with the sole intent for other parents to understand the environment before they dive in as quite a lot of energy, time and money will be involved if you choose to enrol in any school of your choice.

This is the best kindergarten you can send your child to. All my four children attend this kindergarten.

It is not expensive. They have a holistic program that expose your child to various subjects.

They teach your children life skills , math and also Chinese and handicraft.

The Chinese teacher speaks to your child only in mandarin, which helps stem learn the language.

The children love going to school and it gives them such a good impression of school, starting them on the right track to  learning.

If you are interested in sending your child here, you need to queue up early to register because it is extremely popular.

Furthermore, the uniform looks great  😃

I am so grateful for what CWK has done for my kids. They acquired discipline, maintained healthy habits and happiness, while excelled academically. The teachers are loving and supportive. I can't ask for more.

The pastoral aspect is very good. During HBL, we appreciated the effort to create engaging in-house videos and worksheets/ workbooks.

Edited:
As many private kindergartens no longer operate because of the changing landscape and costing, I hope to express our family's gratitude. CWK has taught my child self-discipline, a desire to do one's best and to behave with integrity. Not that it really matters, but of the 6 children who went into her primary school, 4 are in the stretch classes.

For us, it was not so much the quantity of content but the nurturing of the character that made the difference. Reflective learning , being metacognitive, has made her purposeful.

My child is proud to say she was a CWK kid and 5 years after K2, she tells me she wishes she could re-live her Kindy years.

A bustling neighborhood mall! This mall is very convenient since it's directly linked to the Clementi MRT and bus interchange. It’s got everything you need across several floors, from a variety of shops and banks to a supermarket, food court, and even a library. It’s the perfect all-in-one spot for locals to grab a meal or run errands.

The Clementi Mall is a very convenient small-to-medium sized heartland mall, especially because it is directly connected to Clementi MRT station and the bus interchange. This makes it one of the easiest malls to access in the west, and it naturally gets very crowded on weekends and during peak hours

The tenant mix is practical and useful, with food options, shops, services, a supermarket, pharmacies, and casual dining places. It is not a huge destination mall, but it works very well for daily needs, quick meals, errands, and meeting up with family or friends

The lower floors can feel quite packed, especially near the MRT entrance and popular food areas. The upper floors are noticeably less crowded, so it is more comfortable if you want to walk around or browse at a slower pace

The Clementi Mall: A very convenient and modern shopping hub.

I visited around 7 PM on a weekday. The mall is directly connected to the MRT station, making it extremely convenient for a quick stop or a dinner visit.

The Shops & Atmosphere:
There is a great variety of shops and restaurants, including a section with many food stalls that feels like a mini-market. I was very surprised to find a massive library right inside the mall! It’s a great place to browse and borrow books in a modern setting.

The Food & Culture:
One "minus point" for me is that the mall mostly contains international brands and chain stores. I didn't find many small, local shops, so it might not be the best place if you are looking to explore traditional Singaporean culture.

The Verdict:
It is a perfect, air-conditioned spot for practical shopping and dining, but it feels more like a standard modern mall than a cultural destination.

This mall is small but very high-traffic because of the students and the MRT connection. It’s functional and has all the basics like a big library and plenty of quick-service food. If you want a more authentic experience, the Clementi 448 Market nearby is where the "real" West side food is, though it’s always hot and noisy.

​Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) offers a highly practical and industry-focused education. The curriculum is regularly updated with input from industry partners, and lecturers, often with professional experience, provide a realistic and engaging learning environment. The focus on project-based learning encourages critical thinking and problem-solving, which I found to be a more effective method of learning than traditional memorization.

​While the polytechnic's modular system can be demanding, with a constant cycle of assignments and projects, it effectively prepares students for the pressures of both higher education and the workplace. The campus life is equally vibrant, with a wide array of CCAs, excellent facilities, and diverse food options.

​Overall, NP provides a well-rounded experience, blending academic rigor with a rich student life. It's a great choice for students who thrive in a hands-on environment and are seeking to build a strong foundation for their future careers.

It sounds like you're planning a trip to Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) Block 23! Here's a quick rundown to help you get there:

1. Take the MRT to Clementi Station.
2. At Clementi Station, exit towards Bus Interchange.
3. Board Bus 154 from Clementi Bus Interchange.
4. Get off at Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) stop.

You can use Google Maps or a transit app like Grab or Moovit for the most up-to-date bus schedules and routes. Have a smooth ride to your company event! 🚍🎉..

Ngee Ann Polytechnic has this warm, almost “campus village” vibe. The greenery is everywhere — huge trees, long slopes, and shaded areas that make the place feel peaceful even when there are a lot of students around. NP is known for being very student-focused, and you can actually feel that when walking through the school. There are tons of small places to sit down and study, from open-air benches to indoor lounges.

The campus spirit is strong. A lot of students seem genuinely happy to be there — you’ll see groups practising dance, people playing guitar, students sitting around chatting after lessons, and clubs actively doing their thing. The Co-Curricular culture is particularly strong; NP is one of the polys where the arts and student life are very vibrant.

What I liked:

Extremely beautiful surroundings; feels like studying in a nature park sometimes.

Many programmes are well-known and respected, especially mass comm and business.

Good social vibes — friendly atmosphere and active clubs everywhere.

Lots of cozy corners to study, hang out, or relax between classes.

What could be better:

The slopes are legendary — going up and down the hills every day becomes a workout.

Classrooms and older blocks are slightly dated in some areas.

Some canteens fill up very quickly, especially during lunch hours.

Overall:
NP feels like a warm, welcoming campus with plenty of life. If you like greenery, community feeling, and a comfortable environment, this is a great place.

SUSS has been a meaningful part of my academic journey, especially since it was the only institution that accepted my application. While the curriculum is undeniably rigorous, it has also pushed me to grow intellectually and adapt to increasingly complex expectations, particularly as I approach Year 3. Some assignments are quite challenging and require a high level of critical thinking, which can be demanding but ultimately rewarding.

One concern, however, is the school’s efforts to combat the use of AI. While the intention is understandable, the system has flagged a significant number of false positives, creating unnecessary stress and anxiety among students. This has, at times, affected the overall learning experience and assignments submission. I hope that the institution can fine tune and resolve this issue soon.

Additionally, certain components such as the e-portfolio—especially in the last semester—feel like an added chores for students. Instead of adding meaningful value to learning, it can come across as repetitive and disconnected from more practical or relevant academic outcomes. Suss might want to review if Eportfolio is truly necessary.

Despite these challenges, SUSS remains a good institution that offers a strong platform for preparing students for the realities of the working world. Its emphasis on applied learning and resilience equips students with practical skills that extend beyond the classroom.

SUSS, some jokingly called it Singapore University of Self-Study due to its nature of promoting adult learners who are capable of doing their own independent learning to improve classroom discussions, are also known as the Singapore University of Stairs and Slopes now due to their recent upgrades by me. I used to not find the trail to classroom not so physically challenging until recently, maybe it’s just me :)

The Singapore University of Social Sciences (SUSS), vibrantly anchored at 463 Clementi Road, is a truly magnificent phenomenon in the educational landscape that I find deeply inspiring for its unwavering commitment to "Head, Heart, and Habit." Having engaged in numerous enlightening dialogues with passionate social workers and empathetic human resource leaders in my network, I am genuinely fascinated by how SUSS operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where academic rigor is inextricably linked to community impact. My acquaintances often emphasize that the pedagogical energy within its Clementi campus is exceptionally inclusive, offering a rare environment where lifelong learners and young undergraduates alike are empowered to master complex counseling or supply chain systems under the guidance of socially-conscious mentors. Public sentiment remains remarkably high, viewing the university not merely as an adult learning center, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in the 2026 silver economy and non-profit sectors. Reflecting on these recurring, authentic testimonials from peers who have transformed their local neighborhoods, I perceive this institution as a distinguished powerhouse of human progress that executes its mission with a level of humanitarian audacity and Singaporean pragmatism that is truly refreshing. This academic landmark is consistently described by those "in the know" as a place where the theoretical sparks of social theory are transformed into the tangible steel of community resilience. Furthermore, the university's pioneering focus on applied learning ensures that graduates enter the labor market as highly specialized, compassionate assets with a distinct ethical edge. I personally admire the way SUSS fosters a culture of mutual respect and social responsibility, treating every student as a future steward of a more equitable society. The campus itself is often praised for its accessible, collaborative atmosphere, providing a professional backdrop that mirrors the inclusive environments our future social pioneers are destined to lead. It is heartening to hear from my network that the university’s flexibility is not just a logistical feature, but a core philosophy that respects the diverse life paths of its students. Many of my colleagues point out that SUSS’s industry-integrated projects provide an invaluable bridge to the real world, ensuring that theoretical knowledge never stays trapped in a book. I find it remarkable how the institution maintains its status as a premier public university while remaining so grounded in the everyday needs of the people. Every conversation I engage in regarding this university reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and lifelong empowerment. The school’s dedication to the "Humanities for the 21st Century" is, in my view, the definitive blueprint for an education that values people over mere profit. Ultimately, aligning one’s academic trajectory with the Singapore University of Social Sciences is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to navigate the complexities of the modern world while remaining part of a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. This institution stands as a brilliant testament to the power of education when it is fueled by a genuine desire to serve the greater good.

I live in Hillview and this is my go-to MRT Station to get to Jurong East that is only one MRT stop away. And it is very convenient for me to get to this MRT Station as Bus number 184 from Hillview MRT Station bus stop to the bus stop at Clementi MRT Station is very frequent. I also like this MRT Station as it is connected to the very popular shopping mall in Clementi - The Clementi Mall.
Many good affordable restaurants nearby. There's a hawker center just below the MRT if you want to quickly grab some loca and Korean cuisines.

It's a clean MRT station. It's also extremely well connected to all the neighboring malls, sheltered, easy access, etc. Lastly, there's also the bus interchange that will bring you further to other parts of town.

That said, it's unfortunately extremely crowded, all times of the day. Especially during peak hours, you'll want to either avoid, or factor in extra time.

This MRT station is an important transport hub for residents in the Clementi area. However, as it has been in operation for quite some time, the current layout can feel congested, especially during peak hours. It may be worth considering renovations or upgrades to better accommodate the increasing passenger traffic and ease the morning rush.

I’d like to commend Elaine Oh for her excellent customer service. She patiently assisted me with refunding my expired EZ-Link card and helped deactivate the auto top-up for my soon-to-expire card. Elaine took the time to clearly explain each step of the process, making everything smooth and easy to understand. Her patience, professionalism, and helpful attitude made the whole experience very pleasant. Thank you, Elaine, for your outstanding support! 😊👏
Worst interchange ever, bus took **15 minutes! , will not recommend and staff was rude, pls make bus faster or else there will be more complaints 😡🥺

A nice bus interchanged.  Very conveniently connected to the Clementi Mall, air conditioning, good displays of upcoming bus schedules, and queueing lines for the different buses.  It's great to catch the usual bus here out of rain when it's raining outside.  Also has exit directly to the Clementi Town Centre without going through the Clementi Mall.
Easily accessible from Clementi MRT. The interchange have a wide range of buses available.

Nice locations and crowded. Nearby there are 2 shopping malls with one big market food court and few coffee shops. Foods are good too! Very accessible too as the bus interchange and clementi mrt are also nearby!

21/2 7.16pm Was walking toward to take 99.Saw the bus driver waiting to take off , i ran towards his door but he pretended not to see me n drove off.I had to go back to q again.Other den dat lots of food to eat at this area.

I always take bus 7.  Today, 18 Sep, took the bus 7. The bus supposed to arrive at 6.33pm but it came at 6.38pm. The bus was going very slow from the interchange all the way to Orchard (where my destination). Sometimes,  the bus would stop or almost stop at traffic light even though it was green. The indian bus driver was eating something.

One of the male cleaner I encounter at clementi bus interchange Female toilet of eclat services which the bus interchange engage their services is terrible. Always put a sign they are cleaning. So if cleaning can't use the toilet even urgent why can't you clean other side? Most of the time commit rude acts like purposely bump into you, splash water at you and do lots of moronic unkind acts, The worst is the cleaner use water hose to assault you. Best lock the toilet so nobody can go in will be forever clean, nonsensical and pathetic.

Ceiling goes over buses so commuters don't get wet in event of rain. Big interchange with a buzz shop selling snacks and a table selling newspaper. A lot of space for buses to come in but sometimes inconsiderate cars wait for people at the bus stop and hold everyone up

Very bad bus no 173 Many times not following the timing...
Today 6.13am until 6.50am I'm waiting for bus...but timing show definitely 6.15 one bus will come but not coming until.6.50
Get a bus number which can go to Sungei Kadut, because bus 925 doesn’t operate on Sunday and Public holiday so please get LTA to get a bus number which can go to Sungei Kadut or Eng Neo Avenue.

This has to be one of the worst bus interchanges I have experienced.

Bus timings are consistently slow, especially for service 285. The intervals are too long, and queues often stretch all the way to the automatic entrance doors. It is not uncommon to see large crowds waiting.

When the bus finally comes, it is usually already packed. There have been occasions where it skips the stop entirely because no more passengers can board. This makes the commute unpredictable and unnecessarily stressful.

Because of these delays and poor intervals, I have been late on multiple occasions. Something needs to be improved, whether it is frequency or crowd management.

Bus 232 to Clementi West is at least 13 minutes per bus. So low frequency and waiting time is long! Although they have multiple buses, but the interval time for other bus even higher (201@14min; and 175@18min). When 232 come, 201 and 175 also coming about same time. Meaning if you miss certain timepoint, you have to wait for additional 10plus minutes for the next bus to come, even through they have 3 buses. You don't feel the convenient of having more option. 😩😩😩

I am honouring Clementi Bus Interchange for being home to Singapore's shortest bus route, Bus 284, which plies three bus stops along Clementi Avenue 4: Bus Stop no. 17211 (between Blocks 315/318), Bus Stop no. 17201 (Block 308) and Bus Stop no. 17219 (Block 376). The total distance covered is 1.8 kilometres long in one loop service. Unlike Woodlands' Bus 902 which ends at Republic Polytechnic, it is a unidirectional or one-way service with one single stop before terminating.

Clementi Bus Interchange is very conducive because of its medium-sized air-conditioned floor space. It is quite accessible and it does have plenty of entry and exit points to Clementi Mall. The arriving berths and departing berths are well-spaced to allow bus drivers ample driving and parking spaces at any time of the operational day.
The timing there is extremely inconsistent. Of all the places I’ve been to, the most disorganised, least punctual, and longest waiting times I’ve experienced were at Clementi Bus Interchange.
The place is not very big but really very convenience to let people go through to all directions.

First time visiting 448 Market & Food Centre in the bustling heart of Clementi.
So many hawker stalls with tempting and delicious-looking food.

Settled on the famous wanton noodles — the owner was friendly, the $4 portion was generous, and the char siu had a nice smoky flavour.

Unfortunately, the char kway teow was a letdown, and it was a pity that the well-known fried carrot cake stall was off for the day.
Overall, this hawker centre is still worth a visit to explore local hawker favourites.

Great Food Hub: A Gastronomical Gem Worth the Hype (and the Queues)

This hawker centre is a true food lover’s paradise, packed with wonderful stalls that serve up incredible eats. There are several iconic spots with notoriously long queues – plus even a Michelin-rated stall in the mix!

That’s why I’ve learned not to jump into any line right away. Instead, I take a few minutes to survey all the options and decide exactly what I’m craving.

It’s genuinely a one-stop destination for great food, though it comes with a small caveat: most stalls are so busy that service is minimal, and a couple of the more famous ones have stallholders who can come across as rude or unfriendly. But if you can look past the no-frills (and sometimes gruff) service and focus purely on the food, this place delivers serious gastronomical pleasure – and it’s absolutely worth a visit.

The food that I loved: Char Kway Teow (Extra eggs and Cockles), Bedok Chwee Kuey, Satay Bee Hoon, Laksa Chicken, Chinese Pork Satay, Wonton Noodles (Average), Handmade Dim Sum and fried Carrot Cake (I prefer the white ones)

The western food stall that's inside, quite solid. The chicken chop portion is generous and sauce is on point. For $6, this is damn worth it. Can use CDC also.

This food center is usually always crowded. A bit hard to find seats so I usually just takeaway.

Leaving my review here of the western stall 448 Western Food/ O Meal(?). I have included a picture of their storefront for reference. Really good chicken chop and chicken cutlet with thick slices of meat! Sauces were on point and the coleslaw is such a treat! The chicken were prepared fresh upon order and you can see the wall of freshly fried chicken!

Worth the buy and best eaten while hot! Accepts CDC voucher as well!

Duck rice is good and price is reasonable. Meat is tender and rice is fragrance. The soup have slight herbal light taste. Might need to queue a while for this during lunch hour.
Curry chicken at this stall is great with flavourful curry and not too spicy. 2 pairs of Chicken wing and small drumsticks with potatoes. Pair with the rice is a great combo.

353 Clementi Avenue 2 Food Centre - this eighteen-stall hawker centre located in a "sleepy" neighbourhood of Clementi may be smaller than the other hawker centres in Singapore, but it has enough good food choices thereat. And I even bother to take a bus here from a bit far away, from the Dairy Farm area, where I live, because of the abovementioned good food choices and its "quieter" than other hawker centre's dining ambience. By the way, my favourite hawker stall at this Food Centre is Jalilah's Corner that serves excellent Malay food staples like nasi lemak, mee rebus and gado gado.

Came by this area arnd 8am after sending son to school. Surprisingly found a malay stall (maybe chinese muslim) Aisha Lee Muslim Food, open on a Monday. Ordered mee rebus and some kuih2. The mee rebus was not bad, rich in flavour. They hv epok2 sardine, potato and yu tiao (not sure if its fresh fried). Theres also a mamak stall just behind ALMF and of course we ordered teh tarik to go with our breakfast.

Foods are decent, the taste is okay.
The price is affordable depending on the side dishes you choose.
I tried nasi lemak, mie soto, soto ayam (consist only sprouts, shredded chicken, and lontong, quite different from soto ayam in Indonesia).
Cosy sized food centre that has been here as long as the HDBs. Good affordably priced local food. Enjoy my dining experience every time I go there. 😃👍✨

Sept 24, we have Braised Duck set and Braises duck Kway Teow. Excellent with lot of duck meat which is tender and nice. Generous with the portion. Unit 01-73 open from Breakfast to lunch .
Mar 25, unit 01-73 one of the best - try again today.

If u are in Clementi Mall, and would like a bowl of good fishballs noodles plus in the comfort of aircon /sheltered fm rain. This is the place at the basement of mall.

This is a chain brand, and l loved their fish balls… authentic, has the same home made taste/ texture.   Just the way grandma used to make decades ago.
The service is fast.. and they have a decent private large seating area.  So don’t have to take your bowl and walk around in food courts/ hawker centres.

Read about the negative reviews after finishing my noodle.

Very true, If they want customers to return, gotto up their game. If you have a bad day sort it out before coming to work.

The front counter lady (tallest abt 1.7m) seriously has a stuck up attitude. Saw her raising voice also to other customers.

I ordered laksa noodle takeaway. The noodles portion is pathetic. Laksa soup is almost room temperature, doesn’t feel hot, and honestly quite bland. It doesn’t even fill 1/2 of the takeaway container. I am a small eater, but even this kind of portion is insufficient for me to feel full.

This Eat stall is located at Clementi Mall basement. A crowd favorite. During peak hours, might need to queue. The Bak Chor Mee is one of the recommended dish. Savory & chewy noodle, top up with minced meat & fishball 😋. Laksa & fishball noodles are also their signature.
Ordered the handmade shrimp dumpling soup and found out that there’s a shell in the prawn which is wrapped inside the dumpling.

Newly opened Grounds on a Hill at Sunset Way is a clean, minimal, coffee-first space. Bright and airy inside, with the smell of fresh coffee carrying through the room. Outdoor seating is breezy and comfortable, anchored by solid wood block benches. Menu is simple with items like sourdough bread, which I didn't sample this visit.

Both white and black coffees were excellent. The house blend leans fruity with gentle berry notes, well-balanced and not overly acidic.

this was my second time at grounds, I had a lovely chat over carefully made coffee, with smiles and a warm welcome by the team. I learnt that this was a family run business, with beans roasted fresh themselves.

this abode in sunset way provides that human touch we need, and a place to really rest and escape for a little!

Very chill cafe that is located further inside from the main road (though the area is relatively small anyways) - but very well worth the walk there as they do serve up great coffee in a cool minimalistic environment!

☕ another coffee that hits the benchmark, the plum flavour really did follow through in my iced white, and similarly for the hot drink as well - not the best of the best but that’s my benchmark for a 5 stars coffee, being able to taste the aroma from my drink

🍞 the sourdough was alright, pretty pricey for the portion though it was a decent snack to be had

Might return to try their tarts in future, and also their other beans too!

Excellent coffee in a very well-designed coffee space, that is dog friendly. It’s one of my favourite places to bring people to. Thrilled to see adventurous coffee businesses bringing specialty coffee to the heartlands and neighbourhoods. Great enterprise by Rio and his daughters Paris and Sydney.

So pleased to have found this coffee shop, a real treasure in the most unassuming of locations.

The philosophy is simple; fantastic coffee at affordable prices. The family that own and run this place have an endearing love of the coffee they make, and they keep it simple - two varieties of filter coffee (the Peruvian Geisha is phenomenal!) and two varieties of espresso, all roasted to suit. You can see the passion that goes into each cup that is carefully & beautifully crafted. A simple but quality food menu as well and that’s it!

There is relaxed indoor and outdoor seating and I all-too-easily managed to pass a couple of hours there with a good book. If you’re in Singapore and you love speciality coffee, don’t miss it!

Heavily recommended coffee destination in the West. OGs in the SG coffee/ barrista scene.

Thought our coffee would be made by the award winning barista, but didn't seem so. Not sure what a matcha mont blanc is, but it appeared to be a regular matcha, topped with vanilla cream. Drinks were good but not distinctive. Am interested in trying more beans from nylon however.

Food took quite awhile to arrive (more than half hour), seems to have been some mix-up in the kitchen. Almost cancelled cuz we had an appointment to keep. Food wasn't exceptional, but was well presented. Looked good. A lot of regulars as clientele.

The lady boss appeared to be in tight crisis mode with how much she zipped around. Commend her composure however.

Decor was tasteful and mellow, enjoyed the look and feel of the area. Seems like many people agree, judging from the eclectic expat clientele. Feels vaguely Christian despite the lack of religious iconography.

Will say that prices are extremely high for the HDB location, and risen significantly in these last two to three years.

Stopped at this coffee shop for a morning coffee. The staff was really friendly, giving off a welcoming vibe. Atmosphere was also very relaxed, with warm, yellow lights and comfortable seats.

As I was the only one ordering, the coffee came really fast. I ordered a Magic Coffee (White Coffee), where the coffee aroma is more potent. The coffee is nothing short of spectacular, made with real effort (as can be seen from the artwork made in the coffee) and aromatic coffee beans. The taste is slightly bitter, which is expected because of the potency.

A really satisfying coffee to awaken your senses and satiate your body with this. There's more items on the menu too. Overall, a good stop for you and your friends to enjoy delectable coffee and snacks!

This is a great cafe located in a quiet neighbourhood. Both the food and coffee are of high quality, and the staff seem to be coffee connoisseurs with extensive knowledge. They are attentive and friendly, which made the visit especially enjoyable.

I would highly recommend their specialty coffee. Although I only tried the Mont Blanc, I’m sure the rest would be just as good.

Wow!!
They only lost 1 star in atmosphere from me instead of full 5 because they just expand. Imagine to come here for great coffee and find no seats.

Came on a Thursday morning around 11.
Absolutely stunning coffee. Brilliant barista skills.
The breeze made me forget I was in Clementi for a moment.
Been coming here regularly for a while and while I understand the gripes about slow service etc, this place is best enjoyed in the off peak periods.
It is a transformative and could I add romantic experience.

place_name: Sichuan Tianfu Restaurant (Clementi) 天府川菜 (金文泰)
review_text: The person who left a one star was right lol, the portion does not tele with the cost of the dish. Dont get fooled by the small medium large cos the actual portion differential is hard to measure but the upgrade cost is very apparent. I ordered medium large Zhi 鸡, for 24/27 dollars? The portion feels like it’s small! I wonder how small looks like. Good atmosphere to dine, but not so good service. The top up of side dish feels more “on demand” than auto. The bowl of fish in sizzling chilli feels overrated, 28 dollars for a few slices of fish. You go outside order fish soup can get even more fish than what they gave you. Fair point they give u a ton of ingredients which adds to visuals but obviously you can’t eat the chilli they give you. Beef salad was surprisingly more worth for money. Tofu I guess ok, good texture not so broken up. Nice cubes of tofu

place_name: Sichuan Tianfu Restaurant (Clementi) 天府川菜 (金文泰)
review_text: If you think it's worth it, you're mistaken. The portion is both small and costly. I am uncertain about the reason behind their outstanding reviews.  Avoid wasting your time by going to the Sichuan Tianfu restaurant. It's my belief that Oriental Food 东方美食 is still superior and they've just opened in Westgate. The price of Oriental food 东方美食 is lower and it has a larger portion.

place_name: Sichuan Tianfu Restaurant (Clementi) 天府川菜 (金文泰)
review_text: This place is tucked away in a mall next to Clementi Mall . Food is surprisingly decent . The food we ordered were all delicious cumin beef, cauliflower were cooked with lard which tasted good. Mappo tofu looks spicy hot but it was bearable . Fish with pickled vege was also nice. We also had the cucumber salad taste was good but wished the seeds were not added to the dish .

place_name: Sichuan Tianfu Restaurant (Clementi) 天府川菜 (金文泰)
review_text: Very good. I like szechuan Chinese foods, they’re so yummy. I will come back again with my friends.

place_name: Sichuan Tianfu Restaurant (Clementi) 天府川菜 (金文泰)
review_text:
Our reservation timing (7pm) was mixed up and we almost did not get our tables readied for us in time, (we arrived at 6.45pm) thankfully this was sorted out after a while and we got seated around 7.05pm. However, this was when the true wait commenced. We asked for the restaurant staff at least four times to take our orders. Each time, they said they will come over shortly but none of that materialised until we had to insist firmly that our orders be taken immediately. At this point, it was already a 20 mins wait. Our orders were finally taken at around 7.25pm.

The waiting game continued. We decided it was getting too absurd and went to the restaurant’s counter to enquire about our order at 8.05pm. The lady boss was apologetic and promised to check it out at that instance. 10 mins later, she returned to our table to inform that there was a mistake by the staff member who took our orders. “He did not key in the order and hence our orders were actually not registered in the system”

As speechless as we were, we decided to just repeat our orders and emphasised for them to be expedited. Finally, our first dish arrived at 8.17pm, followed by the 2nd one at 8.19pm, and 3rd one at 8.24pm. Thereafter, it was another long wait for our last two dishes which came only at 8.51pm and 9.02pm.

To give credits to the restaurant, the food was awesome. But a lot needs to be done to improve the restaurant’s management of peak hour workload.

While we were disappointed with the horrendous waiting time, the lady boss’ service recovery and attitude were sincere throughout.

We will probably come back again (maybe the other outlet) for a second try because of the food quality.

I visited this yakiniku house for lunch on a weekday. The overall experience was functional, though the cost-performance ratio was average. Regarding the food, the quality of the meat is consistent with the price point; it is advisable not to have high expectations, but the portions are sufficient to ensure you leave feeling full. This establishment seems particularly well-suited for hungry students looking for a substantial, filling meal. One point to note is the service charge, which felt somewhat high relative to the level of service provided. Aside from the basic delivery of dishes to the table, there were no notable service interactions or extra hospitality during the visit. In summary, it is a practical choice for those prioritizing quantity over premium quality in a casual environment. If you are looking to satisfy a large appetite on a budget, this location fits that need, provided you account for the additional fees.

first visit, nice experience.
went early before 6pm for dinner, not much queue.
crowd came in after dinner.

ingriedent fresh, portion average.
ownself cook using condiments by the side, important point everything works.
furnishing is simple, useable for its purpose.

staff overwhelmed but still friendly and helpful.
Decent meat. Price is ok. Seems cheaper but the portion I wouldn't say is the biggest. The meat and seasoning is alright. For a quick meal, I think that's ok.

We had our first time dining at this outlet. Since we arrived quite early from the dinner service, we didn’t have to queue much.

The meat and side dishes are a great selection, and the meat is incredibly tender. The staff is also very attentive and lives up to their 3-minute rule of service.

A special shoutout to Ms. Nini and the rest of the staff for their exceptional service.

Weekday Monday night. Been 20 minutes and the raw food is not even here yet. Staff were overwhelmed. Only one auntie knew what she was doing and the rest just stand around clueless. Please train your staff better and change your service timing.

Huge place with many stalls serving local food and stores selling a wide variety of goods ranging from fruits to household items. Definitely check out the vibrant night market too. There's an MRT station and bus interchange within a short walk so it's highly accessible.

Good neighbourhood food Center at Clementi central opposite MRT train station.
Have not been here for sometimes (2 years) quite few of my favourite stalls have moved out 😢

Many people smoke everywhere, resulting in second-hand smoke exposure that is harmful to public health.
Public walkway is being obstructed by shops displaying items and poster banners, making the corridor very cramped and difficult to pass through. This worsens during raining as pedestrians are forced to walk through the congested walkway.

Bustling town centre. They have everything here. Huge hawker centre reuted for good food from Hainanese meat ball porridge to fish ball noddles, chicken rice, braised pig trotter, sesame chicken, roast meat fried kueh teow, whampoa Bean curd and chinchow/Bencurd combo drinks. All very authentic hawkers fare.

Happening post Covid times. Very busy during peak hour and when there are street food and pop up stalls. It becomes dead quiet after 9 plus at night. Not visible from Clementi MRT station and bus interchange but just 5mins walk away surrounded by Grantral mall, clementi mall and the low level HDB flats with 1st level shops.

FairPrice Finest is very conveniently located at Clementi Central, situated in the basement of The Clementi Mall. Being right next to the bus interchange and MRT station makes it an easy stop for commuters. The supermarket itself is huge and carries a "premium vibe" that significantly elevates the overall shopping experience compared to standard outlets. Whether you are doing a full grocery run or just browsing the mall, this store definitely suffices.

Really enjoyed the customer service here! Although I didn't speak well in Chinese and didn't know how to use the products, the two staffs I talked to there were really helpful! Would buy from again! Thank you!
I went to buy Charsiew, roasted pork and roasted duck. It looks delicious when displayed but is dry when I brought home even after microwave.

Well organised and spacious grocery store, with good quality products for reasonable price. It spans over two levels of different types of products with big headings to navigate around easily. Nice atmosphere to spend time walking around and shopping. The staff there were kind and friendly, very helpful in finding what we needed. I got some groceries and also tried out their sushi selection. It was pretty fresh and very convenient to eat from - a mix of salmon sashimi, salmon sushi, and cold soba noodles with dipping sauce.

So happy to have a fairpricr finest at clementi mall. It has all the groceries and food stuff needed for daily life. The variety of meat and veggies are very great, also got a lot of fruits. Only thing i am unhappy about is they dont provide the cheap smart choice bread jumbo pack, and the cheapeast fairpricr white bread is always short of supply. Expect a long queue at peak hours (5-7pm), but there are quite a few auto checkout machines do the waiting time is acceptable

Today at 3.10pm, me and my wife was served by a cashier named Wong Yu Qin. She provided us with patience and excellent service. She also has a pleasant personality. She sets a very good example for her colleagues to follow. Keep up with the excellent service. Well done 👏
Very good outlet of Fairprice to shop! So so fresh the fruits and vegetables. Mostly all the products can find here. Thank you so much
Kindly operate the self check out terminals, 2 elderly staff manually helping people to scan the groceries/items were too slow. Understaff? Cut cost? So much people, even at 11pm

What is the purpose of self checkout if you close it? It's barely 11pm but only 2 manned couters open. Took me 25 mins just to pay for my purchase! Won't it be more efficient if you have 1 couter open with another staff manning the self-checkout?

Big departmental store at the Clementi Mall.
Right next to lots of eateries.
Can take the shopping cart right to the parking lot.

Was getting some cigarettes from this 7-11 at 10ishpm you know routine stuff got the pack and paid, the Indian cashier behind the counter was nice enough. Next thing i know, i hear a voice that i can only describe as "Karen" like demanding to know how old I am. I turn around and i see this aunty in what i remember to be sports attire and blue eyeliner on the lower eyelid and wondered who the hell is this person. Of course I don't go around handing my ID to random people. After demanding a few times, she exclaimed "I am a 7-11 staff too!", I glanced over to the Indian cashier and he nodded in the most traumatized way possible, which somehow prompted her to say in smuggest way possible that she could show her "staff ID" to me. Anyways I showed my ID to the cashier and got the hell out of there. I don't tend to leave negative reviews at places but this one was really funny to me so i thought some of you may enjoy it too. Anyways funny and exceptionally negative experience, I would find another 7-11 if I were you. :)

I always encounter such nice and polite cashiers here. Today, a male cashier working around 9:30 pm helped fulfill my pick up order and was kind to replace missing stock with a different flavor. Thank you😊😊

Got this bakerland kueh bahulu from 711 and is crispy outside, soft and airy inside…  it’s screaming festive nostalgia tbh. Try all 3 yummy flavours:
🍯 original – the OG, sweet & comforting
💚 pandan – my fave, super fragrant and nostalgic
🍯 honey – soft, mellow, and made for tea time ☕

not too sweet, super light, and dangerously easy to finish the whole pack 😅

Got one Grumpy lady there and another middle aged woman. I told them about a deal literally pasted on the prices of the drinks, they say the deal didn’t exist. I politely ask them and they so rude say the line is busy, so rude only know how to talk. Genuinely horrendous, if y’all can’t even do ur jobs properly just go home and rest, no wonder y’all can’t find any other job than 711

This Indian lady working around 10:20pm on 7/3/2026,  is super rude and don't care about customers and keep talking with her boyfriend on phone with the earbuds.She blames and disrespects the customer.So many complaints are about her but no one cares ????

👍 Excellent service by Rahm, who went the extra mile to help to deliver my purchases to my house nearby. Was at the store and saw some great deals for drinks but hesitate to buy as heavy and bulky. He kindly offered to assist me for delivery to my house complimentary and 6 cartons of drinks were personally sent by him. Enthusiastic and kind service of him 👍

Last night, I went to this store to purchase a copy of the daily Shin Min , which I did occasionally.
It was at  23.11.44 HR dated 10-10-2025 and the person managing the counter for my purchase was a young female named UMA going by the receipt. At the counter, I noticed droplets of water on the desk and remarked that I cannot put the paper down for her to scan for fear of wetting the newly bought papers. She audaciously answered that she cannot be wiping it everytime a customer wetted it because of the ice-cream, cold drinks, etc that leave drops of water at the counter. I impressed upon her that the matter could easily be resolved by simply having a piece of dry cloth and wiping it away in-between customers when such need arises. I calmly explained that keeping a clean counter is part of good customer service which she disagreed. It may be a small matter, but  her attitude is uncalled for and it just show how lethargic (despite her youth) she was towards even a simple menial task to keep a clean environment for customers! What could I say? But to give her a 2-star review and hope she change for the better for the good of herself and the Company she worked for!

I ordered foodpanda from them, always missing items. I bought the briyani and drink combo and dint receive my drink. Then recently i ordered 2 lava cakes, missing one. Saw the reviews on foodpanda also same, they always get the orders wrong.
Cannot get my parcel as the Manager Maja said only QR code will be accepted even I got the OTP number! What a rubbish! Even the Ninja Van stated can collect parcel with OTP number! Bad service!
I felt uncomfortable as the staff came across as rude and judgmental. I believe the service can be improved, and I hope my feedback helps to make future experiences more positive

It's clean and well maintained enough given the very cheap price of using the gym. Wide selection of machines and equipment considering the space constraints so really no complaints. What more can you ask for.

I came here while I was a staff at SportSg, the staff there didn’t seem to like that I was entitled to free entry.

Did my workout which included bench press without using the clips and left. A week later when I came back the staff told me “I noticed you didn’t use the clips last time, remember to use it” in an unhappy tone.

Clearly this was personal as most people didn’t use the clips and the staff went as far as to remember me even after a week.

Not sure what they have against SportSg staff using Activesg facilities for free as part of staff benefit

Well-equipped and well-maintained equipment, friendly staff and all the areas of workouts are covered, what more you need in a Gym, if we can manage time well and be a little considerate, it should serve the purpose. I highly recommend an active sg gym if you want to avoid a colossal membership fee at private gyms and take full advantage of the facility for such an affordable price.

Standard public gym split across 3 areas:
1. Mezzanine level for cardio (treadmills...)
2. Cardio room on right side with treadmills, bikes, ..
3. Machines and free weights on left side as you enter, with a small stretching area.
Good variety of modern equipment.
There is also a hi-tech machine for your overall testing: simple test is about 5 mins, comprehensive one is 20+ mins

Changing and shower (cold) are shared facilities with the badminton/table-tennis users. (Note: not so modern)

C-19 impact: alternate machines are not available in the gym (3) for use because the overall space is limited.

Access: the gym is located at the back of the badminton courts, so keep walking from the entrance all the way to the back.

I used to come here quite often but eventually got sick of it because of the overall experience.

tl;dr Staff in this gym are sometimes really rude (It depends on the day). Badly maintained equipment. Only one squat rack (with crappy graduations), damaged safety arms. The one squat rack and 4 benches they have are also commonly hogged by some of the regulars who love to leave their towels there and disappear (leaving the equipment underutilised).

1. I've seen staff shout at gym goers (yes shout) for dropping weights, when the lifter clearly had ANC headphones in (literally "OI DONT DROP THE WEIGHTS."). Why not just tap on their shoulder or approach them like at Delta? Like another reviewer said, the staff should really learn from how Delta does it. In Delta, they actually approach you and talk you like a human being. Generally the staff here are way too rude. I've also seen some gym regulars there shout at other people. Sounded like a fight was going to break out. Not a good look.

2. They're busy enforcing all sorts of rules like no leaning against their false wall or no weighted dips. Come on, if someone weighs 60KG and wants to strap in an extra 10KG, how's that not allowed compared to someone who weighs 80KG using the dips machine? Instead of enforcing this, how about enforcing your no hogging the benches and SINGULAR squat rack during busy hours?

3. This is really an extension of the previous point. There are gym regulars there that like to hog the squat rack, leave their towel there, and then just walk around and chat with the other regulars. (I've also seen the same guy hog benches and squat racks for 5-12 minutes between set). Man isn't even around for me to ask to share. Somehow the staff are okay with it.

4. The one squat rack they have is REALLY badly maintained. The rubberised top layer of the safety arm was broken off in quite a few places. Not sure if they've replaced it since I last went. But it was wrecked for quite a while.

I will mention one plus side:
Despite what I've said about the overall environment and the hoggers, I have to say that most of the regulars there are still quite friendly. The ones who don't hog are typically happy to share equipment (you'll see this happen a lot in the machine section, not the free weights section). So if you're not into free weights, it's probably still worth going for.

Never going here again after I realised that Delta was just miles above. I'm willing to take a few more train stops because that's just how different these two branches are.
Really clean gym with well-kept equipment and handy lockers. The staff are super friendly and always make you feel welcome. Great place to work out!

As a member of Anytime Fitness club, I have global access to many branches of the club. While my home branch is in Toronto, Canada, i had the luck over one month after Lunar New Year to keep up my workouts at AF Clementi in Singapore.

I was visiting family in Clementi Heights and this AF branch was the closest to me, although I note that there are at least three other AF branches within a single MRT stop or a short bus ride. That said, I was very happy to continue my workouts at the Clementi location, especially with support from staff member and trainer Greg Tham.

Greg seemed to know everyone who visited the gym, and he was extremely helpful to me -- a relative gym newbie -- in discussing my overall goals (at the time just staying in shape and losing weight through LNY), as well as delivering valuable advice to improve my reps and sets.

I was also very pleased that although this gym is older than my Toronto AF branch (Garden District), the equipment and overall cleanliness of the location was excellent. I did take advantage of time away for long workouts and in a building that is well-air conditioned!

I understand that Singapore has more than 100 Anytime Fitness locations, but I needn't go anywhere else, as this one suits me just fine. I look forward to my next rip to Singapore and working out at AF Clementi.

Been a regular in this gym for years. Just want to come out here and give a honest review.

Awesome staff and coaches with great depth of knowledge in gym. Been to many gyms but the staff here is really welcoming and caring towards the members here.

Big gym with new hack squat machine 👍🏻 machines well maintained with cloth and sanitizer provided.
Amazing gym! Great equipment, positive vibe, and professional staff. I always leave feeling energized and motivated

Greg is an exceptional personal trainer whose thoughtful approach and attention to detail truly stand out. He’s fully invested in my progress. Training with Greg feels both structured and personal, which makes all the difference! 👍🏻

This is the place where you can exercise or play sports. They have sports hall for badminton, basketball indoor and outdoor, and many other sports activities. It has two swimming pools of olympic size and a children pool.

It has indoor air-condition dance studio.

You can use Active SG to book and access scheduled activities in the Sports Centre.

Transport to Sports Centre  is convenient. Nearby eateries are within walking distance. The Sports Centre is just within Clementi Central. The wet market and food centre is just about 200  metres away.

This place is a one stop place for family and friends where they want to play, swim and having together for the entire day. The government made it possible for the residents near and far to use the facilities to cultivate a healthy lifestyle. Yes. This is a place to recharge physically and mentally, to foster family and friends gathering and a healthy community.

After renovation in May-2024, the toilets and shower rooms are improved. The ball cover nets for table tennis are improved; much neater and less chance to slip. The flooring is also improved. As of Sep-2024, the air flow in the hall becomes better than last May just after renovation and the temperature becomes bearable now. Tables are mostly improved but some tables (especially 3, 4 & 5) are old and the bounce are not so good. I have also noticed the gym room, which I have never used so far, has some improvement too. Vending machines for drinks are available as usual. Water coolers are also available for free too. Badminton courts there are very popular too. Sometimes, they transform into basketball courts. Actually, just at the outside of the hall, there are two free-to-play basketball hoops. Bicycle parking is available but not sheltered.
Big sports hall for badminton, pickleball and table tennis but could be quite warm during the play.

Good sports recreational centre. Predominantly indoor badminton courts and table tennis courts. Has an indoor and outdoor basketball court as well. vending machinea has some healthy snacks options. Sports drinks vending machines available as well. Has a separate complex for swimming.

I think there are lot of hits and misses with this Sports Centre. I like that there is an open area outside the Sports Centre where they put 3 basketball rims for people to use. Massive big sports centre with many courts to offer. To my disappointment, the Sports Centre does not offer any spectators style of sitting area. So for parents waiting for children lessons to end, have to vie for the badminton court bench or sit on the floor. There is also a cafe outside the sports centre but interestingly, no one is selling anything. It is more of an air con seating area for people to sit around for free.  A lot of vending machines outside of the Sports Centre for drinks choices.

Facility is good but the place is suuuuuuuper hot and stuffy. Not enough ventilation. There’s no aircon, no fan and no natural wind even. Feels like a big sauna inside, even before starting exercise….

Very amazing sports hall. I go there to play weekly and have a lot of fun. Court is in great condition and they renovate very often (which sometimes can be very annoying because there will not be any courts available). I would surely had given 5 stars if it was not for 1 thing. The floor is full of dust which sometimes can make it very slippery. Hope they can improve on that thing. If they do, I will surely give 5 stars no doubt!

P.S. Do not trust anyone who say the wind is heavy as they are lying

Appreciated they already furnished the floor, much much better than before.
BUT! The wind is so heavy, it brings in the rain drops, making it extremely dangerous to play there. Me and my friends nearly lost our knee and hamstring. The nylon mesh netting is in place, it was there since long long time ago but  they are not effective at all.
I'm sorry to make these comments but safety is always FIRST priority.

Sarah, did a wonderful job in explaining the details of the redeeming process! But it is the APP that needs to work better in telling customer of the promotion and discount is usually before or after because I am still making payment in full and I did not see any notice at all for a discount being credited or not, nor also a confirmation if the discount is provided?

For apps feedback, please do call me too for a next follow up at 93624477 if possible and update me of the discount whether is it being credited back to my activesg account would be great! Cheers
Nice place for badminton

It is a great place for people of all ages to stay active and practice swimming. It features three different pools, including a children’s pool, a standard pool, and a deeper pool for more experienced swimmers, making it suitable for different skill levels. The facilities are generally well-maintained, and the environment is comfortable for regular visits. Occasionally, there are additional activities such as water play features for children, which add to the overall experience. It’s a good and accessible spot for exercise, learning, and family outings.

Clementi ActiveSG Swimming Pool is a great place to swim. I like the cooling temperature of the competition pool and the shower. The crowd is not too many on a Saturday morning.

The shower water is strong and cooling. There's water cooler to refill water.

friendly staff
good for lap training
I will come again

The pool is next to the AYE expressway but you don't get the noise disturbing from it.
And next to the  swimming complex there is an indoor stadium for others recreation sports activities.
The pool is relatively quiet in the morning, only uncles and aunties comes for their morning swim. It will be crowded on weekends as there are swim school conducting lessons.
The mrt station is just a 7 mins walk. Also many eatery near so if you are hungry after you swim, you can just fuel up. Do take note that the pool is closed every Wednesday for maintenance.
The main pool is open after the repair ( June 2023 )

Clementi central Bus interchange and MRT station are 10 minutes walk away, so transport is a breeze.

Food options nearby (Hawker Centre, Coffeeshops, etc) are *chef’s kiss*—perfect for refueling after your swim.

Right next to the swimming complex, there’s an indoor stadium where you can get active with other sports-basketball, badminton, table tennis.

Mornings are pure peace—mostly OGs (aka uncles and aunties) getting their laps in. Weekends? Total chaos, thanks to the multiple swim, wushu, dance schools running lessons.

it is a small but convenient mall that I often visit to watch movies at the cinema and grab a meal at Saizeriya. While it is not very large, it serves its purpose well with a few dining and entertainment options. Its location is a major advantage, as it is close to Clementi Sports Complex, The Clementi Mall, Grantral Mall, the bus interchange, and Clementi MRT Station. This makes it easy to explore the surrounding area and visit multiple places in one trip. Overall, it’s a practical and accessible spot for a quick outing.

Kumar Mess quality was sub standard. Not worth visiting again. The cost is high than little India restaurants but food quality was below average. Chicken soup was not bad,  Mutton varval had no flavour, nandu omelette was poor quality, vegetables were poor taste. Overall the restaurant charged me 69 dollars fir two persons. I have eaten at Madurai Thalakulam Kumar Mess. The quality has dropped drastically. Advertising was power but food quality need improvements.

Please avoid this place if you can. The male dentist is horrible. He claims he knows everything and gave a lecture about tooth bevel. My front tooth filling ended up super horrible, the gum receding is worse than before the filling and after he tried to fix, the middle line of the tooth is off even after I told him not to carry on. This kind of dentist can give good lecture but cannot deliver results. I think he is not very good at listening as well.

321 Clementi is a small shopping mall that has a variety of shops in it.

The entertainment facilities include Cinema and game arcade.

The services include Dental, Hair, Chiropractic, salon and clinic services.

Food options ranges from Chinese, Western, Japanese, etc.

They also have gym facilities provided by fitness first.

There's a art shop as well.

They have 2 level of carparks.

We're at the Koi shop. All one could see are busy hardworking staff fulfilling the orders spooled to them by the self service terminal which helps productivity a lot as there's no need for an order-taker and a cashier. The backend of the shop is fully visible to anyone. A notable discovery -  there's a staff recruitment notice at the shop, setting out the key info that a prospective job applicant needs to know. 👍

It is a vibrant hub offering food like Burger King, a gym, basketball and table tennis courts, and more. It hosts events such as career fairs and volunteer opportunities. Conveniently located near West Coast Park, Clementi Woods Park, Kent Ridge Secondary School, West Coast Market, and West Coast Plaza, it serves as an active and accessible spot for residents to eat, exercise, and participate in community activities.
There is a lion dance troupe that practices every Friday at 7

Exchanged my steps tracker there by appointment. Had my trace together token exchanged due to flat battery too. A convenient and airy space. There is a fast food outlet there. There are ATM machines around too. A good location to book private hired cars too.

Review: September 2022
Staff at the CC are polite & helpful & hope that it remains such.  The  management should regulate training offen & remind their role in community center .   The facilities are getting better and useful for all.
The POSB VTM alongside the ATM'S.
Video teller machine (VTM) is an electronic banking outlet that allows customers to complete basic transactions, request banking services or receive immediate assistance with the help of live video conferencing consultation with a remote bank teller.

Review : November 2021
The staff working aren't trained well in their interacting skills , more offen than not during my visits which I've noticed.
Just as I was registering my entry,  distance away from those seated staff & they questioned me as to what I wanted .
The committee club is obviously for everyone who needs to be updated or for a particular cause.
The staff should be well mannered & ethical to handle any visitor , by NOT being prejudice.

Great place for community activities, event hostings. There is a big hall, basket ball court. Do check out for the upcoming events on the notice board. Extra curricular activities, dance, music classes are also held for children. Had a chance to view the FIFA World Cup 2022 final screening.
Super good playground. Hope it maintains and is well taken care of by the residents. Location is superb also because you can go buy your kopi Peng from the nearby coffeeshop while your kids play

Name is a little misleading as nothing here is about firefly unless the graffiti. Many kids playing basketball. Minus 2 stars due to inaccessible designs. Many knee level walls built in the estate to prevent global access to the park. Only through a small walk way as entrance. As such, I have to jump down the short wall which may break my knees. Imagine elderly. Built in a new estate.

Love this park, and the pleasant and relaxing atmosphere surrounded by beautiful greenery. Some common floral and fauna spotted.
Different floral at different times of the year.
Good place for hanging out. Air is really fresh, it has a basketball court and playground. We had a class party at Firefly park. Kids friendly. Really good place.
Pleasant park with 2 playgrounds, trampolines, a basketball court, 2 fitness corners, small running track and an amphitheater!

There is a large playground in this park, with an elderly exercise area and a basketball court. This is a good place for the residents to exercise and have fun and that this park has facilities for all age group to enjoy. However, there is no toilet in the park so you need to go to the nearby food court for toilet. Overall a good park and I have fun here enjoying the scenery and playing at the basketball court with my friends.

🌳Clementi Neighbourhood Park is located at the junction of Clementi Road and Clementi Street 13, bound by Maju Forest and Brookvale Drive.
🏟️🥋🪑A sparing neighbourhood park with an Amphitheatre centrepiece, several Pavilions, an Adult Fitness Corner and strategically located benches along its footpath.
🌳☔A little tired-looking green space, but nonetheless serves the nearby Clementi residents who prefer the tranquility it offers, to the more popular Ulu Pandan Park Connector.
Great neighborhood Park. Top chat. Chat. Ten out of ten chat. What's your chat? Mysterious...
Is a very quiet park,normally not many people as when i passed by.

The Clementi Nature Trail offers a beautiful experience with its lush greenery, untouched beauty, and dense forest. However, currently, there are no signs or directions available, looks like that trail is still under development.

Clementi Nature Trail forms part of the Clementi Nature Corridor that would eventually connect up with Holland Green Linear Park and onward to the Rail Corridor.
At the moment, only this stretch of the trail is completed.
This stretch of Clementi Nature Trail offers a generously wide path covered with short soft grasses. Running next to Ulu Pandan Road winding up a gentle slope it ends at a forested area (not connected yet, so you need to U-Turn back).
As the young trees were newly planted, it may not provide enough good shade.
Nonetheless, enjoy the tranquility it offers.
Trail path seems to be laid but the metal boundaries yet to be removed when visited on 4 Jan 2026

The Clementi Nature Trail is a scenic nature walk located in the Clementi area of Singapore, offering an escape from the city’s hustle and bustle. The trail winds through lush greenery and is popular among nature enthusiasts, hikers, and bird watchers. It provides an opportunity to explore Singapore’s rich flora and fauna, particularly the biodiversity of the surrounding forests and woodland areas.

The trail connects various parks and green spaces, making it a part of the larger network of nature reserves and trails that promote outdoor activities and environmental conservation in Singapore. The Clementi Nature Trail is suitable for those looking for a peaceful outdoor experience, with well-maintained paths and opportunities for leisurely walks, photography, and relaxation in nature.
Those for who seeking bird singing and need quiet  moment.

This spot is indicated on Google Maps. It's a green zone in the heart of Singapore with thick foliage and quite a lot of wildlife to be seen. As of October 2025, there are some rough dirt trails running through this nature area but, in wet conditions, the trail will be quite slippery. The local authorities are planning to put a developed trail through the forest but as yet, only marker tape is in place to show where the trail might run.
Safe travels,
Mega.

On 8th March 2025, Saturday morning, I took the MRT train to King Albert Park Station and then took bus 74 at King Albert Station and alighted four bus stop away opposite the Maju Camp. There is a path behind the bus stop where I alighted leading to the old jurong railway, from there I walked towards the direction heading towards Clementi Forest. As you walked, you will come across muddy streams and fallen trees and it is best to carry a walking stick with you. It was quite tough walking the path leading to the Clementi Forest but I made it and enjoyed it...
Nice forest trail and one can experience nature and wildlife without leaving the shores of Singapore.

The most amazing hike I've done in Singapore. It's a bit tricky there are streams to jump across and very steep sections you have to climb up or down. Would recommend proper footwear and backpack and sunhat. It rained very heavily when we were halfway through but that made it more fun and helped us cool down

A deep forest surrounded by residential areas, but this is true nature.
If you want to challenge this forest, make sure wearing waterproof shoes and trousers that can be washed easily, even better with an alpenstocks, or the muddy roads will teach you a lesson.
The entrance is not easy to find, so ask an experienced guide will be better.

Buffet - Korean Bbq Halal

Very very surprise at the meat quality. Went there 2 times this month.

Very pleasantly surprised. Pls order the top range of the buffet menu.  You get to order those meat in the pictures shown.

These are items different from those on the buffet counter and the quality is good. As a meat lover you will enjoy these meat and be surprise by the quality.

Will come back definitely.

Excellent value for money.  Beef and lamb was tender although you can tell its tenderised.

Free flow of everything, deserts drinks, condiments, kimchi etc.  regular tier is good enough

Only two staff in the floor but they work hard and kept all in good order.

Will definitely be back
Crystal is an excellent manager and continues to bring smile to customers faces! Thank you for being so warm and personable! Fast food service too!

It was a wonderful lunch with my wife and her best friend at Seoul Garden in Clementi Mall. The place itself was clean, comfortable, and had a nice, cozy vibe perfect for a relaxing meal.

We enjoyed a wide variety of food options whether for grilling or making a delicious hotpot. The ingredients were fresh, and the choices were impressive! One of the highlights for me was the kimchi it was exactly the taste I had been looking for and did not disappoint.

To top it off, we ended our meal with some ice cream. Although the chocolate flavor had run out, the strawberry was still really good. All in all, it was a great dining experience. We left full and happy definitely planning to come back again!

The best outlet for Seoul Garden. Best food, best service. The staff there are amazing. From the cashier, to the people bringing the food to those cleaning and tidying tables. All are amazing. 3 times I went to this outlet and they never ever disappoint. Hands down my favourite. Much love to the staff there ❤️

NParks Allotment Gardens scheme provide opportunity for gardening enthusiasts to hone their skills; and even grow their own produce. Small elevated garden plots are on lease through balloting at various parks, heartlands and estates around Singapore under the scheme. A great way for seniors to get active and spent time outdoors.

place_name: Beach Road Scissors Cut Curry Rice - Clementi Mall
review_text: warm and comforting curry. soft vege and wet style. very nice. came on a Saturday evening. staff very friendly.

place_name: Beach Road Scissors Cut Curry Rice - Clementi Mall
review_text: I do not want to say anything bad on this store. Owner pls look into it with your staff.

place_name: Beach Road Scissors Cut Curry Rice - Clementi Mall
review_text:
Ordered pork chop curry rice but the experience was quite disappointing. Initially, the staff passed me chicken chop instead of pork chop, which already showed a lack of attention to the order.

After correcting it, the pork chop I received looked very off in colour, slightly greenish and not fresh-looking at all. When I raised this concern, the staff brushed it off and said it was due to their marinade to “soften the meat.” Honestly, that explanation did not feel reassuring.

What made it worse was the staff’s attitude, which was dismissive and not customer-focused. Instead of addressing the concern properly, it felt like they were just trying to brush it aside.
Hope the stall takes this seriously and reviews their food handling and preparation standards.

place_name: Beach Road Scissors Cut Curry Rice - Clementi Mall
review_text: Tried the curry rice from this outlet, and it’s the same great taste! The curry was rich with 3 types of flavours including high coconut, mild spicy, and black braised sauce. The cashier auntie was friendly and detailed in her work. The curry was so good that I had to ask for (free of charge!) an extra bowl!

place_name: Beach Road Scissors Cut Curry Rice - Clementi Mall
review_text: Food is nice, better than the one at Jln Besar.

Visited a friend and i don't see crack tiles everywhere. I understand that their shuttle bus is permanently servicing the residence to clementi which is a must for this area considering the walk in from clementi is quite far. However, they have a bus stop on along AYE which is just 4-5mins walk. I would say it is not as inaccessible as i first thought it was....
