Is a very well- kept condo ...suitable for small family as the floor area not very big. But its environment is nice ...got a very well built roof garden :)
Met an old friend here. The place looks nice. We lost in touch soon after. Pity
viewed two units. location not bad, but the workmanship is terrible. it is ridiculous that a 5 years condo has peeling tiles everywhere and broken sewage filter here and there.

It is a cosy place to stay. Ambience of a resort. Without telling you more, you will be thinking as if you are staying in resorts such as  Phuket, Bali, Pattaya or even Seychelles. Reasonable size function room with nice blueish swimming pool coupled with nice beautiful clouds. The units of each apartments ceiling is about 3m high. Behind it has a big n nice public park. Built n designed by World Class but minimum it a class of its uniqueness. Hard to come by with nice and big landscape. Nowadays it is difficult to find a condominiums with such a big and scenic landscape.
Can see very beautiful sunset in the evening
My favo place for morning walk 🚶‍♀️🚶‍♀️🚶‍♀️🚶‍♀️
NEA has done a great job with what little space this country has to create a lush green corridor.
Many bird-loving photographers gathered together without safe management measures.
estimate to fix by end of 2023

Ulu Pandan Park Connector is a well-loved green corridor in the West, stretching alongside the Ulu Pandan Canal. It links key residential areas like Ghim Moh, Clementi, and Pandan Gardens, while connecting to other major trails like the Rail Corridor and Bukit Batok Park Connector.

It’s a great route for walking, running, cycling, and even birdwatching with wide, well-paved paths and plenty of greenery along the way. The connector also passes by several small parks — including Faber Heights Park — and offers occasional rest spots and fitness corners. While not the most scenic path in town, it’s peaceful and practical, with good connectivity and a strong community vibe.

I’d rate it a 4 out of 5. It’s functional, accessible, and enjoyable for daily exercise or a weekend ride.

Amazing place to walk/bike.

Lots of trees for cover and separate lines for walking/cycling.

There are a lot of people especially during evenings and weekends, but there’s enough space for everyone.

The views of the river are beautiful and you can hear and see a lot of birds.
A nice place for jog or walk. After part of the PCN closure (due to the landslide at the construction site of BTO), there are lesser cyclists passing through this section of the connector.

This is quiet PCN that runs along the canal, large enough to fit all mobilities, on both sides of the canal. There are breaking up to cross roads but only a few easy to cross (no pedestrian bridge)
You will also find an old railway bridge and a spot where eagles stay around.

Too much air pollution from fumes generated from PUB work around the canal especially Clementi Road, Sunset Way and Ulu Pandan Road junction. Also noise in the evenings can be heard by residents living in the area.

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 463 Clementi
review_text:
Today is my first Parent Teacher Conference Session!

Teacher Zhi Ning and Teacher Pooiyan have share about the progression of my child in school such as her strength and weakness. Those example are very useful so I can assist my child to guide her at home to improve and continue what she is good at!

With the clear examples given by the teachers earlier, I know my child is well take care by each of them!

Each time when I pickup my child from school, teacher will also tell me  “1 update of the day” This simple update makes me feel the connection with my child daily.

Thank you again Principal Janna for this good culture and environment which you have set for the school!

Thanks for also sharing about the sweet moment which you have seen of my child when you are there in class too!

Children are happy learning and parents are “Fang Xin” too!

I’m really thankful for my child to have My First Skool Clementi being her first school experience.

Looking forward to the next Parent Teacher Conference Session!

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 463 Clementi
review_text:
I am writing this review to express my deepest appreciation to Principal Janna & teachers who have dedicated themselves to my daughter at My First Skool.

My daughter’s immense progression is a testament to the collective & coordinate effort of the teaching team, specifically Teacher Zhi Ning & Teacher Hui Ern. Rather than working alone, these educators operate as a seamless unit. Each teacher involved in her journey has brought unique strengths, providing tailored attention, structured cognitive development that provided a nurturing environment that caused my daughter’s confidence & skill to soar.

The synergy between the Principal & the teaching staff sets a gold standard for early childhood education. I wish them all nothing but continued success & hope to see them recognized for their merits as they have clearly earned it.

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 463 Clementi
review_text: Really thankful to the teachers and staff at Infant Class “Love.” Special thanks to Ms Lela and everyone in the team for always showing so much care and patience. As first time parents, it gave us great peace of mind knowing our son is in such loving and capable hands. We truly appreciate all that you do every day!

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 463 Clementi
review_text: I have a good experience in the parent teacher conference. The teachers are able concise feedbacks on my child and there is two way communication.

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 463 Clementi
review_text:
This Teachers' Day, we would like to convey our deepest appreciation and gratitude to the teachers and staff here at My First Skool ❤️

My daughter started playgroup 2 months ago and took some time to adapt before settling down. We would like to thank Ms Rissa and Jinni Laoshi, who were extremely patient and caring towards her! We witnessed how they comforted and reassured my daughter whenever she was upset, helped her with lunch when needed, and ensured her safety when they went out for outdoor activities. We also appreciate the time and effort Ms Rissa and Jinni Laoshi put in for the weekly updates - my husband and I love them and always look forward to seeing pictures of what our daughter has been doing in school 🥰

Both the principal (Ms Janna) and the Deputy Centre Lead (Ms Deanna) are very genuine and approachable 😊 We have also noticed that the teachers from other classes, who occasionally helped to bring my daughter in during morning check-in, were also friendly and warm - often greeting her by name with a smile before carrying out the daily checks and leading her in to the classroom. Their kindness and gentle support have made all the difference in helping my daughter feel safe and loved, so we are really grateful for that.

Thank you to all the staff at MFS for creating a nurturing environment where our daughter (and other kids) can grow, learn, and feel happy. We feel at ease sending our child there, knowing that she is in good hands :) Wishing all of you a very Happy Teachers' Day, we appreciate your dedication and effort so much!!💕
Attended ACJC's traditional biennial funfair. Such an exciting event! Kudos to all the students and teachers who made the event so grand.

Not only does this school love sucking out money from its students to allegedly maintain facilities that 1. students NEVER get to use and 2. NEVER become cleaner or better in condition, they also enjoy developing bladder diseases in their poor students.

Teachers, especially the discipline master, ridicule and punish, sometimes even forbid, students from simply using the toilet. A 5 minute trip to the toilet to empty one’s bladder from holding it in for hours is seen as an act of sin, a crime being committed by this school that praises itself for being so loving with the grace of God.

During chapel, assembly, or school events where students are gathered in the hall, using to the toilet is seen as a threat, a crime. Students will either be held captive in another room where they will not even be able to see what is going on in the main hall to appreciate the event, will be told to take the route that is much longer to come back to the hall (which makes absolutely no sense as one would have to walk past much more people if their reason is disturbing the course of event), or simply, told no and scolded for “not being able to hold it in”.

Teachers say to students in a humiliating, condescending tone that if they cannot hold their bladder for more than a few hours, they should “get it checked”. This is either implying that students should not stay hydrated or using the toilet is undesirable, when it is part and parcel of how the normal human body works. Over time, with students not being able to use the restroom when they urgently need it, kidney and bladder diseases may be manifested. Urinary Tract Infections are amongst one of the many diseases students would have a much higher chance of having after holding in their urine for extended periods of time just to adhere to some school expectations. Not only that, it is simply extremely uncomfortable sitting still with a full bladder.

I sincerely hope the school will be more considerate of students’ health and needs and be more understanding.

I went to ACJC this morning at 11:30am as I wanted to purchase a swimming paddle for my child. At the entrance, the security guard Mr. Liao asked me to register and requested for my IC. I simply asked him whether it was necessary to provide my IC, but instead of explaining politely, he responded in a rude manner and told me not to “talk too much,” threatening not to let me in and even challenging me to complain to the authorities.

I believe such an attitude is not appropriate, especially within a school environment that should set an example for students in communication and respect. If students were to overhear such an exchange, what impression would they be left with?

I sincerely hope the school can look into this matter and ensure better communication and professionalism from the staff. Your improvement will be very much appreciated.

Wonderful Junior College providing a holistic education. Tremendously benefitted from my experience here at ACJC in 2003-2004, with lifelong friendships and bonds still going strong 20+ years later. Blessed to be part of the ACSian family, with teachers and even our former Principal being ever supportive of our individual endeavours. The sports facilities were fantastic too from the track to the swimming pool and gym, I credit a lot of my sporting journey from this too. Thank you, ACJC! 🙏🏻

The location is strategic, as it sits between 2 mrt stations, and surrounded by many eateries and shopping malls. The sport complex is big, there's football field, running track, swimming pools, as well as badminton court.

After 15 years, NJC's boarding school (only) was officially closed in 2024 to return the land for state use.

These images hope to honour the memories of those 500 students and teachers boarded there every year

For more at #shuutur

Very disappointing. The secondary level teachers dont really teach. I was expecting some principles to be taught in class so I kept quiet and observed my kid's progress. Wasn't taught. Disappointed. They also got 1 alumni to teach while waiting for her A results. Teachers not teaching in way that can be understood. The school relies on private tuition and hard self study. A few teachers talks disrespectful like are sarcastic. Student prefers the previous principal

One of the famous schools in Singapore and undoubtedly with lots of heritage, history and culture. As you step into the school you feel the nostalgia and history working together to build a wonderful, hopeful and resilient new generation one after the other.

Shoes and shoelaces that are garish in nature are not allowed. W school, W people, W teachers, and most importantly, W chillies, popcorn and math.

Everyday I go to school and I look forward to seeing my math work. The piles and piles of math homework does not do itself. It sure is great experience and practice!!!

The accidental cohesive nature of this school is truly great. All that gleams is not gold. The equal funding given to the aesthetics and science departments are truly interesting.

I recall the days I went to NJC. Oh my, it has been quite a bit... I can recall the days we got our exam results back. I remember the smiles our teacher tried to hide from the students when they gave us those earth-shatteringly wonderful results. They smiled so wide, so wide we could barely see the tears forming in their eyes as they looked at our shattered expressions in pride.

The teachers love teaching, sometimes a little too much. I see their beautiful teary-eyes smiles upon their weary faces whenever they step into class. I see it, I feel it, I know it, it is ingrained into each and every one of their fabric of reality. As an alumni of this school, I missed the days of boarding. The chaotic thwacking of doors whenever we closed a door was truly inspiring.

The myths are not true. We do not have a leaky roof. In fact, it is the best roofs of all. The roof's whole is about winning. In fact, it is so winnable that it has always allowed water through it. It has never lost its purpose in letting water through.

One of the key problems today is that math is undervalued in this society, and that there is too much emphasis on the pursuit of arts. What even is the use for arts? Useless pining? Useless uselessness? Useless emotions? None of these things are needed. Join NJC, for you will see the world for what it is, through good, or through better, or through best, and that, is through math.

Our integrated programme is good. No, the best at INTEGRATING!!!! Our streak of streaky teary students crying tears of joy at the sight of their beautiful eggs received on their math papers is unmatched. One must join this institution to truly understand the meaning of life. For it is to MATH.

I truly apologise for this accidental cohesive multicultural heritage-ridden serviced and honoured post. NJCians truly deserve more math homework.

Teachers are unable to teach. You need to engage tutors to get your child to understand the material. They overload the students with homework and you pray that your child survives this barrage of tests, the humiliation of failing and the lack of proper help to improve. Don't send your child here unless he/she is prepared to weather the storm and you are prepared for the many teacher/parent sessions.

​SIM GE offers a top-tier "Global Campus" experience in Singapore. By partnering with prestigious universities from the UK, Australia, and the US, they provide high-quality, internationally recognized degrees.

​What stands out:
-​Diverse Academics: Access to world-class curricula (e.g., UOL, RMIT, Birmingham) on one campus.
​Vibrant Student Life: Over 80 clubs and modern facilities that support a holistic education.
-​Career Support: Excellent career services and networking opportunities that prepare you for the workforce.

Very well facilitated and clean toilets in here. Blk A Classrooms are generally better in comparison to older ones in NP Blk 72 next door. As a SUSS student, I prefer classes in our own Blk C. However, the library in Blk A (SIM) is much more equipped than Blk C (SUSS). Did I forget to share that Foodclique here owns the cafeteria in SUSS?? Game-changing

One of the nicest things about Singapore is how even university campuses have beautiful, peaceful corners.
While exploring a campus with my friend, we came across a small fish pond with a gentle fountain in the center.
The sound of water and the colorful fish swimming calmly made it such a relaxing spot.
It instantly became one of our favorite memories from the visit—simple, quiet, and unexpectedly beautiful.

Singapore Institute of Management offers academic and professional programmes focused on practical skills development. The institute provides modern facilities that support learning and career readiness.
I had a tour visit and lunch there. It was a great experience, the campus is huge, and the food at the canteen is affordable and tasty for students.

I often take the bus from this stop, and it tends to get very crowded during peak hours, especially in the mornings. Many students use it to travel to SIM, Ngee Ann Polytechnic, or Kent Ridge Secondary School. Because of the high demand, buses can sometimes be full, and my friends and I from Ngee Ann Polytechnic have almost been late for school a few times. It would be great if the bus frequency or bus size could be increased to better accommodate the morning crowd. Overall, it’s a convenient stop, but crowding can be an issue during peak times.

“Why is Singapore’s bus system so bad? Buses don’t run like a pendulum — instead of coming one by one at 5–8 minute intervals, I have to wait 15 minutes for two buses to arrive at the same time. What kind of management is this? SMRT really needs to fix it.
Take bus 201 to go to Opposite Kent Ridge secondary school bus stop.

The MRT is near a mall a market and food centre. There is rest room. There are some coffee shop near here too. The place is extremely crowded in weekdays, weekend is over capacitated. Needs expending badly.
201 is always late and leaves someone running to catch up

I read good reviews about the roasted chicken that it is soft, tender and juicy, somewhat like Kenny Rogers. But it was real disappointment that mine came out dry and overcooked, definitely not juicy. Just the taste is good. The risotto is watery on the base, taste is ok. The waffle chicken is regular. The smoked chicken truffle carbonara is good. Overall the food is above average, but for that price, I expect it to be better. Ambience is good, in a nice HDB neighbourhood, soft music background, clean and new interior, and not too crowded on a PH when I patronized. Vegetarian options are indicated in menu.

Just found this place which I believe opened only in Jan 26 (Mistake - was informed end 24)

Had the Truffle Carbonara, Cheese Baked Rice and Beef Bulgogi Don. Each item was about $14-$18 and though not cheap per se being in the neighbourhood, but items like the Cheese Baked Rice was done very well, much better than Swensens which has become too commercial. Thus, you get really burnt Cheese that had crisps by the side. The Truffle Carbonara had really nice bits of chicken, but we felt the sauce was too sour. Also had the latte and Butterscotch latte and the butterscotch coffee was really good. Guess its a coffee bar after all. Nice touch was the free flow of water, thus rest of my family who did not get any drinks could still have water alongside.

Ambience wise, was really a nice cosy hang out in the neighbourhood. Went on a public holiday, so only two tables were having meals, half the place were customers using their laptops with only a drink in sight. Saw they had a sign discouraging study, thus quite certain it always has many customers hanging around with just once drink due to its cosiness.

Service wise, think overall still alright, only thing was our stuff took awhile to come out, even though we were the only other table having our meal. But guess maybe that's why our Baked rice came out well done, maybe because of the time taken to do it up well. Was told reservations not taken for public holiday, but guess did not need any.

Hidden gem in Clementi. Set below a cosy HDB it's easy to miss this. This is my kind of place, very quiet , not too crowded, and a very friendly waiter/barista..
Ordered the breakfast set. Sunny side up was over cooked, the eggs didn't have a runny texture but other than that liked the remaining items. The mushrooms were great, sourdough, the right texture.
What I really liked was their attitude and service. They got me an Oatcap and when I mentioned I wanted it later after the meal, the waiter proceeded to make a new coffee for me later. Appreciate the flexibility !! And he was kind to explain different menu options when I stepped in..
Will definitely come in again !

Stepping into Monday Coffee Bar, one is met with a calm and unpretentious atmosphere. The interior is thoughtfully arranged, offering a comfortable setting for both solo visits and small groups.
The menu provides a range of coffee drinks alongside light meals and pastries. The food itself is straightforward—neither remarkable nor disappointing, just familiar and consistent. It fulfills expectations without standing out, making it a safe choice if you’re looking for something simple. The beverages are well-prepared and maintain a reliable standard.

Service is professional and unobtrusive, allowing for a relaxed experience without any fuss. Overall, Monday Coffee Bar is a dependable café for a casual coffee break or light meal, offering consistency and a pleasant environment.

The breakfast food is ok, tuna prawn sandwich is a stand out. the drinks are great, love their coffee blend man. So good we came back for coffee weekly
Counter staff service can be better and pls don’t charge $3 for a babycinno…
Ordered the fried ramen. Nothing inside except a badly fried egg on top. Totally not worth the price. Portion is miserable. I could have eaten Maggi Mee at home and it would have tasted the same.

Had a great experience at Nene Chicken. The staff were friendly, attentive, and served us with a smile. The food was fresh, delicious, and arrived promptly. The restaurant was clean and well-maintained, making the overall dining experience enjoyable. Keep up the excellent work! ❤️
the food is really really good the fried chicken tenders r so juicy and the service is super good too

First time trying Nene Chicken in SG and I’m satisfied! 🔥 The chicken is super crispy on the outside and juicy on the inside. The sauce is amazing especially the spicy one, it really hits 🤤 The portion is big too, very filling. The place is clean and comfortable. Definitely coming back again!

Highly recommend this shop for does who love chicken, overall👍  good delicious food & also good stuff taking order fast and food
If I not worng that is one stuff name fariz who is take my order overall job well done guys👍

Exceptionally a perfect getaway peaceful place!
The charming small cafe is located in the same building with primary school in Clementi Woods Park, a pleasant greenery peaceful place for hiking, exploring natures in the breeze morning and a delightful brunch.

The food exceeded my expectations. The savory waffle was absolutely delicious! The Egg Benedict had poached eggs cooked with bagel sesame, perfectly done.

The waffle was thick and chewy, satisfying in texture, and went perfectly with the toppings. The owner was creativity in the dishes and excellent coffee.💞☕️

Highly recommended to soak in a personalized and exceptional dining experience!💞

The café is run by one person, who puts great care into both the coffee and the food, and is very warm and welcoming to customers. Today, on her recommendation, I tried her homemade cookies with coffee—absolutely delicious. And, my breakfast was great too!
It’s located just down from the car park, next to a kindergarten, inside Woods Park. It’s a quiet and peaceful spot, perfect for relaxing and enjoying a nice cup of coffee.
I found a great spot today.

Quaint and cozy cafe tucked away in Clementi Woods! The owner is a 1-man show but she's very friendly and the food is delicious (see how I emptied the plate 😆) I ordered the grilled cheese and ham sandwich.
If you stay nearby it's worth making a trip down :) But note they are closed on weekends and otherwise open from 8am-4.30pm!

Auntie Doris is very sweet and caring. I went there with my parents and we had lengthy conversation and very good food. Not a second we felt like we are in a Cafe, but it was more like a home we were invited to have a breakfast in. Very silent atmosphere with soft music inside this play school pulled me back into my childhood.

A very cute and cozy cafe tucked away in the corner of a building next to a preschool! I went to grab lunch and study, and the lady was very kind and friendly. My friend had a similar experience where he went and ended up talking with the lady there for a long time. The grilled cheese and ham sandwich was delicious and I really enjoyed the atmosphere there. I wish I had more time to visit again!

Fantastic supermarket located within walking distance of the NUS University Town featuring a terrific selection of products as well as remarkably reasonable prices. There is a great combination of both local goods and imported goods. Very organized and easy to find whatever it is you may be looking for.
We would like to compliment the staff service especially Aw Jian Chong (fish counter) for his excellent service and patience adhering to our requests.

We always enjoy shop there their staff is so friendly n food is fresh.
Whatever we need they will have.Cheers

Edit: 2025 June, it is now opened 24/7!

Advertised to be 24 hours however it's no longer 24 hours. The selection of goods available is vast, from groceries to electrical appliances to DIY sections etc, but if you're expecting something more exotic, might have to make a trip to fair price extra at Clementi Mall instead.

Good selection of fresh produce, including meat and fish. The place is not that large but still packed with stuff. But make sure to remember that this is Sheng Siong, not Cold Storage, and have the right expectations. (For instance, I don't think I found fresh herbs like Thyme or Rosemary.) Friendly staff who will often try to sell you specials while at the church our counter, but not pushy.

Staff Kang did the Extra Miles for customer  by offering to order stock from F&N supplier on 5 June 26 and told us to pickup next day for 3 cartons of soft drinks. (352 Clementi Avenue 2)
The soft drinks in shelves are individually, it's difficult to pack and carry for customers while cashing out.
I am very surprised staff of Sheng Siong are helpful and friendly with the added services, normally others supermarkets will say all stock display or no stock.
Keep it up and Cheers to All Staff of Sheng Siong.
🛒 Sheng Siong @ Clementi Ave 2 = budget hero! Great value on daily essentials, aisles neat & promo tags everywhere 🏷️. You always spot a deal — stock up, save cash, shop happy 💡. #ShengSiong

Absolutely in love w this Sheng Siong! It's in a very accessible neighborhood with two bus stops and a parking lot right next to it.
Open 24 hours with a huge range of selections with unbeatable price.
Best part is the staff are super warm, and helpful, which you don't see a lot around Singapore.

Be warned - it gets very crowded over the weekend and things run out faster!
Come during a weekday lunch break or after an evening run is the best :)

Next to the Sheng Siong is a long winding ramp from Block 352 that's about a storey high. Those riding bicycles or electric scooter can reach this supermarket in Block 352 and just next to Block 352 is Block 353a which is a small hawker centre and market
Very nice... Similar to Fair Price.. the prices are quite affordable..

Absurdly inflated prices, and the meanest staff in the country (itself an achievement given the standards of customer service in convenience stores) mumbling incomprehensible words behind a mask. It will end up hastily closing like the cold storage that preceded it unless management steers it in a radically different direction.
The two guys working there are very friendly. They generally do a good job keeping things in stock too.
Just opened today. Variety of selection, especially fruits, drinks, frozen good, ready to eat food, everything there. Looks great.

I love AF Clementi 354! Cozy 2 floor gym, with the cardio machines and racks on the first floor, while you have pin loaded machines on the second floor. You can find almost everything you need to hit all body parts. Highly recommend if you stay near the area!

ABSOLUTELY AMAZING! This Anytime Fitness branch is hands down one of the BEST gyms I’ve ever been to! The facility is spotless, modern, and packed with all the equipment you could ever need. But what truly makes it exceptional is the staff — especially Mel. She is an absolute superstar! Friendly, motivating, and incredibly knowledgeable, Mel goes above and beyond to make every workout fun, inspiring, and energizing.

Every visit feels like a boost of positivity and encouragement. The energy she brings is contagious, and she genuinely cares about helping everyone reach their fitness goals. I honestly can’t recommend this place enough — it’s more than a gym, it’s a community full of support, motivation, and awesome vibes.

If you haven’t been here yet, you are missing out BIG TIME. 5 stars are not enough to describe how amazing this place and Mel are!

A gym with a great functional training space, equipment and a really welcoming vibe. The staff are always friendly and helpful, and I appreciate how quickly new equipment gets added. They’ve even kept my lost-and-found items safe for me, which says a lot about their care. I genuinely love training here!
very clean and well maintained gym with a comfortable atmosphere. the staff are friendly and welcoming, especially mel who is always helpful and approachable. always a great experience here!

Gym is pretty spacious and has plenty of Matrix machines & cool attachments. However it is extremely hot and stuffy. Out of 7 aircon on the second floor, only 2 are turned on. There aren’t even 10 people upstairs now - I can’t imagine how much stuffier it would be during peak periods!

Located on the 2nd floor closer to Blk 210A, this is probably the biggest and tallest climbing playground in the entire Clementi town! For kids aged 6-12, pls check for hot surfaces before playing as advised by the notice.

This playground can also be accessed via the stairs near Blk 207.

beautiful brand estate with lovely lanscaping, loads of amenities like Sheng Shiong supermarket and Kopitiam corner. It is also very well maintained and cleaning. therevwas a cool breeze blowing and made it even nicer.

A new BTO development in Clementi, which features playgrounds, fitness corners, and connected to the PCN. Expected more additions by the 2nd quarter of 2024 include Sheng Siong supermarket and food establishments.

A very nice place and have alot of playground also good sunset location
The bad thing is that Google have wrong pictures on one side and correct pictures on the other side

HDB residential blgs.
Next to PCN.
Next to Clementi Fire Station.
Next to Newly Relocated Clementi Police Station
Sheng Shiong
Kopitiam

An all-rounder HDB apartment that creates a new wave of intergenerational living under one roof. Most blocks are connected with sheltered walkways that link most of the apartment complex as well as creating a more accessible-friendly pedestrian experience with Sheng Siong, Kopitiam Corner, 24/7 Laundromat and necessities stores all within walking distance of one another.

​The park's main activities are conveniently clustered around McDonald's, which serves as a great central spot for dining, socializing, and recreation. Aside from active play, it is a fantastic place for gatherings, featuring camping grounds, barbecue pits, large open lawns for picnics, kite flying and jogging.

West Coast Park is a long but relatively quiet park that stretches along Singapore’s western coastline. It’s a wonderful place for a relaxed outdoor day, especially for families with kids

One of the highlights is the large playground, which is one of the better ones in Singapore. The climbing structures, slides, and play areas keep children entertained for a long time, and there’s plenty of open space nearby for running around

The park is also great for cycling and casual walks. You can easily rent bicycles or family pedal carts near the entrance and ride along the park connectors while enjoying the sea breeze

For those who enjoy spending more time outdoors, the park has BBQ pits and camping areas, making it perfect for weekend gatherings with friends and family. There are also scenic spots along the waterfront where people fish or simply relax and watch the ships in the distance

I do like to come to this nature park by the sea - to take in the salty air and the cool sea breeze, both of which relaxes and then invigorates me. I like to hear the waves crashing against the shore and to be among the greenery in this nature park - in particular, the bamboo fringed walkway as regards the latter. The amenities at this nature park is adequate - with enough car parking spaces and public toilets. And overall, this nature park is well-maintained.
Well maintained park and a good place to spend time alone or with family and friends. Suitable for all ages, whether to exercise in the mornings or catch a lovely sunset in the evenings.

West Coast Park is considerably smaller than East Coast Park (ECP), but it remains a highly viable zone for R&R. I have scouted the perimeter and identified several optimal coordinates with good sightlines to hold position and decompress.

Civilian population density in this grid is minimal. The decreased foot traffic results in a secure perimeter and a highly tranquil atmosphere—ideal for lowering your guard and recovering stamina.

Be advised on two minor variables:

First, visuals on the maritime border are compromised and do not match the aesthetics of ECP.

Second, heavy transport vessels are active in the adjacent waters. My MJOLNIR armor’s environmental sensors occasionally detected industrial exhaust drifting into the area. It is non-lethal, but noticeable without a helmet filtration system.

Overall assessment: A solid, quiet sector for downtime. Proceed to coordinates at your discretion.

We’ve had such a wonderful experience with Zee Learning Cove! The teachers are incredibly attentive, caring, and genuinely dedicated to each child’s well-being and development. They create a warm, nurturing environment where kids feel safe and happy every day.

One thing we truly appreciate is the strong communication with parents – we’re always updated on our children’s progress, daily activities, and any concerns, big or small. It really shows how much they care.

The principal is always approachable, supportive, and hands-on, which gives us even greater confidence in the center. We go to work each day with peace of mind knowing our kids are in great hands.

Highly recommend Zee Learning Cove to any parent looking for a childcare center that truly partners with families and puts children first!

I couldn’t be more grateful for the incredible care, expertise, and support my child has received at Zee Learning Cove. From day one, it was clear that this is a place where every child is seen, heard, and nurtured with patience and love.

The staff and principal Ms Bel are deeply compassionate and truly committed to helping my child reach her full potential. They take the time to understand my daughter’s unique needs and strengths, and create personalized learning plans that works.

The school also does an amazing job involving families in the process. They offer regular updates and practical strategies we can use at home.

I would like to extend my heartfelt thanks to Ms Bel and teacher Sabrina for the care, attention, and genuine compassion you have shown towards my daughter.

If you’re looking for a school that specializes in early intervention with heart and skills, look no further.💕

Zee Learning Cove provides exceptionally careful childcare. Teacher Sherine ,teacher Bel and anll the teachers are attentive, nurturing, and deeply committed to each child’s well-being. Safety and personal development are clearly top priorities, and they take the time to understand every child’s unique needs. It’s reassuring to know that children are not only cared for, but also genuinely supported in a warm, enriching environment. 👍👍👍👍
My child has been under the guidance of Teacher Sherine, and the progress has been truly heartening. With her support, my child has become more confident, able to communicate well with others, and has developed strong independence.

As a working parent, finding the right learning environment for my child was one of the most important — and emotionally loaded — decisions I had to make. Zee Learning Cove has truly become a trusted partner in my child’s upbringing.

The environment is warm, welcoming, and thoughtfully designed to make children feel safe and curious. What impressed me most is how observant and attuned the teachers are to each child’s emotional and developmental needs.

During a recent parent-teacher conference, I used the word “naughty” to describe a particular behaviour. The teacher gently and respectfully reframed it, reminding me that every child is unique — often expressing themselves in ways that reflect their needs or feelings. That moment really stuck with me. It reflected the deep respect they have for each child’s individuality, and their commitment to partnering with parents, not just managing children.

Zee Learning Cove gives me peace of mind every day. Knowing that my child is not only cared for, but truly understood — that’s everything to me as a parent.

Zee Learning Cove stands out as one of the most exceptional child care centres we’ve experienced, offering highly personalised EIPIC programs that are thoughtfully tailored to each child’s individual needs. The team—led by the dedicated Principal Bel, along with Teacher Sabrina and Teacher Sherine—demonstrates unwavering commitment, attentiveness, and genuine care for every child’s growth and development.

What truly sets Zee Learning Cove apart is their focus on holistic development: from attention to tasks and social interaction to building confidence and independence, they prioritise what truly matters in early childhood education.

Under the nurturing guidance of Teacher Sabrina, we have witnessed remarkable progress in our child. She has blossomed into a confident, communicative, and independent individual, and we are immensely grateful for the care and expertise provided.

place_name: Simon Eio Learning Centre - Primary, Secondary and JC Math & Science Tuition @ Clementi
review_text: My son used to score below average in Mathematics during primary school and began to struggle even more when the syllabus became more demanding in Primary 5. After enrolling in Mr Eio’s classes, we saw a remarkable transformation. His understanding and confidence in Mathematics improved significantly, and he went on to consistently achieve A grades throughout upper primary and secondary school. We are immensely proud that he attained double A distinctions in his double Maths in the O-Level examinations. None of this would have been possible without the dedicated guidance and excellent teaching of Mr Eio.

place_name: Simon Eio Learning Centre - Primary, Secondary and JC Math & Science Tuition @ Clementi
review_text:
My daughter, Denise has been with Mr. Eio since Primary 6. Within just one year, her Math improved tremendously—from a C to an A. Mr. Eio is a great motivator and has a wonderful way of helping students understand Math more easily and quickly. He is truly an excellent teacher.
My daughter has just received her O-Level results and achieved A1 for both A-Math and E-Math. The teachers and administrative staff are all amazing and very supportive. I highly recommend this tuition centre—don’t hesitate to enrol your child here.

place_name: Simon Eio Learning Centre - Primary, Secondary and JC Math & Science Tuition @ Clementi
review_text: Mr Eio is a really great teacher to have if you wanted constant improvements in every lesson. Being very detail-oriented, he focuses on the process and steps on answering difficult questions, while ensuring complete understanding from his students. Due to his diligence is guiding me, my Math results has improved from a B4 to A1 within one exam in Sec 1. Thank you so much Mr Eio for making me realise my love for Math and Amath🤍

place_name: Simon Eio Learning Centre - Primary, Secondary and JC Math & Science Tuition @ Clementi
review_text: My girl doesn’t like Math, but she loves Mr Eio’s lessons. His teaching style makes difficult concepts easy to understand, and his jovial personality makes lessons very interesting. Eventually, my girl improved, and did well in her O-Levels ✨

place_name: Simon Eio Learning Centre - Primary, Secondary and JC Math & Science Tuition @ Clementi
review_text: Mr Eio can tame a lion. My boys are very active and easily distracted if the lessons are boring. But they love to attend Mr Eio’s Maths classes and shown huge improvement from AL 3 to AL 1 for P4 boy and consistently AL 1 for P6 boy ❤️🥰

place_name: Comics Library at West Coast Community Centre
review_text:
To use this library you need to register your passion card or ez link first. Talk to the community center guy for more help.
It looks like they brought all of the books from Anchor point

place_name: Comics Library at West Coast Community Centre
review_text:
Library is in the community centre. Large collection of comics. Kids can read there or borrow books home.
Regsitration to enter is more cumbersome though... need to register 3 days prior to visit, or onsite before 6pm. Hope they can look into that to allow more spontaneous registration.
Borrowing is through NLB app, so all is good!

place_name: Comics Library at West Coast Community Centre

review_text: Again remind me with david
Nice place
I had such a fantastic experience at Clementi MRT Exit D! The service was incredibly smooth and fast, making my commute a breeze. Highly recommend! 🌟🚄💨
A lot of nice food. I like the 88 chicken rice stall. The uncle serve the roasted/steam chicken in separate place. Price also very reasonable too.

A very convenient bus stop, it's directly in front of the West Coast Market Square, there is a Community Centre, a shopping mall (West Coast Plaza) and 2 schools (Grace Orchard School & Kent Ridge Secondary School) nearby. There are buses that brings you to Clementi Mall, Clementi MRT Station and Clementi Bus Interchange (Bus 175, 201 & 282) as well!
Curry rice used to be good. But recently, price shoot up so much. Ask for price b4 order.

Wet and cooked food market. Busy normally with resident but crowded during lunch from the surrounding industrial parks. All prices reasonable.
Several hawker stall that recommended is the Lucky Star hainanese curry rice, mixed rice( no name at corner stall beside the chicken rice) stall, 88 chicken rice, Hup Kee braised duck rice, ka le Yong tau foo, Soon Heng fish ball noodle and Yon Yam fried bee hoon.
Specially mentioned the Soon Heng fish ball noodle and Yon Yan fried bee hoon which both have always long queue and sold out very fast.

West Coast Market Square or Blk 726 Clementi West Street 2 Market (not to be confused with the nearby Ayer Rajah Market and Hawker Centre located at Blk 503 West Coast Drive).

New premium bus service no. 726 plying from Teban / Pandan Gardens - West Coast - CBD - Beach Road, from 15 November 2021.

This is one of the very good fish soup stall if u are in the west coast/ Clementi area.  Their fish is fresh, and they are not over-cooked.  Hence fish slices having a succulent texture.
Their next gem is the large minced pork ball.  Apart fm the golf ball size, they are also very flavorful.. (becos the minced pork is marinated with fine dried flounder fish powder, giving a rich umami flavor).

Quite similar to Grouper King’s minced pork ball.

Will be back!

Don't worry about quality, we have quantity, best sums up West Coast Market Square. Truth to be told, if not for the fact that I have to go to Inchcape Centre for car servicing, I would not step foot here. While there are many stalls and food options are readily available, the quality of food here leaves more to be desired. Characteristically of all large food centres, many stalls here sells the same items, and repetition is common place. After eating at 7-8 stalls, the only decent stall is the duck rice stall. All the other stalls cannot make it. Sorry to say, the place is over rated. Both vegetarian, noodle stalls serve mediocre food. It must be tough for the residents there to live with such a mediocre food centre. Give this place a miss. Not worth travelling here for a visit.
This econ rice is so good. Huge variety, tastes good, super well priced. $3.5 for my eggplant, tomato egg, and meatballs.

Great food here. A place where we can get authentic traditional hawker food. Like coming here, good food, good vide to get a sense of the old laid back Singapore feel. There are also many sundry and food supplies around the market. A great place to be.

$3 a plate of fried oyster! I took a second look at the price. Yes, $3,$4,$5. One of the most economical fried oyster you can get. Many food stalls here. Not to bad, but i prefer those that are crispy rather than starchy. He has a mix of both, i would say great for a quick fix, small plate $3 for teatime! Opp west coast plaza.  Cash only. #01-187

The ice cream and waffles are tasty! I had cookies monster and waffles. The service was also commendable. Good neighbourhood location too. Nice place for friends to hangout.Thanks to Yuai and Yuan Sheng for accommodating to my request. Cheers!

really enjoyed their gelato and waffles! the waffles were crisp at the edges but had a chewy centre, which made every bite really enjoyable 😋 the hojicha gelato had a strong tea fragrance without being too sweet, while the pistachio one was another good and safe choice.

for a sweet & savoury combination, you can even get their mentaiko tater tots that were freshly fried and went perfectly with the umami mentaiko sauce 🍟🥔

Crispy, golden waffle topped with a scoop of creamy ice cream—simple, delicious, and perfectly balanced. Warm meets cold in the best way. Definitely a must-try.

Great waffle café with a cozy vibe. Friendly service, and fair prices. Definitely worth a visit!
Great friendly and fast service. The waffle is nice but abit dry today. Anyways hoping for paddlepop flavour!!
really amazing one of a kind waffles and unique ice cream flavours and super conducive to chill or study  will come back again! 🍨

place_name: Wen Kee Live Seafood 文记活海鲜
review_text: Absolutely delicious! The price are damn worth it and value for money, all the seafood were all very fresh and the portions were generous. All the crabs was absolutely amazing! One of the Best live seafood I ever tried 👍 Highly recommended Wen Kee Live Seafood 👍👍👍

place_name: Wen Kee Live Seafood 文记活海鲜
review_text: The food is awesome and cheap. Fresh seafood must come here.

place_name: Wen Kee Live Seafood 文记活海鲜
review_text:
If you’re looking for a place that delivers both quality and value, this spot is definitely worth a visit. The black pepper crab has a standout—rich, flavourful, and surprisingly affordable for the quality you’re getting.

What really elevates the experience is the service. The staff are attentive, hardworking, and genuinely friendly, making you feel welcome from start to finish.

Great food, great service, and great value—it’s the kind of place you’ll want to come back to and recommend to others.

place_name: Wen Kee Live Seafood 文记活海鲜
review_text:
Went during mother's day crab promotion.. Crabs were fresh.. but the crab beehoon was wayyyyy too salty, didnt manage to drink the soup. Salted egg squid and yam ring were nice.. Fermented curd fried pork was disappointing..

It was busy night due to mothers day.. so the service was abit slow...

place_name: Wen Kee Live Seafood 文记活海鲜
review_text:
The food was delicious and wonderful.
Also, price was affordable and it was definitely value for money.
The owners were very friendly and service-orientated.
My family and I will definitely frequent this stall
👍👍👍

So far the taste of penang food here is the closest to real penang food at penang.
Char Kuey Teow is nice although lacking a bit of wok hey.
Laksa is nice with all the correct ingredients and make sure you need to add in the prawn paste.
Cendol is nice here although the ice is a bit rough.
Will be back for more next time.

Food : Mostly slightly above average good. The meat was fried till too dried when we visited.

Service : Normal service.

Atmosphere : Aircon Zichar

Price : On the high side, considering it is an Aircon Zichar place.

Ordered the Fried Kway Teow ($7), Kway Teow Soup ($6), Prawn Noodle ($6) and Assam Laksa ($6).

The Fried Kway Teow had a slight hint of wok hei, which was encouraging, but overall the flavour felt quite muted. The savoury depth and caramelised sweetness you usually expect from a good plate of char kway teow wasn’t quite there, it leaned more towards lightly seasoned rather than bold and punchy.

The Assam Laksa broth was noticeably diluted. It lacked the usual tangy intensity and complexity that comes from a rich tamarind and fish-based stock. There were only a few small bits of sardine in the bowl, and I didn’t notice any torch ginger flower (bunga kantan), which is typically a key aromatic ingredient that gives Assam Laksa its distinctive fragrance and sharpness. Without that element, the soup felt flatter than expected.

The Prawn Noodle was decent, the broth had some sweetness from the prawns, but it didn’t have the deeper umami richness or porky intensity that makes a bowl memorable. It’s passable for a quick meal, just not particularly standout.

The Kway Teow Soup was probably the most straightforward and reasonable of the lot. The broth was light and clean-tasting, with simple, comforting flavours. Nothing fancy, but easy to finish.

Overall, if you’re coming here, manage your expectations, the flavours are toned down quite significantly compared to what you might expect from more traditional Penang-style stalls. That said, taste is subjective, so you might enjoy the lighter profile more than I did. Worth trying once to form your own view.

Had a satisfying meal at Island Penang Kitchen near West Coast. The highlight for me was definitely the Assam curry fish head. The fruit rojak was refreshing and well-balanced, with that addictive sweet-tangy sauce. A comforting spot for Penang favourites when the craving hits. 🍽️✨

I like the fried kuay teow too. Yum!

Don’t bother coming here. Fried kway teow is ok but lacking wokhey. Asam laksa is a total disappointment. Watery, no prawn paste, no fish meat. And the service staff behaved like hawker centre aunties ie they were overly eager to clear every plate as soon as it was empty even though we were still eating ; reaching across the table and over our half eaten plates as they did so. Don’t know what was the rush since we had dinner at 5pm and there were still empty tables around. So NOT COMING BACK. Instead, you should just go to ABC Market Jason for Penang fried kuay teow and asam laksa, or to Upper Bukit Timah food centre for the Penang fried kuay teow. Way better, at a lower price point and without the annoyance.

West Coast Plaza is a pleasant small-to-medium sized neighbourhood mall with a good mix of restaurants, cafes, enrichment centres, retail shops, and everyday essentials. It has a relaxed atmosphere compared to the larger malls in Singapore, which makes it comfortable for casual shopping and meals

The mall is especially convenient for nearby residents, with useful tenants like Cold Storage, Anytime Fitness, Starbucks, Toast Box, and various dining options spread across the different levels. There are also quite a number of enrichment and tuition centres, so the mall feels family-oriented and community-focused

The layout is spacious and relatively easy to navigate, although some parts of the mall can feel a little quiet during off-peak hours

Quaint shopping mall in the neighbourhood. One of my most frequented malls in Singapore. Overall a pretty well-rounded shopping mall with many shopping options like Mr DIY, Daiso, Puma, Cold storage etc. While this shopping mall does not have a Macdonald or food court, there is a subway, and a newly-opened Popeyes. Those looking for cheap bubble tea can go for Mixue, or Fun toast and Toast Box if they are craving for coffee. There are also claw machines in a corner if you are into that kind of thing.

My daughter goes to Mindchamps and learns Taekwondo at Ildo at West Coast Plaza, so we frequent it every now and then. I love the Cold Storage, Starbucks, Puma, Wine Connection, Sanook, and other shops. It's a good and quick way to unwind and relax with some retail therapy.

They also have Candyland, which is my daughter's local favorite indoor playground.

It is a convenient and well-rounded mall, especially for residents living nearby. It offers a good mix of facilities, including dining options, toy stores, pharmacies, tuition centres, and gyms, making it suitable for everyday needs. The mall is usually quite lively, with regular events such as sales promotions and small activity setups in the central area, sometimes including playgrounds for children. Overall, it has an active and family-friendly atmosphere, making it a practical and enjoyable place to visit.

A quiet mall frequented mostly by locals. Has cold storage with good wine collection, two wine shops, Korean provision shops, parlours and eateries. Prices are mid level. Good for small kids for its indoor (paid) play area.

Since moving back to the west from the east, Sheng Siong has become one of my usual supermarkets that I frequently get my groceries from. The supermarket is spacious with a wide variety of selections, and everything is always neatly arranged. The vegetables and fruits are fresh as well.

The staff there are also very friendly, especially two ladies whom I encountered.  Darchana and Santos are very helpful and professional. Darchana always smiley and very helpful whenever assistance is needed. Small gestures like these really make the shopping experience more comfortable and pleasant.

I was in a rush so I didn’t manage to take more pictures, but I’ll update with more photos the next round.

Cashiers are fast and friendly. Will remind customers if they are Chas or Pioneer on the designated days.

The 2 storey Sheng Siong is 24 hours. Appreciate the long operating hours.

Peaceful shopping can be done after mid night.

Early in the morning, there's staff to help butcher pork to ur desire. Choose a piece of meat and they minced for u. Pork Belly if not much people, they can slice it for u.

Fish counters operate till later timing then pork. Large selection of prawns, fish, crabs.

Outdoor area, there's plant section, veg section and promotion section.

Variety is huge!

Efficient, tidy, with enormous range of food, groceries, household appliances and sanitary products that you might not find in normal grocery stores.

The clean and well lit enterprise also keeps jaywalkers and suspicious people away, there used to be provocatively dressed women harassing motorcyclists for cigarettes years ago. They and an ever rising count of shoplifters have been dealt with swiftly by the authorities.

It helps that the staff are very friendly and welcoming to all cultures and preserve the Singapore spirit of orderly and honest business.

24-hour supermarket at West with a wide range of food & many varieties. Never worry about being unable to buy fresh seafood cod this outlet 24 hours yet can find almost everything we need. From food to household items, too

I love supermarkets, especially the bigger ones. I miss the old Sheng Siong Supermarket at Tekka Mall, which had unfortunately closed years ago.
This outlet seems rather big to me; Spacious with a great selection of affordable daily essentials. A really good place for grocery shopping.

** 4% special discount for seniors (Singaporeans/ PRs) 60 and above, every Tuesday and Wednesday.
**The Tem Tem Protein Tempeh Chips sold here are suitable for vegetarians, healthy and super delicious. Get yours now for the coming Chinese New Year. You’re worth pampering.
Great product offering. Owner is really kind and helpful. Really enjoy my time shopping grocery here. Highly recommended.

Delight Supermart offers products at good value. There are many offerings that i cannot find anywhere else.

The shopowners are really kind everytime i go there, always caring and going the extra mile.
The staff was excellent in tending to my needs despite the peak hour crowd. Super patient. Also found a lot of my childhood treats there. Highly recommend!
Excellent service . Hygienic environment.Neat and fresh vegetables. Paynow and NETS accepted.Frozen food also available.My go to shop😄Must visit
Great selection of goods. Shop owner is very kind helped me find my goods

A neighborhood convenience store, strategically situated to meet local needs, offers easy access to a variety of fresh produce, microwavable items, and packaged foods, including toppings for cup noodles. However, it's worth noting that the store, though new and well-located, is relatively small and may pose challenges for wheelchair users due to limited accessibility.

Pros:
Location, exactly where the neighborhood needed
Obvious location
New, so all fresh food are stocked up

Cons:
Not so wheelchair accessible
Too small
Lack of variation for food choices

This is a very convenient 7-11 in the neighborhood, where residents can easily buy fast food, snacks, and daily essentials. With many children living nearby, it’s also a practical spot for families to grab quick treats. Overall, it serves the community well and is easily accessible.
Abit cluttered on the exterior of the store. Experience would be better if store personnel are more attentive

Right next to the bus stop.
Caters to Seahill and other nearby condominiums.
Great addition to the neighbourhood! Small but well-stocked store, items are always fresh, and staff is very kind.
Fresh and quality poultry! They now have pineapple-fed chickens too and is even tastier than normal chickens! Price also very reasonable, advance orders comes with vacuum pack services somemore!
The chicken is so fresh and the owners and staff are friendly. Plus, the price is great! And the delivery of fresh groceries is super convenient.
Really impressed with the freshness and quality of the chicken here. The staff were friendly and helpful, and the grocery delivery was so convenient. Definitely a store I’ll keep using.
Responsive seller! Delivery was fast and efficient! The crabs were fresh and value for money! Will definitely patronise them again!

Please go elsewhere if u want what u ordered.

I placed an order for red grouper days in advance, but on the scheduled delivery day, I was informed there was no stock available. I was not notified of any potential issues with my order beforehand. This is completely unreliable.

The shop owner initiated the cancellation before offering any alternatives. If you dismiss every customer's question about why this wasn't communicated prior to placing the order as abusive, then that's your own perspective.  Unfortunately, this is a he-said, she-said situation.

And to correct, 90% of my order value was for 2 red groupers that was meant to host my guests for the same day. Unsure how the owner deemed to be just one item that was missing from the whole order. Also,  the shop owner states that "the whole wet market did not have grouper this morning", not sure why, but i managed to get my red grouper from the wet market on the same morning after my order was cancelled.

I wish you all the best in your business. :)

Anytime Fitness West Coast Plaza has a spacious layout with a good variety of equipment for both strength and cardio workouts. There are plenty of machines, free weights, and functional training areas, so it generally doesn’t feel too cramped even during busier periods. The gym also has the usual Anytime Fitness convenience of 24/7 access

However, cleanliness and maintenance could definitely be improved. Some machines looked dusty, there were areas with visible litter and overflowing bins, and overall housekeeping did not feel up to the standard expected for a premium gym membership. A few parts of the equipment also appeared worn out and could use better upkeep

It's a decently sized space, considering how everything is so cramped in Singapore. The place is in need of a revamp, though. The functional area and studio are huge and could be put to better use.

I'm happy because I can be caveman and do my unga bunga lifts, but would be nice if the plates didn't have a sticky residue on them. A good clean and refreshed equipment would be neat.

Some machines are too faulty to be adjusted. Tried adjusting the machines to fit my smaller body proportions but got cut in the process. Leg curl machine cushion has been replaced with a matt since i joined in jan and could have been there for quite long already? Hope the machines can be replaced soon 🙏🏻

The gym floor is spacious after renovation. It is more powerlifting friendly - 3 deadlift platforms, 3 squat racks. Couldn't say the same for the plates which are aging and worn. Plenty of machines for legs like hip thrust, hack squats. Too bad most of the gym-goera here couldn't appreciate this. They have a spacious functional training area and group classes too. There are plenty of shower stalls here as well. This would be an ideal outlet for me if it wasn't for the distance (i live in Yishun).

Anytime Fitness at West Coast Plaza is a modern, well-equipped gym. Accessible 24/7, this branch is particularly spacious.
With a wide range of cardio and strength training equipment, a functional training area, free weights, and spaces for class.
The gym's atmosphere is conducive and well maintained. Overall a clean, spacious environment for users in the area.
New-looking and brightly colored playground with guard rails for young tots.

New playground update for 801 West Coast ParkView Playground just dropped, with the slide receiving a fresh coat of blue paint. This highlight of this playground is a lookout tower that children can climb to reach a hammock where they can relax before sliding down the spanking-new spiral slide.

For little climbers, there are Playworld climbing squares with tread blocks that provide grip and help prevent slips, ensuring safe play. Thoughtfully designed ledges around the blocks give kids extra support for their hands, making climbing easier and more fun.

Adjacent to the tower, there’s a pirate ship-themed Playworld playground. It features engaging play panels where children can turn handles to activate sounds or solve puzzles—perfect for imaginative play. To top it all off, the playground includes gentle mounds decorated with delightful sea creatures, adding a whimsical touch to this family-friendly space.

The newly constructed apartment in the neighborhood includes a notably modern and well-designed playground. A wise decision was made to separate areas for younger and older children, ensuring a more suitable and safer environment. The classifications make sense, with open spaces and safer equipment designated for younger kids. The playground is wheelchair-accessible, and numerous overhead shelters are available in case of rain. While everything appears well-executed, a potential improvement could be considered for the choice of colors in the design.

Pros:
Assessable for wheelchair and bicycle
Location
Safety
A lot of overhead sits in case of rain

Cons:
The choice of color for the playground

It’s a new estate with two new playgrounds. We were just staying a couple of blocks away (the older blocks) but we brought our kids to play here as it’s new. Apparently the kids enjoyed the new facility and we hoped it would be well maintained over the years. The place is very windy at night. Interestingly, I’m currently writing this review seated by the side of the playground while the kids are playing.
New play area for kids for weat coast park view residents.
Two nice slides 🛝, but could do with more equipment and probably more seats with shade.

My daily morning charge up spot. I go here regularly for yoga, fresh air and morning sun. Cozy grass and shady tall trees for the perfect picnics and naps. Plenty of space. Beautiful flowering plants outline a well equipped children's play area with slides, see saws, swing etc. Adult exercise station present with 5 to 6 machines for training upper and lower body. Covered benches present to shelter from the rain if needed. Very clean and well maintained. Perfect place for jogging, exercising, picnic, kids, the elderly, walking dogs etc.
Suggested improvement: can provide a water fountain for drinking water.

On November 27, 2005, West Coast Place Playground was opened to the public by the then Member of Parliament for Ayer Rajah SMC, Dr. Tan Cheng Bock as part of the upgrading project for the residents of West Coast Gardens. There is a plague that commemorates that auspicious eventful day as one sits in a sheltered gazebo with two large-size white-picket picnic tables. There is an open-air gymnasium to workout in the open space under hot sun and a huge playground for the kids to enjoy a great play date with friends and families.

At 8.27pm 4.5.22, this playground park is in total darkness. Why are the lights not switched on? Aren't they sensor controlled? Danger lurks for one who walks through it. Parallel to the park, weeks ago on 26.4.22, it was total darkness also at the Drive road as ALL the lights lining both sides of this road blacked out from the junction with the main road right through the whole length of Drive. So dark it was that a sense of danger popped up as one walked through to the destination at the other end. 😩

It's now been beautifully decorated ahead of National Day.

This review is only on the kids play area. (With the swing and slides).
The facility is ageing. Some areas of the rubber floor is wearing off, spewing shreds of flakes around - which is quite a hazardous condition for the children.
Not the best lighting post sunset, which also needs sprucing up for safety of park goers.
There are child care centres close to this park, so an upgrade could really benefit the community.
A newly renovated playground for children. Children can play swing, slide, and run, cycle inside the playground. With big old trees, the playground is well sheltered

After the ABC active beautiful clean PUB upgrading for 5 years. Finally there is peace and greenery. More animals and diff plants.
And chickens could fly high up the trees till level 2 -3. Gallery 18/18.
----
There are more variety of birds but the squirrels disappeared.

A pair of cats between Blk 607 and 613.

The water stream, reflection and sunset is beautiful.
Simply Beautiful & Tranquil.
Nice green space by the canal with plenty of benches to sit and relax.

This particular Park Connector rans along Sungei Pandan Kechil section of West Coast Road to West Coast Highway.
It's a short 450 metre paved path.
Despite its short length, it's heavily utilised.
Residents living around the estate would walk or jog up and down this short stretch of the Connector in the morning and, in the evening.
The path would be exposed to  full sun in the early morning hrs. And seniors just love walking or do stretching exercises in the sun.

A very pretty park connector that’s perfect for running and cycling, linking West Coast Park with Clementi Woods Park. I often see people jogging here, and with the new HDB and elderly home completed, it’s even more convenient. It connects residents to nearby bus stops, the community centre, and other places easily.

Our son has been in Chatsworth since he was in infant care, and now he’s in Pre-Nursery. He has always been quite an anxious child and I’m so thankful for the teachers and staff in Chatsworth for supporting him through his journey there 💕

He now looks forward to and enjoys school (though still cries sometimes at drop off) and playing with his friends and teachers. He talks about good memories in school and is really growing in his confidence. Thank you Miss Nicole and Deng Laoshi for all your care and efforts! We also appreciate all his past teachers and other staff in school ❤️

PS: The school is absolutely beautiful -  gentle lighting, lack of clutter and surrounded by greenery

My daughter loves this school! She’s been attending since pre-nursery till K2. The premises are beautiful and the teachers are excellent. Classes are very wide ranging, plenty of arts and crafts + science and math. Highly recommended.

Very nice staff, great access to greenery and awesome indoor playground. I only expect a bit more sharing on the daily schedule.
Overall very happy that I enrolled my daughter here. Lots of gratitude for the kind teachers Ms Aoi and Huang Laoshi!

Chatsworth Preschool Review – A Disappointing Experience

When I enrolled my child at Chatsworth, I did so with the belief that paying a premium fee would translate into a more holistic education and a higher level of care and support compared to local government kindergartens in Singapore. Unfortunately, this was not the case.

One of the most concerning issues was that my child went without an English teacher for almost eight months. This was shocking and deeply disappointing. Although I considered withdrawing my child, I hesitated as I didn’t want to disrupt his adjustment to a new environment again.

I also found that the school’s curriculum did not align well with the requirements for local Primary 1, making the transition more challenging. Additionally, with a large proportion of the students being from Japan, the focus of the curriculum seemed to lean more toward supporting their needs rather than offering a balanced, holistic approach suitable for a broader student base.

Most concerning was the school’s lack of accountability. Instead of addressing areas where students were struggling, the responsibility was often shifted back to parents without adequate support or intervention from the school.

In summary, paying a premium does not guarantee a better quality preschool experience. In our case, Chatsworth fell short in several key areas, and I would advise other parents to evaluate more than just the price tag when choosing a preschool.

My son had been attending this school since he was a 4mo infant until 2.5yo, when we sadly had to withdraw due to house moving. He really enjoyed his time there and would constantly talk about his teachers with a lot of affection. There were many well-planned activities for the kids, and sharing sessions conducted occasionally by the teachers to update parents on the school curriculum as well. My son loved going to school and we would always drop him off with a peace of mind before heading to work. Thank you so much for all the care he has received :)

07 Feb. 26’
Having a group of 23 pax pre-CNY gathering with 捞鱼生.
I like their curry fish tail & fried chicken middle wing,虾酱鸡。
1st time here on Mother's day, Dinner for 11. Food is good, Price is reasonable - $219 for 10 dishes. Service is great. Server advise the right size for each dish. Does not oversell.

We had a great birthday celebration! The food was quite enjoyable, but there was one unexpected twist—the steamboat staff mentioned the fish would have both its head and tail, yet when it was served, the head was mysteriously missing. Where did it go?

The soup was delicious, though! There was yam in it, but it had completely melted, blending into the broth so well that we couldn’t taste it directly. Overall, a satisfying meal with a little culinary mystery.

Found this Zhi Char from google. So we decided to give it a try. And they definitely deserve a 5 star review. Off the beaten track nest in the industrial area. They are relatively new to Redhill Forum building. With the aid of google map is not difficult to locate them. Very friendly and meticulous service. The lady boss did some great recommendations and we enjoyed most of the dishes. Homemade Ngoh Xiang, Yam Ring, Prawn paste chicken, French Beans with Dried shrimp are nice. We specially like the 南乳炸肉 Fermented bean curd Fried pork is interesting and pack with flavor,  Assam Fish tail tangy and refreshing great dish to goes with rice , Salted egg Squid done in perfection soft n not chewy, evenly coated with salted egg batter, and not forgetting the homemade seaweed tofu from far look like sponge but every mouth feel bite binds nicely. 8 dishes Value for money great portion for 9 of us. Wallet friendly! We left the place with a happy tummy. highly recommend 👍🏼

Lovely food at affordable prices. Fish head curry and chilli crab was very good.!!! Very friendly service too.
Will definitely visit again soon.

The food was excellent overall. The ribeye was cooked to a perfect rare, exactly as requested, and the accompanying sauce complemented the steak very well. The pizza was also impressively crispy and enjoyable.

One thing to note: please keep the canned Coke chilled before serving. When a warm can is poured over ice, much of the carbonation escapes immediately, making the drink taste flat. The staff were very helpful and replaced my Coke three times. On the third attempt, the waiter brought me a properly chilled can, and that one had plenty of carbonation. Thank you for going the extra mile to make it right.

Great service, great steak, and great pizza. I would definitely come back.

Lazy Lizard @ Pasir Pangjang
Dont worry. There isn't any real 🦎.
Food is good and price generally dont burn a hold in your pocket. There are several public parking lots after the traffic light junction on the left.
Lazy Lizard (Pasir Panjang) is a cosy spot. Jap Rice Bowl is a crowd fave, but I'm hooked on their steak — generous portion, love the oysters too. Prices feel reasonable for what you get. 👍

I had a good memory to Stone Wood beer in Australia but this one is so diluted and was disappointing for me. Also had oxidized taste. Will not come back.
The food was also mediocre.
One good thing is not so many people so if you wanna work in quiet place, would be good. We went lunch time Saturday.

So I recently checked out The Lazy Lizard in Pasir Panjang and honestly? Straight bussin. I'm surprised more people aren't talking about this place. The prices won't break the bank and the service was really good throughout.
I went a bit overboard and ordered the pork rack, messy burger, and lazy burger. No regrets though.
Can we talk about the fries for a second? Both the straight-cut and curly fries were on point – crispy, well-seasoned, just perfect. And the mashed potatoes? Buttery goodness with just the right amount of truffle oil. Not too much that it overwhelms everything, you know?
But okay, the pork ribs. THE PORK RIBS. I need to emphasize this because they were absolutely insane. So tender they literally fell off the bone, super juicy – I'm pretty sure they sous vide these for like 32 hours or something because the texture was unreal. Honestly, they need to advertise these more because this dish alone is worth the trip.
The burgers were also really solid. I mean, they could've asked how I wanted my beef patty cooked, but even without that, both burgers exceeded what I expected. Good quality ingredients, and that melted cheese with bacon? Chef's kiss. Absolutely bomb.
If you're around Pasir Panjang, seriously give this place a shot. It's one of those low-key spots that really delivers. Just make sure you order the pork ribs fr fr no cap fax.

Clementi Woods Park looks like it would normally be a peaceful and green neighbourhood park. The surrounding area has plenty of trees, walking paths, and gentle slopes that would make it a nice place for a relaxing stroll

Unfortunately, the park is currently under renovation, and large sections are closed off with construction barriers. Because of this, visitors can only walk around the perimeter instead of entering the park itself, which limits what you can see and do

The environment around the park is still pleasant for a short walk, but it’s best to visit again after the renovation is completed to fully enjoy what the park has to offer. Hopefully it will reopen soon with improved facilities

Had a refreshing visit to Clementi Woods Park, a hidden gem filled with towering trees, shady trails, and a peaceful atmosphere. The cool breeze and chirping birds made the walk even more relaxing, offering a perfect escape from the city's fast pace. Whether it’s for a quiet stroll, some fresh air, or just to soak in the greenery, this park is a great spot to unwind. Definitely a place worth revisiting!
scenic trails perfect for walking or jogging. Sparrows flit through the trees—an increasingly rare sight in Singapore, making their presence feel like a quiet surprise.

Quiet, clean and wonderful place for strolling, jogging or running your dog. There's a dedicated place where 🐕 🐶 can runs freely. I have seen squirrels many times, even a rooster 🐓 If you want to be close to nature, this is the place. Has quite a bit of slope, good for strength build.

It's a lovely park to take a walk around. Lots of greenery, trees and grass, basically lots of shade around. Walk path is neatly paved and comfortable for walk and as the park is down a slope the paths have few steps to climb too. If your a parent/ nanny with a child in a stroller then it's not too easy to take the stroller up or down this park (unless, I haven't explored one yet). This park has a dog park, a small community garden too. There are exercise structures, small gazebo like structures. Lots of benches and open space too. It also has two playgrounds, one with regular metal play structure and another nature play slide.
Wonderful Omakase served complete with wine pairing. Open kitchen concept so you get to see the chef prepare the food
it is convenient for students who learn in Waseda Shibuya Senior High School
Very lush grounds. Helps with the nature feel because it is just beside a large park. Lots of ponds with fishes too
Excellent and out of the world dining experience that I’ll recommend anywhere ❤️
Chef Gab's beef steaks and selections bagus la. Topped with selection of wine...

My child has been in this preschool since he was 1.5 years old, and has always received care and attention from the teachers. The teachers are very patient in teaching him, and the school also provides a lot of extracurricular activities and various interesting courses. When my child comes home, he will show us the Chinese poems and nursery rhymes he has learned. Although the kindergarten is not big, it is a warm and loving place

Love the school and kudos to the principal as well as all teachers who put a lot in effort in teaching and cca activities. The school provides Robotic class and Wushu classes every week. The year end concert is very well done where the children are participating in the concert (dancing, singing) and the parents are even invited to the concert to watch. My kid is having a great time with the school and we are very blessed to stay near the school!!

Friendly teachers and staff that go the extra mile, good student-teacher ratio and a cosy environment.

Update: Change in management to Kensington has led to an injection of investments. Noticeable improvement in curriculum, programmes, infrastructure and activities involving parents. Glad to see progress.

My son started at this school since June 2024 and I’m really happy with his progress in development. He seems to be really enjoying himself and when I ask if he likes his teachers he always says yes! :)

I also like that the school organises many events for the children and also for parents to participate in together with them if they would like to.
