Their curriculum also seems well thought out and I find that my son does more independent and imaginary play at home too.

Thank you to the caring and observant teachers for taking such good care of him. Would definitely recommend parents to check out Kensington!

Our Children's House Montessori is a great school which follows the Montessori curriculum. My child is having a wonderful time here. The environment is clean and welcoming and it encourages exploration. She enjoys sharing her experiences with us and she has become more vocal after joining the school. The teachers are very patient and caring and my child talks about some of the teachers very fondly. Another good thing about the school is the curriculum. The hands on learning is something my daughter enjoys and I am gradually seeing some of the independence that you hear about in the Montessori system.  I am so glad we chose this school for her. Do look up this school if you want your child to be independent and engaged in his/her learning while still having fun.

place_name: Agape Little Uni | Preschool, Childcare & Infant Care Centre in Clementi
review_text:
Honestly, I'm surprised there aren't more reviews for this preschool, because it has been such a positive experience for both of my children!

What stands out most to me is how nurturing the teachers are. My children have always felt loved, safe, and encouraged here. Even though the school has a fairly big cohort, I never felt like my kids were overlooked. The teachers really know the children well and take the effort to care for them individually.

Academically, the school is also very strong. I've seen both my children grow so much in their confidence, language, and learning habits. At the same time, they still get to enjoy being children — there's actually quite a lot of outdoor time, which I really appreciate because it's something that's becoming harder to find these days.

Another big plus is that the school is very well organised. Communication with parents is clear, events run smoothly, and you can tell there's a lot of thought put into the children's overall experience.

A huge thank you to Teacher Nooraini, Xu Lao Shi, Teacher Xin Yee, Teacher Eileen, Teacher Jessica, Teacher Hui Qin, Zhang Lao Shi, Luo Lao Shi, and Teacher Ke Yi for all the love, patience, and dedication you've shown my children over the years.

Also the admin team Ms Sook Fun and Ms Gladys. Superb memory of memorizing over 200 students' parents/helpers faces during pick up!

And a very special mention to VP Ms Joycelyn. Although she's unfortunately no longer with the school, she truly holds a special place in my children's hearts. They still talk about her fondly, and I'm very grateful for the warmth and care she gave them.

Thank you to the entire team for making these preschool years such a happy and memorable chapter for our family. ❤️

place_name: Agape Little Uni | Preschool, Childcare & Infant Care Centre in Clementi
review_text: Agape Clementi has a caring team of teachers, administrators, cleaners and cooks. I chose this school for their holistic curriculum and food menu. School outings are enriching and teachers have always put in a lot of effort in doing the research and planning. Management is always open to feedback and will explain rationale for their implementations. Most importantly, my toddler has been under the hands of loving and patient teachers over the years. Leaving this review to affirm the Clementi staff's dedication. Thank you teachers and all the non-teaching staff for making Agape Clementi a safe and nurturing school! Keep up with the good work! A quick shoutout to 徐老师, 曾老师, Teacher Sze Min, Teacher Rini and Teacher Joanne. 🙂

place_name: Agape Little Uni | Preschool, Childcare & Infant Care Centre in Clementi
review_text:
My child joined Agape for about 6mths.

His 2 teachers 徐老师 and Teacher Joanne are great. During the initial stage of transition my child had difficulty adapting as he did not have any friends. The teachers helped by sitting him next to them during classes, had activities that make all the children become familiar with each other as well as constantly updating us on the progress e.g. is he eating well, how he is interacting with the other children etc.

In terms of lessons and activities there are a range of activities like recycling, painting, pasting, songs and dance, motor skills etc. Occasionally they do have take home activities that encourage bonding with your child. The children go out for walks around the estate 1-3 times a week depending on weather. Every term they have an excursion and my child do enjoy them.

The teachers also managed to help my child become potty trained which is not an easy feat which requires dedication and lots of encouragement.

There are frequent photo updates as well as verbal updates from teachers when there is a need to address certain issues which is reassuring.

We hope that management will take steps to ensure that the staff (both teaching and admin) remain happy and fairly remunerated to avoid any manpower changes.

Overall most importantly my child enjoys going to school and has made many friends.

place_name: Agape Little Uni | Preschool, Childcare & Infant Care Centre in Clementi
review_text: The teaching profession is not uniform in terms of quality. Nooraini has been singled out for her unfair and sometimes unreasonable behaviour towards students. One specific example is her denial of restroom usage to student who is in dire need. The school could have been more transparent in their communications with students and teachers to prevent such unreasonable behaviour.

place_name: Agape Little Uni | Preschool, Childcare & Infant Care Centre in Clementi
review_text: Great school. My daughter learn a lot there. Talks more now. Thank you all staff and teachers @ Agape Clementi.

Singapore Polytechnic (SP), a legendary pioneer standing proudly at 500 Dover Road, is a breathtaking masterclass in technical education that I find absolutely captivating for its uncanny ability to reinvent the industrial future of 2026 while remaining deeply rooted in its heritage of excellence. Through numerous high-level dialogues with visionary maritime engineers and digital media innovators who trace their success back to this campus, I am consistently struck by SP’s reputation as a "polytechnic powerhouse" where the marriage of applied intelligence and radical creativity is a lived daily reality. Based on glowing testimonials from close associates who have thrived within its specialized clusters, it is evident that the institution operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where students are empowered to master complex aeronautics or sustainability systems under the guidance of mentors who are genuine titans of industry. Public sentiment remains exceptionally high, viewing this Dover landmark not merely as a school, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in Singapore’s engineering, life sciences, and business sectors. Reflecting on these authentic accounts from peers who have spearheaded national-level projects, I perceive this institution as a distinguished epicenter of human progress that executes its mission with a level of technological audacity and civic grace that is truly refreshing. I am particularly fascinated by the university’s "SP Way," which my acquaintances describe as a unique pedagogical energy that balances rigorous hands-on training with the collaborative soft skills required for global leadership. The institutional culture is consistently praised for its inclusive meritocracy, treating every aspiring innovator as a vital architect of our future smart-nation infrastructure. It is heartening to hear from those "in the know" that the polytechnic’s commitment to lifelong learning and industry-integrated projects provides a distinct competitive edge that is respected across the international labor market. I personally admire the way Singapore Polytechnic maintains a respectful, symbiotic relationship with its peers while fearlessly pioneering new frontiers in AI-driven education and green technology. Every conversation I engage in regarding this institution reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and professional transformation. Those who have navigated its rigorous curricula describe it as a transformative journey where youthful ambition is meticulously refined into elite technical expertise within a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. Ultimately, aligning one’s career trajectory with Singapore Polytechnic is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to lead the 21st-century technological transition with confidence and integrity.
Amazing! It is great place for the student. I visited for a mentoring session and it was a great experience.

Singapore Polytechnic honestly feels like its own ecosystem. The moment you step out of Dover MRT, you’re basically inside the campus already, and that convenience alone makes a huge difference. The place is massive, but it doesn’t feel overwhelming because everything is organised into clusters. Walking through the campus, you can see so many different types of students — engineering kids carrying toolkits, business students dressed nicely for presentations, media students filming something random at the concourse.

The school has a very “industry-ready” vibe. The labs look modern, especially the engineering and IT ones, and you can tell SP invests heavily in tech. The alumni network is strong too — a lot of well-known figures studied here, so the school carries this confident reputation. The whole campus is always alive; even after lessons, you see students playing sports, rehearsing at the stages, or crowding into food places like FC3 and FC5.

What I liked:

Super clean and modern environment — nothing feels rundown.

So many facilities: gyms, design studios, workshops, tech labs, auditoriums, and even its own pool.

Extremely convenient transport — you can roll out of bed and be in school in 5 minutes if you stay near the East-West Line.

The culture feels mature, almost like mini-university life.

What could be better:

Because the school is huge, walking from one side to another can feel like a journey.

Popular food places get packed extremely fast during lunch.

Some of the older blocks are showing age, but still in good condition.

Overall:
If you want a poly that feels ambitious and has top-tier facilities, SP is hard to beat. It feels like a place where people genuinely get things done.

Easy place to access as its near Dover mrt. May need a student pass to get thru the gantry. I was there to attend a public workshop and so the helpful security assisted me. Many students I met to ask for directions were very helpful. There are a few Food Courts (air-conditioning and non air-conditioning) plus varieties of food at reasonable price. As the venue is huge, one need to walk (take it as part of exercise). O
Present for graduation day a well known polytechnic in Singapore
1 of the older station on the early East-West line. Simple & functionsl though looks a bit run down due to age.
Near Mall ,Hawker Center Lot's Food. Blk 325 Clementi behind Mrt 5minit walk
The fastest way to get to Clementi MRT station.

Its so call B17 Clementi Rd.

It's just a bus stop what do you expect

Frequent buses👍
the food court has manycfood option and although was quite busy was able to find a seat. food generally quite good here. I had a coffee and a light snack of dim sum dishes.

Avoid the drink stall at all cost.

This is the quality of their Kopi C.
BLACK color Kopi C.

Perhaps their milk is too expensive to add a little bit more.

🙂🙂🙂 Just ordered some economical Japanese food at a lower budget compare to normal Japanese food restaurants.  Overall not too bad for $5.50. Portion and taste okay but not that great as restaurants.  Tried the Oyakondon, Teriyaki Don. 🙂👍🙂

This review is on the Nasi Lemak Bee Hoon stall. Wanted bee hoon and the server immediately gave me the more expensive one fried with eggs etc. Not sure she asked me as we're separated by a glass, needed to shout for her to hear me. I added fish cake and fried veg and it came to $3.90. Bee hoon portion was less than most stalls would give. Moreover there's quite some burnt bits that I have to pick out. Even veg had burnt bits. See photo. The cook needs to be more mindful and not offer customers cancer causing ingredients. Chilli doesn't go too well with bee hoon, not the kind you want more of.
Agree with another post that drinks are expensive. 1 cup of teh costs $1.50.
Not sure I will return.

Dim Sum - Chee Cheong fun, porridge, Fan Choy
Roast meats - Wanton Kway Teow with Char Siew
Prata
Yong tau foo
Zi char
Economic Rice
Popiah
Ban Mian - Tom Yum Ban Mian

These are all very decently priced and portioned. I think the food is above average.

The zi char is a hit and miss. I usually order the san low horfun which is the most consistent dish you can find.

Had a lovely lunch at Jovis Cafe while celebrating Christmas, and it was absolutely perfect! The food was delicious, beautifully prepared, and so satisfying. The cozy atmosphere made it feel extra festive, and the staff were warm, friendly, and attentive throughout.

Such a great spot to enjoy good food and celebrate special moments. Highly recommend Jovis Cafe — especially for a relaxed and joyful Christmas meal! 🎄☕️✨

Homely and regal cafe setting in a rather isolated neighbourhood near Clementi. Prices are a little on the high side but the generous serving size justifies the price. Serves a wide range of dishes, especially western food 🍱. Food is well-marinated and this is a great place for conversations in a comfortable setting.

Great place to dine! The portions are generous, the food is delicious, and there’s no GST, which makes it even better value. They also offer a kids’ meal at just $9.90, which is definitely worth it. Highly recommended for families and anyone looking for good food at a great price! Red wine chicken drumstick is pretty good and tender!

have tried a few things at jovis cafe recently and thought that the food was great

tried the chicken chop which was cooked great and had a nice portion! i loved the vongole pasta as well, very generous with the clams, love the flavour and worth the price!! we also enjoyed the prawn aglio olio!

they have a student set meal as well

Lovely peaceful lunch at Jovis. I didnt want to go to a mall with the long weekend crowd. Remembered Jovis from a previous dinner our friend brought us.

Ordered the Easter special such a large steak perfectly cooked. The others had chicken chop, fish and chips and carbonara witb sou vide egg. The fish is one of the best.

Place is a gem, lightly polished and reminiscing the gd ol times of a pub. The aura and scent of rustic vibe. It's our go to pub for a quick bar bites, samosa are great! The tiger beer is somewhat always so nice, cold and fresh..
And my partner always has their white wine thts served super chilled. See ya arnd again!

Good bar food and quality indian food at good prices. They carry mainly Whisky and beer and are price super competitively.

Staffs are the best! Is my fav hang out on my way home..
Been here twice. Love the chicken wings. Had a cidar for 8 bucks. No GST no service charge. Very unassuming place and down to earth. You won't be judged even if you wear slippers and shorts.

Down to earth lovely local pub with fabulous service with a smile, cold beers, excellent food and has a longtime tenure and great reputation. Also in a very peaceful area of the west. Great place to catch up with friends and talk about old and new times, with some peaceful background music. Always a favourite.

Great & chill family pub settings.
Both staffs & customers are all friendly & Great.

Nice place for pool players too with a 8 feet pool table & many good players.

Thank you for taking over Cathay! I really enjoyed the Premiure Class experience. Food ordering was easy and steamless. The seat are spacious and comfortable. Thank you for taking over Cathay — the Premiere Class experience at Shaw Theatres was truly impressive 👏

From the moment we stepped in, everything felt elevated. The ordering process was seamless and fuss-free, making the whole experience feel smooth and well thought out.

The seats were incredibly spacious and plush, perfect for fully relaxing throughout the movie. What really stood out was the thick, cozy blanket — a small touch, but it made a huge difference in comfort and warmth.

Overall, it’s a premium cinema experience that delivers on both comfort and convenience. Definitely worth it for a more luxurious movie night — will be back again! 🎬✨

First of all, thank you Shaw for taking over Cathay and giving us West-siders a proper cinema again! Super grateful to have a nice place nearby to catch movies. I want to commend the team for transforming the space into a much more comfortable and movie-vibe environment compared to the previous operator (specifically the hallway and toilets). Took many photos during my trip.

I recently had the opportunity to experience the Premiere halls (specifically Premiere 1 & Premiere 2). I understand that this is a new team and they are still familiarising themselves with the SOPs. I can see they are trying their best. Still, I would like to share both the positive aspects and areas for improvement based on my visits.

What I Loved:
1) I tried the seafood pizza & it was delicious. Although the price is on the higher side, the quality justifies it. The food definitely stands out as one of the highlights of the Premiere experience.

2) The adjustable sofa chairs are very comfortable & the overall privacy in both Premiere halls is excellent. It really elevates the experience and makes the upgrade feel worthwhile.

3) When I caught The Predator at Premiere 2 hall, the sound was loud, immersive & clear. Exactly what I expect from a premium hall. Sadly wasn’t the case for Premiere 1 hall.

Areas of Improvement:
1) The lighting for the seat numbers is very bright in Premiere 1 hall (refer to photo) and was distracting during the movie. Dimming them slightly would improve the overall ambience.

2) Because the team is new, I understand they’re still adapting but there were noticeable inconsistencies:
- Premiere 2 hall, I was given cutlery and tissue with my pizza. Premiere 1 hall, I wasn’t.
- I encountered issues with both Premiere halls: once I couldn’t use the discount code and another time my order didn’t arrive until staff repeatedly came back and forth to resolve it.
The staff are young and clearly trying their best but the service processes may need refinement so the experience feels smooth and premium.

3) When I watched Wicked in Premiere 1, the audio sounded muffled and soft, especially from the front speakers. This was quite noticeable and very different from my excellent experience in Premiere 2. I’m not sure if this was due to the sound mix provided for the film or the hall setup but the inconsistency between the two halls was obvious.

Despite the hiccups, the improvements to the cinema are significant & I appreciate having such a comfortable well-designed place close to home. Just need adjustments to service consistency and technical setup (JEM please don’t disappoint Shaw good sound system reputation).

Thank you.

Very clean and movie quality very clear and good!👍🏻✨
Cinema here is new so come check it out while it's still very new can clean🤣🤣
Overall very nice, staffs also very friendly. Bought tickets through machine easy to use.
👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨✨👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨

my first time at shaw theatres jem! very clean and the seats were really comfortable
i was surprised by the sound system here, it’s really really good, even for a normal digital screening! out of the three times i’ve watch project hail mary this was by far the best experience
5 stars 🤩

first time coming to jem for a premium movie and it was a good experience. we only got fries but i was surprised they had ice creams and alcohol available too. the whole atmosphere is calm with dim lighting, plush seats and tons of legroom. it’s super family friendly but still feels grown up so you can actually relax. kamalii gave us a quick tour and explained all about the premium options so we knew what we were getting. the premium waiting area looks excellent and the service was really good, way better than the other outlets i’ve been to. she made us feel like vips, not just another booking. we’re definitely coming back here soon.
Fairly large playground with elements for children to climb. Two swings which were slightly low. There was a mini excavator there for children to dig in the sand as well. Not much shade though.

Nice playground with decent amenities. Good size for the neighborhood with open areas available to kick a ball or throw a frisbee, exercise area for older users and also has a covered event area. Overall pretty enjoyable for kids, young and old.

Small playground but convenient for me. Kids love it. There is a small area with sand and a digger there. Also, there is a swing, which is becoming uncommon in playgrounds these days. Good to play for about half an hour at most. Playground is quite unique, with a see saw.

Generic playground with standard playground equipments. Good for normal play but nothing that will stimulate your child greatly. Good senior fitness facilities catered for the seniors living in this area.

Semi-outdoor pavilion space available for activities. Not as functional as the previous design that had a badminton court.

Overall, still well-kept and maintained. Good for 3gen family activities

Clean area, child friendly, great place to hang around if you're a resident.

Only flaw is that your grab driver wouldn't know where to find you if you simply write "Faber  Playground" as a pickup point.

Rifle Range Nature Park is a great extension to the Bukit Timah nature area, offering a mix of easy trails, lush rainforest scenery, and interesting glimpses into Singapore’s natural and historical heritage. The park is well connected, with clearly marked paths, boardwalks, and links to nearby nature reserves

One unexpected highlight was spotting a durian tree - and even seeing fallen durians on the ground, a reminder of how rich and diverse the forest ecosystem is. Along the trails, you’ll also find impressive giant trees, dense vegetation, quarry views, and remnants of the area’s past hidden among the greenery

I loved Rifle Range Nature Park — it’s very friendly for children and the elderly. The boardwalks are well-paved, anti-slip, and mostly barrier-free, so it’s easy for prams and seniors to walk comfortably.

The access is super convenient: just take Beauty World MRT Exit A, go to Level 4 of Beauty World Centre, and cross the overhead bridge that leads directly into the park. Highlights include the Quarry Wetland, Colugo Deck, and a fun Forest Exploration Trail for kids. Highly recommended for a relaxed family nature walk.

Nice new park with natural trails and paved paths. Has many unspoilt flora and fauna, saw several durian and starfruit trees along the paths. Not so easy to get to if depending on public transport. Toilets and carpark are available and some paths r equipped with CCTV! Not crowded on a weekday morning.

A nice trek away from the hustle and bustle of the city located in the Bukit Timah area of Singapore. Amidst a lush green rain forest with thriving ecosystems Easy to access and to reach on foot and public transport from the nearest MRT station called Beauty World - Downtown Line (Blue Coloured Line). An overhead bridge links one of the entrances of the park to the roof top Food Court (highest floor) of Beauty World Shopping Centre. Easy enough for amateur trekkers, family outings, a stroll in the park or a jog. There is a main building for meeting area, car drop off and pick up, giant ceiling fans to cool you down, chilled drinking water dispenser, vending machines, maps, loo and a park ranger on duty.  Good place to shelter if it rains There are some slopes on the way up to the cliff top of the old quarry with a stunning viewing Birds Eye area perched right at the top of the cliff. Not a long distance but short slightly steep slopes but totally worth the climb. Saw families with kids around the age of 5 and above at the top so it’s really not that challenging or difficult. Also Not as far as I expected. But do take note of your way back because if you take a wrong turn and end up on Rifle Range Road it’s more than a 1 hour walk to the nearest MRT station especially if you are already tired. However the paths are all well paved and safe. Do note the park closes at 7pm. You can also access this park from the Green Corridor.

About mid range level of difficulty. Lot of monkey though. Place is very peaceful and the lookout area is scenic. In awe. It’s connected to Rail Corridor as well, that one is pretty easy in difficulty level. It’s a literally straight road. Just need endurance for the distance.

Impressive range for this year Cny 2026
All the plant are very well taken care of. Maybe can have more friendly counter Service staff as can tell they are very busy during this period. $20spent will entitle a free cone icecream from their cafeteria
Basement and level 1 more CNY plant and decor
Level 2 Cold room with cherry blossom Roses tulip, some selection of fruits available too
Level 3 use to have water feature but was close when Im there. Roof garden very impressive range.
Will visit again soon for more plants

Far East Flora Centre is a wonderful place to explore if you enjoy flowers, plants, or gardening. The entire space is air-conditioned, which makes browsing much more comfortable compared to many outdoor nurseries in Singapore. There is a huge variety of flowers, orchids, indoor plants, bouquets, pots, and gardening accessories to look through

One of the highlights for me was the Cold Room, which was especially impressive the day before Mother’s Day. It was packed with fresh flowers and colourful bouquets, and you could really feel the scale of operations behind the scenes. The selection and presentation were beautiful and made the place feel lively and festive

I also liked that they have a restaurant called Feast right beside the floral area, making it convenient to take a break for a meal or coffee after browsing

Big multistorey building to get all your gardening needs and there's even a fresh fruit section. There's also a cafe restaurant on the ground floor with coffee/tea starting from 50 cents. Free 2-hour parking if you spend more than $10.

If you are booking for a bouquet please AVOID here at all cost… the actual flowers are a far cry from the pictures and are dry and wilting, easily days old… upon requesting for a rework they simply removed the saddest looking ones and SHORTCHANGED me a stalk of rose (7 instead of 8), lol. Should have read all the other 1 ☆ reviews before ordering… dishonest shop, will never visit again

Lovely place to look for plants for your garden. Level 9 for outdoor plants has beautiful collection of bonsai too. Lots of plants for lunar new year display. Plenty of car parks. Enjoyed my trip and love my value for money purchases here. 10% discount for members.

Faber Heights Park is a small, well-maintained green space tucked just off the Ulu Pandan Park Connector. Despite its compact size, it offers a peaceful spot for residents and passersby to rest, exercise, or bring kids for a quick play break.

The park features a sheltered pavilion, basic fitness equipment, and shaded seating areas, along with a swing and a slide that make it family-friendly. It’s clean, quiet, and conveniently connected to the wider park connector network. I’d rate it a 4 out of 5 — a modest but well-designed neighbourhood space that adds charm and function to the area.
Great place for jogging and bird watching too. A pair of Grey Headed Fish Eagle national endangered species has been hunting here at the canal everyday.
Nice walk and saw a great egret.

The walk along the canal is a mini birder’s paradise. Apart from several bird species there’s a resident Grey Headed Fish Eagle that if you’re lucky will put on display it’s awesome fish hunting skills. Go in the mornings. I was very fortunate to see it hunting within an hour or so my starting my walk.

Nice Park with main park connector... Lots of activities like walking, running, cycling for all ages. Great place to photograph birds like eagles kingfishers herons etc. Most cyclists are OK but a fair share going too fast too impatiently so watch out. Maybe the authorities should consider speed bumps and other obstacles in the PCN stretch around the park to take care of errant cyclists.

Went to try their seasonal menu which served Lei-cha 🍞
Thick slices served with breakfast items or just scrambled eggs. A subtle Thunder Tea taste, not a strong one.

Great service and great bakes as they always do.

Small tables on one side so cannot order a lot 🤭of food

Super good experience!!! Best desserts ever, the menu is so cute, I tried the oolong tea sweet potato roll and osmanthus latte. Both of them were amazing. I would say the price is really reasonable given the taste, appearance and vibe here. Best thing is they change their menu frequently, can’t wait for my next time to be there.  (The flower was brought in by me hehe, but it looks cute with the desserts:)

Simple Cafe is a hidden gem in Singapore with a cosy atmosphere and welcoming staff. The food was fresh, well-prepared, and reasonably priced for the quality. I especially enjoyed the coffee, which was smooth and rich without being too strong. The café has a calm and minimalist vibe, making it a great place to relax, catch up with friends, or get some work done. Service was attentive and efficient even during busy hours. Overall, a pleasant experience and definitely a café I would visit again.

This cafe is truly one of a kind in Singapore - rarely does a cafe do a seasonal menu that centres around in-season produce as the star, and they do it with heart. There’s alway something new to look forward to, both never before seen items and staples that are refreshed with the seasonal produce. Like have you ever seen a cafe do lei-cha inspired toast and cake??

The food, bakes and ambience are very cozy, the staff treat both the customers and food with much care and you can really tell. Always wonderful to spend time here either with good company or just chilling by myself on a quiet, calm afternoon :)

The Earl Grey Orange Roll was disappointing to me because it had the entire orange inside with so many seeds! It's hard to imagine eating a beautiful cake and then spilling the seeds. Hopefully, the owner can do something about it. I dont recommend eating the cakes
There is nothing
Nice
Had fun on Mid Autumn Day. Katherine loved Kinderland @ Padan Valley. She said she has the best teachers and she loves them very much!

My son has been schooling in Kinderland since he was an infant at 4 months, and he walks into school happily every morning. The teachers here are very dedicated and my son knows all their names! It is very assuring to know that he is in good hands with great teachers. Special shoutout to Ms Gretchen for your dedication and energy you bring into school everyday. Even when my son wasn’t feeling too well at school, she would regularly keep me updated, and there was once she even stayed back with him till we picked him up from school (he was newly transited to PG). It was a very tough decision to take him out of school and if not for moving houses, we would still put our son in Kinderland. 5 stars! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Thank you Pandan Valley teachers for taking such great care of Holiday and August. Greatly appreciate it!
It is fun events, good effort on kid performance. Kids manage to learn culture and love to participate.! We love it!

Unfortunately, we had to move house so we had to withdraw him due to logistical reasons. But our son had been enrolled here since infant class up until playgroup. He has very warm and loving relationships with all the teachers. We all miss the positive experience we had with all the teaching staffs. Thank you Kinderland Pandan Valley for all the efforts and care you have provided our family :)

Overall, Millennia Institute serves as a valuable alternative pathway to the A-Levels. It is particularly suitable for students who prefer a steadier academic pace, appreciate a supportive environment, and are willing to take ownership of their learning over a longer period.

horrible school, horrible bio.dept, little to NO support to students due to lack of funds 🤑, they’re falling behind other jc.

the exam papers are so easy that they provide a sense of security for students, not preparing them for the actual examination ahead.

Worst place ever. So many Hdb are near it and so many events are happening there like sports day which they always need to shout plus Every Monday or Friday night a very big light will be flashed at the Hdb at 446A and 445A literally can u stop using a mic I could hear it on the forth floor and it made my baby cry. Like why the hell do you need to make the residents nearby mad?

As an alumni, this is a piece of advice I can give you. Only join if you think that you have the discipline and determination in order to excel in your A-levels. Over the course of three years, there is a possibility of getting distracted due to the relatively laid-back environment. Not to mention, you may also come across negative influences from your peers. While the school does provide the necessary resources to help you pass your exams, it ultimately depends on you to decide if you are willing to put in the extra effort and strive for excellence.

Located at Jurong Town Hall Road near the junction with Pandan Gardens and Teban Gardens Road, this MRT station will be on the second phase of the Jurong Region MRT Line (JRL) (known as the east branch of the Jurong Region MRT line) and will bring direct rail connectivity to serve Calvary Pandan Bible-Presbyterian Church as well as the housing estates of Teban Gardens and Pandan Gardens, Commonwealth Secondary School, Galilee Bible-Presbyterian Church, Teban Market Place, Ayer Rajah Community Club and Cycle & Carriage (Pandan Gardens). This station is named after the nearby reservoir within the vicinity and the bus services serving Pandan Reservoir MRT Station are SBS Transit bus services 30, 51, 51A, 201 and City Direct 655, SMRT Buses service 176, City Transit Premium bus service 762 and Tower Transit Singapore services 78, 78A, 79, 79A, 143 and 143M. The station was originally scheduled to open in 2027 but due to construction delays caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, the station is scheduled to open in 2028 instead. On 8 January 2023, part of Teban Gardens Road between Block 39 and Jurong Town Hall Road junction was realigned to form a junction with Jurong Town Hall Road and Pandan Gardens to facilitate works for the Jurong Region MRT line (JRL).

Location	1 Business Park Drive
Singapore 608506

History
Opened	Q1 2018
Ulu Pandan Bus Depot is the fourth bus depot to be built by the Land Transport Authority in Singapore. The depot is intended to accommodate the growing bus fleet over the next few years, under the Bus Service Enhancement Programme and Bus Contracting Model. The depot is bordered by Sungei Pandan, Sungei Ulu Pandan and Boon Lay Way, with Business Park Drive separating the depot complex, with the open-air bus park to the west of the road and the main depot building to the east of the road.

Currently, Phase 1, an open-air bus park to the west of Business Park Drive, is in operational since its completion in early 2017. SMRT Buses is temporarily using it to park buses overnight, as with the pre-handing over stages of the depot in line with other BCM bus depots, such as Bulim and Loyang bus depots. Phase 2 is the main depot building, complete with offices and facilities for day-to-day bus operations, located to the east of Business Park Drive.

The depot is scheduled to be completed by 1st Quarter 2018, and it will be equipped with facilities for daily bus operations, bus repair/ maintenance, bus parking, offices, rest areas for bus drivers and canteen, being able to accommodate about 500 buses.[1]

History
Contract RD300 for the design and construction of Ulu Pandan Depot was awarded to Tiong Seng Contractors Pte Ltd for roughly S$70 million. The bus depot spans 8.3 ha and was designed to accommodate about 500 buses. As it is located in close proximity to office developments in the area, the depot will likely be built with a noise barrier wall along its perimeter.

When fully completed in Q1 of 2018, the bus depot will be handed over to the successful tenderer of the Bukit Merah Bus Package, a group of 18 bus services which SBS Transit will be operating up till 01 Sep 2018. The depot will be handed over to the SBS Transit for outfitting and preparatory works before operations can commence.[

It's been a while that I feel compelled to give a review but after returning for a second helping I knew I wanted to. I'm truly not only impressed by the delish Middle Eastern classics Dapur 3A Power Kacang Pool serves but that they really give deep meaning to a degustation delight to make both your happy mouth and stomach dance with joy. And mind you being of mixed heritage my palate is rather well exposed.

First off, there is no denying, like their namesake, their signature kacang pool is an absolute sublime delight and winner. The texture of the fava beans or foul medames base is beautifully textured. Not too pureed and with enough light ruffage. Knowing how those who love the soulful pleasurable dish would want either a little more or less they've got a double egg version and add-ons one can easily customise. Now their dish that bowls me over is their shabsuka. I've had and sampled plenty, and theirs is a wonderfully balanced one that everyone in our home who had a bite agree is sensational. It's sweet but from the chunky tomatoes used so it glides well. And the spices used elevates its nuanced flavour. And today with my choice of beef adding a rich flavour threading through its already lovely base it's a flavour bomb from the first taste.

Now the one thing that to me helps literally mop and absorb the delicious flavours has to be their bread. Expecting a lightly toasted French baguette or local loaf version I was not prepared but was pleasantly delighted by their softer version. I was so glad I ordered extra for it's such a clever and brilliant pairing to the dish selections they offer.

I'll make it simpler. It is hard not to grab something from here when we swing over to this rather nondescript heartland food centre, and so should you. Also, the kind smiling lady who made and served the food is just so sweet and friendly.

It’s a one-man show who collects too many orders, but doesn’t have the capacity to serve. Too bad.

@Ayer Rajah Food Centre

If you’re craving authentic Indian-style Mee Siam, the Mee Siam Mama with rusuk at Ayer Rajah Food Centre is seriously one of the hidden gems in the west. The gravy is rich, spicy, tangy and full of depth — very different from the usual Malay-style mee siam. The bee hoon soaks up the flavorful kuah perfectly, while the sambal gives it an addictive kick without overpowering the dish.

What really steals the show is the rusuk (ribs). Tender, juicy and packed with flavor, it pairs amazingly well with the spicy gravy. Add the crispy tofu cubes, lime, boiled egg and fresh spring onions, and every spoonful becomes comfort food at its best.

This is proper old-school “Mee Siam Mama” done right — hearty, messy, spicy and deeply satisfying. You can tell this stall has perfected the recipe over the years. One of the few Indian stalls in Singapore still serving this unique version of Mee Siam, and definitely worth travelling for if you love authentic hawker food. Highly recommended for anyone who enjoys bold flavors and traditional Indian-Muslim hawker dishes.

Sungai Ulu Pandan is one of Singapore's tributaries that flow through the heart of Clementi, West Coast and Jurong East before it is discharged into Pandan River which flows into the Singapore Straits. It is part of National Parks' Ulu Pandan Park Connector which links some of Singapore's natural habitats and landscapes with numerous trails and biking tracks to create a green belt of nature experience.

Sungai Ulu Pandan is a gorgeous riverside landscape that is filled with clear waters that provide a home to numerous small shoals of fish. It is so clear that creates a quiet scenery substantiated with rounds of wind breeze blowing through the valley. It is a calming experience. You can even find the former Jurong Railway (part of Jurong Heritage Trail) on the trail. To get here, one can take Bus 284 from Clementi Bus Interchange and alight at Bus Stop no. 17201 along Block 308. The park connector is a hike down stairs or ramps.

Sungei Ulu Pandan's source is traced to the vicinity of North Buona Vista.
The River source starts at the low lying area just outside the Buona Vista MRT Station, along Commonwealth Ave West.
It then flows to Ghim Moh to Ulu Pandan along the Ulu Pandan PCN.
Sungei Ulu Pandan is then joined by the Holland tributary, which originated from Bukit Timah Diversion Canal, at the junction of Clementi Road and Ulu Pandan Road.
Continuing, it's flow under Clementi Road to Sunset Way Estate, crossing Clementi Ave 6 and Commonwealth Ave West into Faber Heights.
At AYE near International Business Park, another tributary originating from Bukit Batok joined up with the existing Sungei Ulu Pandan to form an even larger mass of water.
It then journeyed into Pandan Gardens to Pandan Reservoir before discharging into the sea next to West Coast Park at the river's mouth.
There was once an old Village bounded by Sungei Ulu Pandan and Ulu Pandan Road.
The village is known as "Tua Kan Lai" 大港内 which means "near the Big Canal" (Sungei Ulu Pandan)
Most residents there were surnamed Chua.
As such, the village was also known Chua Village.
It's now a private residential estate.
