Beyond just a pre-school, maplebear sunset’s teachers are the ones that make the place special. We are very fortunate to have Ms Apple and her team of excellent teachers help our boys navigate this important phase of their lives. They are more than just educators and they go above and beyond expressing genuine care for my boys.
Caring & Attentive Teacher!
A really quiet library, you can choose different seats, and there's even a pod for you to record/conduct a meeting!

Why aren't we allowed to bring drinks inside? We're all adults here and if SMU can do it, why can't SIM? If you ask me to drink outside I'll just transfer my drink to my bottle and doesn't that defeat the purpose of the rule? Just let us drink in peace.

Also why does it close at 9 pm? If it's a school library shouldn't it close later?
Overall ok, but the ac so cold it felt like i was in the arctic
Good facilities and the newly refurbished library gives a fresh look for SIM students to study!

My wife and I were here today (22 January 2026) for brunch. While it was my wife's first time visiting this cafe, I was last here ten years ago and this can only mean that this cafe is still going strong after all these years and that is no easy feat in Singapore for a cafe or eatery. My wife and I ordered the following for our brunch: a Laksa, a Mee Soto, a Pandan Cake Slice, a Kaya Butter Toast, a Teh Large and a Kopi Regular - and also a Sugee Cake Slice to take away. The Laksa here was good and clean-tasting (I only wished that it came with blood cockles), the Mee Soto was good and came with a bergedil, the Pandan Chiffon Slice was EXCELLENT (fluffy, light and yet flavourful), the Kaya Butter Toast was very good (in particular, the butter and the kaya spread), and both the Teh and Kopi were robust and flavourful. Overall, the food and beverages here were very good. Service here, in particular by the lady chef was very friendly and polite. The dining ambience here was cosy and comfortable. I highly recommend this cafe tucked away in the quieter part of Sunset Way.
Beef Rendang, a flavourful Indonesian/Malaysian dish, is served with a coconut-based soup and pressed rice cakes. Atmosphere is good but busy cafe on Saturday brunch time.

My go-to breakfast spot whenever I cycle along Ulu Pandan PCN. I love their local delights menu, especially the sugee cake! Tried the mee soto (special of the day) even though it’s not on the menu. Sedap!
Didn't manage to capture nice food photos but the cafe was small & cozy. Owners were smiley and friendly that makes great dining experience! Per person spending around $20 +/- for meal & drink.
Mee rebus and laksa are average. Prices are reasonable. I like the iced tea served in a glass and not plastic cups. Cafe is comfortable and nostalgic. Service is friendly.
The morning sun really burns.. needs more shelter

🚆🚉The Railway Tracks, Girder Bridge at Sunset Way, and Truss Bridge over Sungei Ulu Pandan were all remnants of the Old Jurong Railway Line that once operated from Bukit Timah Railway Station to Jurong Industrial Estate.
🛤️An interesting piece of Singapore's history.

A beautiful piece of memorabilia to the history of railway in Singapore. It’s also a good reminder of how nature heals and what would happen if manmade stuff was left to nature. It connects to the Jurong railway bridge but there is no access to the main bridge (maybe under maintenance).

Used to walk on this on the way back home from (now shuttered) Clementi North Primary School and cross the bridge over the water. Thanks for whoever added this into Google Maps - childhood memories activated!
Some remnants of the track but otherwise nothing to see. Also not great for walks.
It's an abandoned railway , very old , very cool. Unfortunately the railway bridge is locked

I was here today (21 DECEMBER 2025) to have brunch at a delicatessen at Clementi Arcade. From the reviews that I have read about this place, I had expected the worse with regard to the parking situation and toilet facilities thereat, but neither turned out to be the case for me. Maybe it was a Sunday, but I had no problems finding a car parking space and when I left shortly after noon time, my car was the only one in the car park. As for the toilet facilities here, I had a pleasant experience - the men's toilet was clean and there was no malodour. By the way, I love the cafes and the Cold Storage supermarket here.
Tiny mall hidden in Sunset Way. Cold Storage and Public Toilets are very convenient. No washing of muddy shoes in the toilet!

laid back mall. I normally come here for supermarketing Cold Storage, coffee at Baker n Cook, Ice Cream at Daily scoop. these are yhev3 things that I mostly come here for and there always up to my satisfaction. great place to chill, not crowded except on weekends. the environment is nice in an exclusive sunset way neighbourhood.

20/11/2024
Visited this mall to get some snacks. Cosy small mall with F&B outlets, supermarket, clinic & school. Limited chargeable gantry open-air parking available.
This mall has nothing much going on. There are, however, a supermarket (Cold Storage), three cafes, an ice cream parlour and a cake shop.

Visited this café for breakfast today with a friend. We arrived around 9am and found the place cozy and welcoming, located conveniently on the ground floor of an HDB block. I’ve heard their waffles are one of their specialties.

We ordered an all-day breakfast to share along with two drinks. Even during the early breakfast hours, there were already a few tables occupied, which gave the café a warm and lively atmosphere.

The staff (possibly the owner) was very polite and attentive. The food quality was comparable to what you would expect from a typical Western breakfast café.

The overall vibe feels like a small, family-run café. Do note that food may take a little longer to be served, so it’s best to come with a relaxed mindset, a bit of patience, and simply enjoy the slower pace of the morning.

Cute cafe. Cozy interiors. Good service.
We ordered the shakshuka (which they kindly modified to be vegetarian friendly), avocado toast, the big breakfast, a waffle and three coffees. Total bill 86$.
Food was good. Particularly liked the waffle. Coffee was good although not amazing. Shakshuka was not satisfactory- the egg was barely cooked, the cheese used was shredded cheese instead of feta and I couldn't find any bell peppers.

Second time here, had a nice cozy afternoon coffee at Coffee & Friends. Cafe has an authentic, comforting vibe. Had dinner here the first time, the beef bolognese and chicken lasagna were delicious (forgot to take pics of them) this time round ordered the flat “magic” coffee with soy milk, which I was surprised they had, as some places only offered oat milk as alternative. The waffles felt light and tasty. Recommended as a good spot with good food, to hang out and relax.

Quality and taste of food was exceptional and caught me by surprise for a small cafe in Sunset Way. Savoury waffle with eggs and sausage was an interesting mix and overall blend of the flavours came out really nice. Waffles was light and fluffy and sausage was of really good quality. Spaghetti bolognese was a hit with the kids and husband, they were asking for more! Plenty of minced beef to go around. The Tarter Tots are as addictive as Golden village popcorns, my son wiped out more than 1 basket of Tarter Tots, had to stop him going for more. Matcha latte had beautiful latte art and tasted really nice. First time here but definitely not our last. Highly recommend to anyone in search of good cafe food!

Excellent service, there were only two people working and a healthy stream of customers but they got everyone’s food and drinks out to them on time from what we could see. The coffee was fantastic, light and flavoured. The waffle portions were huge for me, very generous. The savoury waffles were unique and tasty. Chewy and accompanied well with the sour cream and salsa. The mushrooms with black pepper were sooo good. Sausage was nice and tater tots were fried very well. Eggs were nicely cooked, not over done or dry. Overall very nice place with good food and service.

Our furkid's regular groomer moved to sunset way, so we had a good 2 hours to our meal. The restaurant was pleasant and not too crowded on a Saturday and the manager came by to chat with us periodically. We ordered the  salted egg crab, "green dragon veggie" (青龙菜) and crabmeat fried rice. The first two were fantastic, but the crabmeat fried rice was a tad disappointing with barely any crabmeat and a lot of salted egg and century egg. Service was great as well and you get 10% off as a member! Will definitely return again the next time we bring our dog for grooming.

Truly enjoyed the food here at a reasonable price. Every dish was flavorful and nicely prepared. The portions were generous and definitely worth the price. The staff here friendly,attentive and make us feel very welcomed throughout the meal. A great dining experience would definately come back again👍

Food is alright total spend $280+++

Service seem abit shortage of manpower

Order raise hand no one attend not once but few time ended up need to go collect myself

Order food cannot speak Chinese only Malays …. English also abit don’t understand…. Very surprise..

Food was ok nothing much to complaint .. price was acceptable but with the amount spend I think can get better service and better food .

The chicken is abit dry and hard the rest are ok

First time there … not sure will go again .

Another impressive meal. Sri Lankan chilli crab cooked to perfection, mantou, a must-try sautéed chicken with crispy yam basket, silver seafood hor fun boasting both crispy and wok hei fried noodle also another favourite. Our usual must-have dessert, coconut pudding, and today we have a delicious mochi beautifully presented, a true culinary delight.

Compliments to the Chef for their attention to detail.

Highly recommend Chin Huat Seafood! We had an amazing spread. The Alaskan crab dishes served two ways were the stars of the night, steamed with chinese wine egg whites and with wok hei beehoon.
Alongside delicious homemade beancurd tofu, 3 eggs spinach, and a refreshing coconut pudding dessert to finish. Everything was well prepared and flavourful.

What stood out just as much as the food was the thoughtful service. They gave us time to take photos of the crab dishes before neatly cutting it up, and nothing ever felt rushed.

The whole team was warm and welcoming, with a special shoutout to Joyce and Joyce (yes, two of them!) for their friendliness.

If you love fresh seafood and genuinely attentive service, Chin Huat Seafood is definitely a place to come.

I was here today (21 DECEMBER 2025) to do some grocery shopping at this supermarket. I love the shopping experience here at this Cold Storage outlet at Clementi Arcade. The shopping aisles here are very wide; the range of products here is also very wide; and it is very well-stocked. Service here was very good - friendly and helpful. The shopping ambience here was also very good - in cool air-condition comfort.

located in a laid back mall., bringing essential convenience 5o the residents living here. the store is not too large butvwell stock withdaily necessities. easy to browse through to look for what you want. I went there but  what ry herb and was happy to find it. end up buy a boytle of exra virgin olive oil as well. it wasn't too crowded and you can take your time looking for what you want. enjoyed my trip there.

Smaller and has less range than other cold storage outlets. Several notable food options were not available like a hot food section and RTE (ready to eat meals) like sandwiches.  Given its location,  this was understandable as it serves a very small niche market.  Service staff were however excellent and friendly.

Visited Cold Storage at Clementi Arcade today and I was impressed by how clean and well-kept the supermarket is. The shelves were neatly arranged and fully stocked, and I even found the items that were not available at a much larger supermarket in Clementi. Kudos to the management and staff for maintaining such high standards here!
A cosy little supermarket serving the Sunset Way community. It's layout is neat and easy to navigate. The staffs are friendly and seems to be enjoying their work.

I honestly cannot believe how bad this supermarket is. The entire place feels like it has not been updated in decades — the shelves, the flooring, even the fridges look worn out and neglected. It genuinely does not feel like you are in Singapore.

Walking through the aisles is quite an experience… and not in a good way. The overall cleanliness and upkeep are seriously lacking. It would make such a huge difference if this place was upgraded or taken over by a better operator — at least then you would expect a cleaner environment and a wider variety of products.

To make things worse, the staff were not friendly at all, which just added to the whole unpleasant experience.

If you are nearby, go ahead and see it for yourself. Honestly quite mind-blowing… but not in the way you would hope.

*SHENG SIONG PLEASE COME HERE!
Not as magical as the name suggests. Slightly sub-par supermarket that doesn't feel that clean. Many shelves were also unstocked. Not much selection of items more akin to a mini mart.

Had a really bad experience last night. The cashier was rude and kept a cold, unhappy face throughout, making customers feel like they were a burden. This is not acceptable for a service role. The owner seriously needs to reconsider the front-line staff.

Really bad experience. They tagged their yeo's drinks at $6.85 but charged $11.65 when checked out. Didn't check but when realised the next day, went back n they said promotion ended, but all stocks there are still tagged at $6.85, n they say they don't have time to change them, so it is customers' fault. I used to buy from them all the while but now will not again. They are not honest.
Small supermarket in Sunset Way. Has your basic essentials but nothing more. Overall doesn’t feel very clean and hygienic compared to the other major supermarket chains.

Auntie Yvonne is the best I have ever met in all the 7 eleven store in Singapore.
She always recommend me what is good to buy .Give her thumbs up for her good job !
auntie with white hair and glasses v rude to customers . Pls do something and make them change their attitude, we do not want them ruining others people say . 7 eleven , pls do something

Night shift staff has no respect he didnt know anything about store and promo
He dosent even know how to communicate with customers.

Old auntie with white grey hair, glasses, extremely poor customer service, got attitude ruined my day multiple times just from interacting with this woman. One time my coin dropped in between the gap of the counter and (I have seen other 7 eleven counters be able to pull the drawer and retrieve the coin) but this auntie didn’t care when I asked her politely if she could help me shift the counter abit. I got angry and she shouted cursed words in hokkien at me. Do better
White hair auntie very rude to customers , shouted at me 😩 forcing me to buy $1 candy to be able to serve other 😩 this is the third time😩

I was here today (21 DECEMBER 2025) with my wife and our young friend for coffee and dessert. We ordered the following here today: two Latte Hot, a Cappuccino Hot, a Noisette, an Eclair and a Strawberry Lamington. The coffees here were very good - flavourful and not acidic. The desserts here were also very good except for the Strawberry Lamington that turned out to be dry and not creamy therein whatsoever. Service here could do with a smile or be more friendly. The dining ambience here was comfortable.

Hidden along Sunset Way, this outlet of Baker & Cook has two of the most friendliest and cheerful staff I've encountered- Pavi & another male gentleman who occasionally serves at this outlet (sorry I didn't manage to get his name). Bread and pastries from here are my fave weekly treat!! Kudos to the two staff for their excellent service and who make visits here such a pleasant experience!

First time dining here, thanks to Google review since we were around the area.
Food and service is pretty good. Ordered the simply scrambled eggs and tuna tartines. Tasty. The bread is nice, fresh and light.
The downside of this place is the ambience. Aircond is weak (quite stuffy), and it’s not a place where you will want to meet up with friends/family and catch up for a bit. If you just want to dine and leave then it’s ok. It’s not really accessible via public transport.

Randomly picked a savoury item from a selection of light bites like quiche and pie exhibited at the counter. Overall, decent fare; nothing quite out of the ordinary to shout about. If you’re looking for a light bite in-between meals, this is a convenient choice.
A nice little cafe with a good choice of bread, pastries and good coffee.. my favourite is their Lemington! A nice place for a coffee and a bit of a break.

My husband and I began a training course  to increase our strength and stability with our personal trainer, Chau, at OneFitfinity. This is our first experience of going to a gym and it has been life changing.  She would video us doing the different exercises and send them to us for our reference so that we  can practise them on our own. We have found this to be extremely helpful. When we are at the gym practicing in between classes, she would keep an eye on us and correct our form when we are not doing exercises correctly.  Now at the end of the course, we wholeheartedly applaud Chau for being the encouraging, positive and inspiring trainer who has given us the confidence to pursue our fitness goals continuously.   Her experience and knowledge spurs us to keep going. She is an excellent trainer.
OneFitFinity is fitted with new, versatile equipment that allows a range of use for beginners all the way to experienced gym users.   All the equipment is well-maintained and kept very clean.  It’s a cozy neighborhood gym.

My training sessions with Chau had been a very pleasant experience. Each time I was so looking forward to attend my session as she would coach me patiently with precise and simple instructions. Even though I have only attended 12 sessions but I could sense the changes in my body, definitely stronger and with better stability. The gym environment is at top notch, equipment and machines are always well maintained! I’m so glad I’ve found this gem!!

Joined the PT sessions at OneFitFinity and train under Alson. This is a great neighbourhood gym to go for a proper mobility, functional and strength training. The trainers at the gym are all friendly and knowledgeable. Willing to guide and teach. Had a great time training there and continuing to utilize the gym after my PT sessions.

Thank you, Alson and Chau for the great sessions and environment!

Chau is an amazing trainer. These past few months of training with her I’ve strengthened significantly. Each session she uses different exercises to target different muscles and is very attentive to building up strength carefully. I didn’t think I would love the gym but I do now thanks to her!

I joined FITFINITY as its first member and have been a member for 2 years. This is a 5/5 gym.

The gym is convenient and accessible. It is open for almost 24h a day so you can get a workout done anytime you want.

The gym is fully stocked with all the equipment and weights you would need for a proper workout. The equipment is new and well maintained. It is clean. There is good ventilation with no rubbery smell.

Chau and her team are warm, knowledgeable, and dedicated. They will show you how to use the equipment properly, suggest techniques and improvements for your form, and become your closest gym buddy.

I highly, highly recommend that you give this gym a shot. I promise you it won’t disappoint.

A calm and serene condo. surrounded by lush green trees. An small amphitheater( which i think never used before) , a bbq pit in a small 45 steps hill( rusty and can’t be used 😞) 500m walk path in the hill is good for a lazy evening walk.
Love it... Every day jocking /walking for us /for dogs and also have kids ground play...nice small park
It was really quiet and peaceful, with lots of lush greenery.

Let me focus directly on the concrete negative aspects to be aware of before you move in as a tenant:

- Fees for Swash and Function Rooms:
You need to pay up to $30 per hour to use the swash and function rooms. These rates are essentially the market prices available from third-party providers.

- Multiple Price Lists:
Multiple price lists are used. The published price in the clubhouse is half the price compared to what is provided to you once you become a tenant.

! Please update the published price, that is published on the board of the Club house, it is twice as low compare to the price that is given by the guard!

- Advance Booking Requirement:
You need to book the facility one week in advance. If your plans change, there is no refund.

- Shuttle Bus Fees:
A shuttle bus is available for a fee, which is acceptable.

- Strict Bylaws: You need to seek management approval if you have the slightest deviation from these draconian bylaws.

Conclusion:
Check the bylaws in advance, especially the items that are important to you and your loved ones.

Beautiful condo with a distinct retro vibe, which I find classy and wonderful. The swimming pools are tucked away in the forest and give you much more privacy than in a typical condo. The neighborhood also offers fantastic privacy since it is away from the main road. At the same time, there are great shops pretty close by. The Park connector is just 10 minutes away, but the condo itself has its own private hill and footpaths to exercise.
The condo bus also has a great colonial vibe and conveniently brings to Clementi MRT and can add stops in between upon request.
The condo events are great, too.
You are surrounded by nature here, away from the hustle and bustle of the city.
Life is helping and sharing ... charity from the heart and without any purpose. Thank you for being with me.

We were searching for new playground sites for our child and stumbled upon this area on Google Maps. It's remarkably tranquil compared to the bustling neighbourhood across the road in Clementi. We'll definitely consider visiting again in the future.

Little quiet playground (age 4-6)  that serves the neighbourhood. Nice big tree reminiscent of the one in botanical gardens, less the big root. Ample picnic spots, no field for ball games, no toilet facilities.

Old majestic trees is the character of SSWay plqyground. We are trulely blessed to live closeby taking morning / evening walks. Lots fond memories of my kids playing in the playground, holding Bday pinic party, cycling around etc..now my Gchildren are sharing their parent' memories playing at the playground ..
Nice small park for local residents, dog walking, strolling, exercising.
Love this place best to exercise morning and night

Park with Retro 90's vibes that has been updated. Lush mature trees with incredible foliage. Sunset is a rare estate that has almost all classes of SG housing, HDB3/4/5 Bedroom, Private condominiums old/new, Semi-Detached, & Bungalows. The diversity is interesting. The park is busy in the evenings when temperature comes down, and is buzzing with kids and nannies. In the morning, it's the exercise crowd, doing their power walks through the estate. Come weekends, there is mix of domestic workers, home owners, families and doggies. Only thing missing is a toilet facility and a water cooler, otherwise, it's a 5 star park.

The area is a tranquil neighborhood with a simple park featuring a fitness corner situated in the middle of a hill. Right above the park is the entrance to one of the condominiums. The surroundings are calm and peaceful.
A really small and simple park with only one exercise corner and a few benches.
Well laid landscape. Ornamental & Flowering Bushes if added will make you feel good!  Garbage bins few & scattered!
Nice place to move around, quiet n cozy
Good morning 🌄
Just visiting for a friend
Close to nature, 10 mins walk from Clementi MRT.
Mdb houses and apartments.

We’ve had a wonderful experience at Cheston Montessori. We moved our son here at 9 months old after he was frequently falling sick at his previous preschool and were initially nervous about the transition. To our relief, he stopped crying within just 2–3 days and has since been thriving, with a 90–95% attendance rate.

Between 14–18 months, we were pleasantly surprised by how many words he began speaking, which we credit to the nurturing and warm environment at Cheston. A heartfelt thank you to Principal Ms Janice for making both parents and our little one feel assured, and to the dedicated infant teachers - Irene, Shah, Anur, Sharon, and Shihui. They are really the best!

Our son now walks into school with joy and confidence, sometimes not even looking back to say goodbye. The children here are always happy, polite, and friendly, creating a truly lovely school culture. We’re very grateful to the Cheston team and highly recommend them.

Just to add, this review was not requested by the school. I’m sharing it simply to help other parents, as choosing a preschool isnt always easy!

I visited many preschools before enrolling my son in Cheston, but after attending Cheston's open house I quickly decided to enroll him there. We are Japanese and my son couldn't speak English or Chinese, but Ms Janice and the other teachers helped him get used to the school.
It has been five months since my son started kindergarten, and I am really glad that I chose Cheston. He quickly adapted to school life and seems to have grown so much. In addition to the daily activities, there are field trips, various events, and recently a school camp where my son stayed overnight away from home with his friends and teachers for the first time, and he came back feeling very confident.

It was a big decision for us to send our child to a local preschool in a foreign country, but now I feel that it was the right decision to send my son to Cheston Montessori Preschool. I would like to thank the principal, Ms Janice, my homeroom teacher, Ms Doreen, and all the other teachers.

Placing our son at Cheston Montessori was one of the best decisions made.  From the very beginning, the teachers and staff have been incredibly professional, attentive, and genuinely caring.

What truly stands out is how much they’ve become an important part of our child’s life. It’s clear that they don’t just look after the children—they nurture, guide, and support them during such a crucial stage of development. We’ve seen our son grow in confidence, curiosity, and independence, and we know that’s largely thanks to the environment they’ve created.

It gives us great peace of mind knowing he is in such capable and loving hands every day. We’re truly grateful to the entire team at Cheston Montessori for the positive impact they’ve had on our family.

Highly recommended school! Both my kids went to cheston montessori; elder son graduated last year and still misses his teachers from Cheston. We joined Cheston a few years back after the old preschool enbloc-ed and I really appreciate the principal being upfront & transparent when incidents happen like when my son fell or hurt himself; Ms Janice would cold compress my son and quickly inform me. Concerns are often addressed promptly and quickly. Also, love the school set up where there is an outdoor space inside the school compound so kids can play outdoors without having to cross roads or play at sometimes dirty public playgrounds.

Thank you Cheston!

We put our son with Cheston in the infant class for 14 months and we are so glad we make the right choice. The teachers there are meticulous and thoughtful. During my time there there's not a single case of HFMD outbreak, hence showing their dedication to ensure the environment is clean and safe.

Shout-out to all infant care teachers, especially Teacher Irene for being accountable. We will miss you all and hopefully we bump into each other in the malls. Thank you.

This is a spacious 4-level library with a quiet and productive environment. There are plenty of tables on every floor, and some rooms come equipped with monitors and whiteboards, which are great for group discussions and presentations. However, the mobile library app can sometimes be unreliable, with issues like auto-logging students out or failing to load the entry QR code, so many students have to use their NP Go! code instead. It would also be helpful if the library added a reminder system to notify users when their room booking ends, as rooms are sometimes occupied beyond reserved times. Overall, it’s a very useful and well-equipped study space despite minor tech limitations.
Nice place to study, but quite noisy especially during the afternoon when people use the discussion room, as it is not sound proof, and a lot of laughter. Noise management can be improved
sometimes there are inconsiderate people who laugh and talk loudly but otherwise nice
Good job! Keep up the great work!
Very good conducive environment to study
A bus stop out of nowhere.

10 Jan 2024. Was here on some matters. Saw this stall selling Chinese food and price is rather reasonable. Order a grilled fish, tomatoes egg and shredded pork.
All is good. Fish is big and tasty.

Was there specially for this Taiwanese stall. I will give it a 6 out of 10 rating. Chicken & Mee Sua is good but the porkchop does needs a lot of work. It is practically tasteless, even with the sauce. Should be marinated for better taste.

The zhicar is good. Order curry pork rice and salted fish fried rice. Salted fish fried rice is a bit oily, the taste is there. Next to the zhicar is aanother stall which I order the potato slice and pork ear. It was nice.
Lastly the wanton soup is so so only but the wanton is supper big. Usually wanton is not in a big portion but it have a big portion and the taste is just average.
Clean environment, plenty of options, fast service, reasonable prices and many open seats. Public toilets nearby.

Good, a variety choices. New editions will be Yong Yau Foo, Chicken Rice, Fish Soup, Bak Ku Teh soup, Fatty Bom Bom Western. Very Goood.

Evenings some shops closed
Enjoyed the spaghetti with chicken chop - value for money!
Coffee shop with spacious, cooling environment and good food selection. Great Western food and noodles stall
Good location. Also food quite good also.
Awesome location! Great wanton mee

The cheapest set of bun, eggs and drink at $2.30!!

Available all day. Can have my comfort food morning n evening too.. good ambience n airy ... one of the best coffee shops around in Singapore!!

Just besides always opened 7-11 store and diagonally opposite West Coast Park McDonald's..

The zu char dishes also tasty.

Newly open, a little remote.

2 crabs priced at $38,$48,$58.
We bought extra 12 buns for $6.

Pictured is takeaway 2 medium sized crabs. Very fresh and size of crab is good. There is extra eggs in the sauce. Worth the price, will definitely buy again :)
Superb food! Fresh ingredients! Not oily not salty, just perfect! Reasonable pricing also!
Food taste is ok. Environment is your typical industry area coffee shop. Price still reasonable.  Good thing is easy to find parking lots.
$58 chili Crab was nice and highly recommended ,only the mantuo got fishy taste.
Not opened many times

The taste is not quite the bcm at hawker center. The mee kia is not the usual too. That said, the taste is ok, i like the soup most and it is refillable and has napa cabbage in it. The satay tasted good, but some pieces big, some pieces small. The Mayamese service staff is very friendly.

Minus 4 stars because:
1) the sliced fish is soft until there is no bite to it and there is no fish taste. Not saying it tasted bad, but not up to me expectation at this price point.
2) the handmade pork ball which I confirmed with that staff, and gotten the assurance that it is handmade, tasted like factory product. The smooth edges (see video) tells it all.
3) Perhaps due to 3 invited groups of influencers/food bloggers, the kitchen missed out on one of the dish I ordered. The portion of the noodle one of them had looked way much more than mine.
4) 1 hr after eating, v thirsty

The pork lard was super crispy, adding so much flavour and texture to the taste. We also added the crispy fish and the portion was surprisingly generous. Service was warm, friendly, and welcoming throughout. You can really feel the heart behind the place & food - will definitely be back again!

I had a really great experience here! The food was delicious. Full of flavor, well-seasoned, and served fresh. On top of that, we ordered pork and chicken satay. It is absolutely fantastic. Meat was tender, juicy and perfectly grilled.
The staff were friendly, attentive and made us feel welcome. Overall a wonderful place for good food and excellent service.

Sweet & clean tasting soup. Fish maw and pork portion not bad. Noodles springy and seasoning was just nice. $9.90 per bowl generally portion ok.

Free soup refills 👍🏻

Really good minced pork noodles! The noodles were springy and well-coated with a savoury, fragrant sauce. The minced pork was flavourful and not too salty — perfectly balanced. Simple comfort food done right. Will definitely order again 👍

A nice and modern mall conveniently located at the Jurong East transportation hub, with sheltered walkways linking it to two other popular malls in the area. The mall is spacious, thoughtfully designed with plenty of greenery, and offers a pleasant shopping experience.

Some sections are non-air-conditioned, which can feel warm during the afternoon, but the open concept adds to its vibrant, lifestyle-focused atmosphere. With a wide variety of food and beverage outlets, it’s a great destination for dining, shopping, and leisure.

Mall is around 10 years old located in Jurong east central and beside the MRT station. Good shopper traffic, more home goods, Fnb and lifestyle/services shops such as Nittori, Dreame and Paradise restaurants etc. Toilet cleanliness and  amenities can be improved. Carpark is easy to navigate.
Great place for casual shopping. Good spread of food. Easy connection to the jurong east train station.

As a frequent shopper in the west, I’ve spent countless hours at Westgate, and it has firmly established itself as one of my go-to destinations. Located at 3 Gateway Drive and directly connected to Jurong East MRT Station, its accessibility is virtually unbeatable . The mall is also linked to JEM and IMM via covered walkways, creating a massive interconnected shopping belt .

Westgate truly shines as a family and lifestyle hub. The layout is a mix of sleek, air-conditioned corridors and its signature open-air "Courtyard," which houses popular alfresco dining spots . However, the crown jewel for families is definitely the Westgate Wonderland on Level 4. This fantasy-themed outdoor playground is a massive hit with children, featuring giant interactive structures where they can burn off energy .

Foodies will be in heaven here. The mall boasts an impressive spectrum of dining options. You can grab a quick, affordable bite at the Food Republic food court in the basement or indulge in world-class ramen at Ippudo and next-level Japanese barbecue at Yakiniku Like . For those with a sweet tooth, the newly opened Kind Kone offers fantastic all-vegan ice cream with unique flavors like "Blue Planet," while Flipper’s serves up their legendary Japanese souffle pancakes . Don’t miss the Isetan Supermarket in the basement, which is a treasure trove of premium Japanese groceries and ready-to-eat meals .

On the retail front, Westgate covers all the bases. You’ll find major anchors like a sprawling two-story Uniqlo and a Japanese department store, alongside high-street favorites like Zara and COS . Tech enthusiasts can explore the Samsung Experience Store or Best Denki, while beauty lovers will appreciate the large Sephora outlet .

If I have to levy one major criticism, it’s the notorious parking situation. The carpark has a single, tightly designed entry and exit point that leads to the pick-up/drop-off area, creating absolute chaos during peak hours . Many reviewers report it can take 30 minutes just to exit . My advice? Take the MRT. It drops you right at the mall’s doorstep, saving you the headache.

Despite the driving frustrations, Westgate remains a vibrant and well-maintained mall. With its excellent store mix, incredible variety of food, and dedicated kids' facilities, it offers a complete package that caters to everyone from office workers to young families. It’s a solid 4 out of 5 stars for me.

Right next to JEM but feels a bit more "atas." The outdoor playground on the top floor is great for families with kids. Lots of popular restaurants like Tim Ho Wan and Din Tai Fung here. The link bridge to Jurong East MRT is very convenient and fully sheltered, which is a life-saver during those heavy afternoon downpours.

This is a great place for Ngee Ann students to swim, as long as you have a towel and a shirt to change into. The facility is well-maintained, and there’s also a free gym on the top floor where students can work out with professional equipment while wearing shorts and a changed shirt. A staff member is always present at the counter to oversee safety and assist students. Additionally, there’s a large hall inside where students can play games or engage in group activities. Overall, it’s a versatile and convenient space for fitness, recreation, and leisure.
Not a NP alumni, but please inject some life here. This place has been dormant for a few years. Can request Starbucks, Macs, KFC, Subway, etc to set up stalls (just like what SP did)
Nice and friendly environment. Teenagers training for National Schools Track and Field competition .
My first SG love.

Really a good place if you're looking to run. The track is well maintained and there are also very clean toilet! There are also basketball court, volleyball court and a soccer field.

The swimming pool is generally clean, I have only been in there once so i cant speak for the rest of the time.

Generally if i will ever come back here again, it will only be for the track to run.

Nice little garden hidden in the midst of Toh Yi Drive! It's open to general public, and it's located right by the multi storey carpark. Not sheltered, so take note especially if rain is an issue. There's nothing much beyond just an entire stretch of plants.
A small estate at Upper Bukit Timah. It is built on gradual slope and is surrounded by low rise building. There is a park behind the  estate. Here you can find a few mango trees.
Nice

place_name: Eng Kong Cheng Soon Community Garden
review_text: not good

🚤⛵🛳️This Promenade Lookout Jetty with a sweeping view of the stunning shoreline, often moored with ocean-going ships, small colourful fishing boats, stunning yachts and even Jackup Oil Rigs is a popular spot for fishing enthusiast, as well as a great spot to view the sunset.
🏝️West Coast Park which was built on reclaimed land was experiencing coastal erosion, in the early days.
📥To alleviate the erosion at the shoreline, a granite barrier, along with a picturesque promenade was built.
🎣🛟A Lookout Jetty was also included along with the scenic Promenade.

The Promenade Lookout Jetty at West Coast Park is a hidden gem offering a panoramic coastal view that stretches from the Republic of Singapore Yacht Club to Pasir Panjang Terminal and Jurong Island. It's a perfect spot for watching sunsets, with the serene atmosphere enhancing the experience. Photographers will appreciate the expansive views and peaceful surroundings, making it an ideal location for capturing stunning waterfront scenes. The jetty is rarely crowded, providing a tranquil escape where you can relax and take in the beauty of the coast.

Good:

1. A small quiet pier at West Coast Park.
2. Good view of Singapore Port.
3. Golden sunsets on non cloudy days..
4. Police cctv camera give a sense of security.
5. Police patrol duty every  night is very reassuring.
6. Great opportunity for photo ops and drone videography.

Bad:

1. Not a scenic beach. The view in the front is spoiled by cranes and ships.
2. Jetty is very short. Doesn't take you far into the sea.
3. Not ample shelter around. Need to run in case of sudden showers / thunder storms.
4. Do not go down into the water unless an expert swimmer. It is deceptively deep esp during high tide.
5. Lower rocks are exposed during low tide. Don't go down as these can be as slippery as ice.

It is a small spot inside the west coast park, where you can sit on a bench and look the harbour. You nearly have the same view as along the normal border path (along the harbour and on the side of the playground area). In the first picture: how you reach the spot. In the second one: the view
My first time to see super boat on the port. The area fits for circling and running, pinic also a good choice

We’ve been with this school for more than two years and have had a very positive experience. What first attracted us was the lovely outdoor playground and weekly activities like mud kitchen and water play. The learning is well-rounded and age-appropriate, but what we value most is the warm and caring atmosphere. The teachers and students share a very close bond, and our child feels truly happy and supported here.

A special thanks to Ms Nichelle for her patience, care, and dedication — she has made a big difference in our child’s journey.

From the very first visit me and my husband made 5 years ago, we knew this was the right preschool for our son — the outdoor space is so big and full of nature exposure, exactly what we were looking for. Now both our son and daughter love going to school every day. The teachers are kind and caring, and we can really feel the love and patience they give to every child. We’re so grateful to have found this wonderful place. 💕
Now my son is graduating this year, and we will truly miss the warm teachers, the joyful environment, and all the beautiful memories he made here. We are so grateful for this wonderful journey and so happy we have chose TSHWC. 💖

The teachers and the overall staff are so welcoming and warm.  They are patient and very caring and really made settling in very easy.

They also patently tell you all details daily of the child’s activities.

Overall this Pats has a fabulous campus with great play areas etc. .

Our son has the most enjoyable years of his preschool days at The Schoolhouse. He loves the outdoor space and the playground. Teacher Nichelle who followed him since playgroup days have been termed his princess and role model in school. Thank you to all teachers who took care of him

My little girl started with this preschool since her Nursery days and is now in K2.  It is bittersweet that she will soon leave this wonderful school.  My husband and I were first attracted to this location due to its spacious grounds and emphasis on play.  My little daughter has been very happy here, being nurtured by teachers and administrators in her various academic and creative endeavors.  We love how the school has various cultural and enrichment activities beyond the classroom.  The goal is always to develop the whole child.  My little daughter will certainly miss this school; it has given her a wonderful start in life.

place_name: KidsCampus West Coast Childcare & Infant Care Centre
review_text: I sent my baby to the infant care at 4months old and he has adapted well in the school, thriving and having fun daily. It is a small centre but good for infants. Does not fall sick as often as I imagined, and he reaches his milestones timely and eats and drinks and sleeps well there. He is picking up Chinese words there which is very good! Thank u wang laoshi and teacher eni and the assistants and pg teachers and principal that help with the infants. You are all very accommodating and easy to talk with. My baby has eczema/sensitive skin and the teachers have taken good care of his skin care routine which I’m very grateful for. Some things that can be improved would be having enough infant care teachers spread throughout the day. Sometimes when I drop my baby off in the morning there is no ifc teacher, so he fusses a lot as he is more familiar with the ifc teachers to calm him down. So I try to bring him in only after 7.45am hoping an ifc teacher would be around by then. It will be nice to have more variety of suitable breakfast options for infants but I understand it’s a small centre. The centres bedsheets are pretty expensive too for the quality so I would have skipped buying them.

place_name: KidsCampus West Coast Childcare & Infant Care Centre
review_text: the teachers and principal has been very kind and  patience to both of my girls. we are so grateful for the positive and nurturing environment. the learning journey my girls been through leave a great impact on their studies as well as their confidence in socialising. they were very introverted before joining kidscampus here. thankyou(:

place_name: KidsCampus West Coast Childcare & Infant Care Centre
review_text: The principal is very warm and friendly, and can switch between Chinese and English, allowing for effective communication. The teachers are very dedicated, and our child tells us that he likes the teachers here when he comes home. Furthermore, the school's organized activities are very meaningful. The school has a great atmosphere.

place_name: KidsCampus West Coast Childcare & Infant Care Centre
review_text: My child has been in this infant care since she was three months old. Over the past year, she has grown steadily, rarely fallen sick, and has been very well cared. She also loves coming to school — she always smiles happily when she sees the teachers. She really enjoys being here.

place_name: KidsCampus West Coast Childcare & Infant Care Centre
review_text: Enrolled my child in this school for infant care & playgroup. Massive love for the teachers there regardless the classes they were teaching. Despite it being a small school, it is super cosy and comfortable, making it a conducive place to gain knowledge. We had to change school due to distance but this preschool will forever be close to our hearts 🤍 special thanks and sending love to Ms Melissa, Teacher Rajes, Teacher Du, Wong Lao Shi & Teacher Fanah 🤍

We have been attending lessons since last year and my daughter loves her classes and teacher. Lessons are well structured and we receive regular feedback from her teacher. With the lesson giing ahead of the school's curriculum, she has no problem grasping the new vocabulary in school. Highly recommend!

My son started his lesson in HCL since Jan and he loves the teacher and the engagement during the lesson. After every lesson, teacher will always feedback the children progress promptly. Highly recommend!

My son has been attending HCL and we’ve had a very positive experience so far. The teachers are patient, encouraging, and able to explain concepts clearly, which really helps build confidence. Lessons are structured and engaging, and we can see steady improvement in both understanding and interest. Communication with parents is also clear and timely. Overall, a supportive and well-run learning environment that we’re happy with.

HCL west coast branch, the staff and teacher here are very welcoming. From my daughter and son who have no confident with Chinese from today their school exam pass with above average in a year. The teaching method really work for them. No regret sending them here.

My daughter has been attending HCL for a semester and is looking forward to the classes every week. Classes are smaller and more attention was given to the students individually. The teachers will also provide feedback after every session to keep the parents up to date on the progress. Highly recommend to check them out if you haven’t.
Nice to have a train station near a industrial area but it‘s a bit sad they have to close a school because of it.
A future MRT station for Jurong Region Line and  Cross Island Line.
Very good. Not open yet
I cannot count how many times we visited this restaurant!😋 We kept on coming back! Check my shorts (redprawntech). Overall, Food is great!

Had a craving for Thai food and dropped by Sanook Kitchen.

Clear Tom Yum Fried Rice: It had that perfect, authentic sour and spicy tom yum kick infused right into the grains. It is packed with flavour, though it is on the oilier side just a heads-up if you prefer your rice drier!

Basil Sotong Rice: Really good! The basil sauce was savory, aromatic, and packed a punch. The sotong (squid) was cooked perfectly nice and tender, not rubbery at all. Pairs beautifully with the white rice.

Thai Chendol: The perfect sweet ending. Super rich coconut milk and sweet palm sugar over shaved ice. It was so comforting and totally satisfied my sweet tooth.

Ice Lime Juice: So refreshing! It had a sharp, tangy sourness that completely cleansed the palate after the heavy savoury dishes.

Great food, fast service, and definitely worth every cent. Will absolutely be back for more! 10/10!

Quite regular to this Thai resto, as it’s a comford food, decent portion, and quite affordable price compared with other Thai resto. Food was quite nice and ingredients were fresh. Have tried their Seafood Basil Rice, Clear Tomyum Seafood Soup, Stir-fried Mixed Vegetable with Mushrooms, Stuffed Chicken Wings with Fish Paste, Steamed Seabass with Chilli, Lime & Garlic. Deep-fried Chicken with Thai Herb, Stir-fried Kailan with Mushrooms. All were quite tasty and flavourful. The free flow popcorn was nice too.  Atmosphere is nice and comfy, service was quick and efficient.

The service staff was friendly and food was good.
Food was served quickly
A simple dish executed with skill, fresh ingredients, and excellent balance—proof that great food doesn’t need to be complicated.”

We were greeted by warm smiles and humorous rapport with the servers, they calmly explained our seating arrangements and took our orders!

The service was detailed, and the food was well made! The beef sirloin was served perfectly tender on the inside, and the fish and chips were crisp. These qualities spelt a wonderful time of gastronomy and camaraderie for the family. Plus, the carbonara quickly became a favorite amongst our two young nephews.

Will definitely recommend this place again!

I’m having dinner at Lazy Lizard right now, and it’s such a great find in the West Coast area. It has a very laid-back, welcoming vibe. Definitely a "hidden gem" for a chill neighborhood meal.

The Food
Duck Confit: This was the highlight for me. The duck is just as good as it looks in the photos, with the skin done perfectly. I absolutely love the sauce (Spicy Cumin Mango Chutney) it comes with; it’s a really interesting blend of sweet, fruity, and just a hint of spiciness.

Classic Ribeye: A very reliable choice if you’re looking for a hearty steak. It was cooked well and satisfied that craving for a classic Western main.

Pan Seared Halibut: This was a great lighter option. The fish was seared nicely, keeping it moist and flavourful.

Menu Variety: They have a solid range of Western comfort food. Aside from these mains, they’ve got plenty of pasta, burgers, and pizzas, plus a good selection of craft beers.

Service & Vibe
The staff here are very friendly and attentive, which makes the whole experience feel personal and relaxed. They were quick to help without being overbearing.

It’s a very family-friendly spot, perfect for a casual dinner without any fuss.

I visited this restaurant specifically to try the Cheesy Messy Beef Burger. The burger was satisfying and well-prepared, accompanied by a generous, heaped portion of fries on the side. In terms of accessibility, the establishment was not very crowded during my visit, which made it easy to enter and find a table without any delay. The staff members were polite and kind during our interactions. However, they spent most of their time in the kitchen or back area rather than on the dining floor. This made it somewhat difficult to catch their attention for additional orders or further assistance after the food was delivered. While the meal itself was high quality and the portions were large, the lack of floor presence from the service team is something to keep in mind if you prefer more attentive service. Overall, it is a solid choice for a quiet meal if you are looking for a filling burger.

Great atmosphere. Food is big portions and nice.
Foods taste can be improved more. Overall is good place to chill.

Despite the lunch crowd on Sunday, the service was incredibly fast and the meal was truly satisfying. We enjoyed fresh oysters, rich beef bolognese, and flavorful lobster bisque. The ginger ale was also excellent. The restaurant is very kid-friendly, featuring a small play corner with toys and special family promotions.

I visited Cold Storage on Thursday to purchase a large potted plant, and I was truly impressed by the excellent customer service.

The plant didn't have a price tag, so the manager and Leyi immediately offered to help check the price for me. Instead of asking me to wait around, they told me I could continue with my shopping. Once they received the pricing information, they kindly contacted me and asked me to return to complete the purchase.

What impressed me even more was that they helped place the heavy plant onto their trolley, making it so much easier for me to wheel it to my car. Their kindness, patience, and willingness to go the extra mile made the whole shopping experience smooth and enjoyable.

A big thank you to the manager and especially Leyi for the outstanding service. It's staff like them who make customers want to come back again. Keep up the great work!

Nice, big and spacious. Great place to shop for groceries.
For vegans and vegetarians, there are also plenty of plant-based stuff.

Huge variety of well displayed items. But due to the manpower shortages, some expired products and rotten fruits are not well spotted.

Like their weekly discount shelves and also the big snack counter.

Cashier system has self checked which is v much convenient.

Helpful staff.

Surprised to see this high-end supermarket all the way in West Coast. Perhaps most of the consumers are folks who live in condo complexes nearby between West Coast and NUS campus.

The items tend to be expensive and sometimes rare items not commonly found in other supermarkets, even the likes of FairPrice Finest. The deli section has a lot of expensive meat grills and imported hams, some as far as places like Italy (some PDO stuffs). The beer section also contains craft beers I've almost never seen elsewhere in Singapore, like Japanese craft beers and Trappists.

The sushi section is also pretty well-stocked, fresh, and relatively affordable. The inari sushi trio is particularly cheap and makes a nice lunch (my favorite).

A nice clean supermarket that’s bright, airy and not overly crowded. A fair amount of their products are imported, so it does leave an impression that it is slightly more expensive compared to the other Supermarket chains.

However, based on my purchases, the differences were negligible, so your experience may vary. I like that despite it not being massive, they have a cold cuts deli, a chicken rotisserie, ribs, sushi counter, even live lobsters and oysters all within reach of each other.

Staff are also willingly to point out where particular items might be. Not a bad place to shop.

Great place for Korean products, even fresh veggies, frozen products and even fresh noodles. Korean utensils pot and pans , from drinks to other daily necessities. Service was also very attentive and friendly,I often sees the country attendant went to fresh corners to arrange the items into order every time after payment, that itself is showing professional, nothing to complain.

Pros:
-everything you need from Korea they have it here
-easy access and clean, will organized
-fresh ingredients
-attentive and professional services

Cons:
-nothing to complain

I'm a regular customer of this Korean mart and have always had pleasant experiences, especially with the friendly male manager who is consistently helpful and welcoming. However, during my visit on 30th April, he wasn’t around, and a lady with glasses was at the cashier. I was quite surprised when she informed a customer that card payments are not accepted for purchases under $5. As someone who shops here often, I’ve never heard of such a rule before, nor has it ever been mentioned by the usual manager. It felt a bit unusual and inconsistent with what I’ve experienced in the past.
I love this place. It has snacks and food not found at Shine Korea.

I usually shop at this Koryo Mart branch and have always had a good experience. The usual cashier uncle is friendly and professional, but he wasn’t around during my recent visit on 5th April.
Instead, a different cashier was on duty, and I noticed something odd—he scanned my items but didn’t finalize the transaction. I paid in exact cash, but he didn’t complete the sale in the system or give me a receipt. It made me wonder if the purchase was even recorded. I stayed nearby for a while, and he kept glancing at me, which felt suspicious.
I hope the management looks into this, as I’ve seen other reviews mention similar things.

What an authentic Korean supermarket to shop at! The boss is very friendly, and you can ask for help if you're not familiar with the items. During the Chinese New Year period, there are additional discounts. Good deal.

Collected a few parcel there, seems like she doesnt want to handle shoppee customers, lousy attitude, dont need her to smile and greet, aleast acknowledge a human standing infront of your counter.
send her for customer service course fast.

To the Owner reading this,
Your Shop is fine, reasonable pricing, convenient to buy daily items. But your cashier is killing your business, collecting parcel also bring foot fall to your shop too.

When her work attitude and behaviour improves, so will your ratings.

Hope  to see cashier improvement soon.

Very very bad service by the cashier, it’s a Vietnamese. First and foremost I’ve got no issues with foreigners, but I’m really fed up with her service. Bought a total of 13 coca colas and asked her if there was any bulk discounts, but she got fed-up instead and rejected me rudely, and I just asked for the whole price and she was fed up and kept sighing here and there like we’re like cheapskate customers. It’s only just asking about the price and I’m a paying customer here. Could have tell me politely. What’s wrong with her? Please take a good look at the photos

Horrible service. When I got my items and approached the cashier, she didn't acknowledge me! She actually continued fixing her parcels for a good few mins while I was just standing there🤯 should i have just left? Would that be doing her a favour? Will not be back even tho I live just opp. Also, her parcels were all over the counter, customers can't even put their purchases down. Joke.

Looks like they're too busy with their parcel collection side biz, to scan ur items for payment🤡

Quite a big supermarket located at a new public housing project. The supermarket is located right below the multi storey carpark, facing the main road. The supermarket was pretty well stocked up when I was there. Reasonable pricing.

If you’ve ever wondered what it’s like to be treated like an inconvenience for daring to be a customer, look no further than this charming little convenience stall near my place. Based on several recent reviews, I see I'm not alone in experiencing the warm hospitality of their cashier – a Malaysian aunty with bleached blonde hair tied up in a ponytail and a permanent expression that screams, “You owe me rent.”

She greets every paying customer like it’s the worst part of her day (and possibly her life), and I've personally witnessed her tossing out curt, borderline rude responses to people just trying to… you know, pay for things. One of my neighbors – a poor soul – even shared that when he politely suggested she be a little more courteous, she shouted back at him. Now that’s customer engagement, isn't it?

The best part? There’s little to no info available about who runs this place, so if you thought you might want to raise a complaint or even just leave feedback – good luck! But hey, who needs accountability when you’ve got that level of charm behind the counter?

Prices aren’t particularly great either, but if you’re in the West Coast Park area and feel like ruining a perfectly good day with a side of attitude and a splash of rudeness, this place is an absolute must-visit!

Foundation member since 2023 Feb. Been coming here almost daily. Chill place with very supportive coaches. Shout out to Dalston, the best coach ever. Also special mention to Kimmy Erul and Cheryl for their guidance.

Joined BFT in July 2025. Fitness has improved! Great coaches, especially Coach Chris as he saw I had challenges from years of no exercise, always patient in correcting my form to ensure I get maximum benefit with minimum stress to the body!!

Seems that the place lacks privacy. Whole gym can be seen from outside. Very small area. Classes seems to be conducted with booking sessions only.

Place looks clean and is always well packed at the end of the day.
Great team here at BFT WCP. Community feel, fun sessions and great trainers genuinely interested in supporting you to achieve your goals.

Coaches are friendly! They will do some explanation before the workout start. The space not so spacious but also the class capacity is small.  I have improve alot after I joined few months! No shower room inside but is walkable distance from my house. I going to BFT almost everyday as it consists of cardio and strength! I always have Fun workout and community is nice!

A bare open grass field beside West Coast Way and West Coast Road, offering quiet and spacious surroundings but rarely used. It lacks dedicated facilities or shade, giving it no clear recreational purpose and serving mainly as a pleasant passive green space.

Not really a garden but big grass field along West Coast Road. Sign to warn against trespassing already set up…  it is surrounded by the landed property and in between west coast central and Pandan Gardens.  Alot of cyclists and runners running to Pandan reservoir and the Park Connector linking Pandan reservoir to Ulu Pandan Park connector

This structure offers one a shade from the current hot sun or shelter from the sudden  rain, however limited it is. It was also intended to beautify the estate, and provide a shaded place for a quick chat while waiting for children to be dismissed from the nearby school.
Hv lifts on overhead Bridge at Newest.  Grocery, Prime supermkt n F n B at this place. More bus number in this area even at West Coast highway.
Beautiful place, fresh air and noise free zone

This park connects the Pandas Garden and the West Coast area, with a canal stream running beside it. Although signs prohibit fishing, locals are sometimes seen trying their luck. The trees provide good shade, keeping it cool in the late afternoon. It offers a tranquil atmosphere with enough greenery between the HDB flats and nearby condominiums. Otherwise, nothing particularly special.

The only downside is that the pedestrian and vehicle paths share the same side, so you sometimes need to move aside for bicycles or vehicles. Another issue is that the signboard is blocked by electric booths, making it unclear to the public.

Pros
– Shady and cool in late afternoons
– Greenery and tranquility between residential areas
– Canal adds a natural touch

Cons
– Pedestrian and vehicle paths overlap
– Signboard blocked, unclear to the public

⸻

This park connects the Panda Garden and the West Coast area, and there is a river running along it. There is a signboard that prohibits fishing, but sometimes you can see locals fishing. The trees provide good shade and keep you cool even in the late afternoon. Between the HDB and the condominium, you can enjoy the calm atmosphere, but there is nothing special.

The only drawback is that the road is on the same side as the vehicle road, so you need to avoid bicycles and cars.

長所
– 午後も涼しい日陰
- It is spread between the houses
– 運河が自然の超ををます

短所
– 歩行者道と車道が重なれてす
– The guide board is blocked

⸻

This park is connected to 熊貓花園和西海鱼地區, and there is a 條運河 next to it. 雖然有智能釣魚的剧情的英语,但有時老老商地地地嘗马釣魚.樹木provide good shade, so that the afternoon time is not 會太炎熱. The environment here is quiet and full of greenery.

The only drawback is that the people's road and the vehicle road are shared on the same side, so it is necessary for the vehicle to be on the same side.

優點
– 下午果是涼戽的樹蔭
– HDB and condominiums
– 運河增添自然流圍

shortcoming
– 人行道與車道重疊
– 告言牌被遭擋不是生

⸻

Dieser Park verbindet den Pandas Garden mit dem Westküstengebiet, daneben fließt ein Kanal. Although ein Schild das Angeln verbietet, versuchen Einheimische hier gelegentlich zu fischen. Die Bäume spenden reichlich Schatten, soddas es am späten Nachtigmat angehemen kühl bleibt. Zwischen HDB-Wohnungen und Eigentumswohnungen bietet der Park Ruhe und viel Grün. Ansonsten nichts Besonderes.

Der einzige Nachtheit ist dass Fußgänger- und Fahrzeugwege auf derselben Seite verlaufen, sossads man Fahrrädern oder Fahrzeugen muss ausweichen. In addition, the Hinweisschild durch Elektroboxen verdeckt und ist für die Öffentlichkeit schwer erkennbaren.

Advantages
– Angenehmer Schatten am Nachmittag
– Ruhe und Grün zwischen Wohngebäuden
– Kanal sort für natürliche Atmosfere

Disadvantage
– Fußgänger- und Fahrzeugwege überschneiden sich
– Hinweisschild durch Elektroboxen verdeckt

⸻

This park connects the Panda Garden and the West Coast area, with a canal running nearby. Despite the "No Fishing" sign, local residents sometimes try to fish here. The trees provide good shade, so it's not so bright in the late hours of the day. The atmosphere is quiet, lots of greenery between HDB and condominiums. And the rest is nothing special.

The only minus is that the footpath and the road go on the same side, so sometimes I have to give way to my bicycle and car. Another problem is that the pointer is covered by electrical cabinets and the view is poor.

Plus
- Shade and coolness in the afternoon
- Quiet atmosphere and greenery between the rooms
– Channel adds a natural accent

Minus
- Pedestrian and car tracks coincide
- The directory is closed, it's not good
